Name: Skydio
Description:
Skydio’s mission is to make the world more productive, creative, and safe with autonomous flight. AI and computer vision are foundational to our products, allowing drones to fly autonomously and automatically capture and process data. Skydio drones help public safety agencies, federal government, and private companies serve their communities more safely and effectively by providing real-time access to information captured with high-powered cameras mounted on intelligent flying robots. Our drones support thousands of organizations, including every branch of the U.S. military, 26 allied nation, 1,000+ public safety agencies across every U.S. state, and 900+ utility providers. 
 
Founded in 2014, Skydio designs, assembles, and supports its products in the U.S., with headquarters in San Mateo, CA, and manufacturing facilities in Hayward, CA. Skydio is the largest drone manufacturer in the U.S. and has shipped 55,000+ flying robots. 

The company is backed by top investors and strategic partners, including Andreessen Horowitz, Linse Capital, Next47, IVP, Playground, and NVIDIA.

Name: Agility Robotics
Description: We started with scientific breakthroughs and layered on top of that world-class engineering. We approach everything with a focus on function. Our humanoid robots were designed from day one to do useful work.

Name: Exotec
Description:
Exotec builds elegant goods-to-person warehouse robotic solutions for the world's largest brands. The company combines the best of hardware and software to offer flexible warehouse systems that drive operational efficiency, add resiliency, and improve working conditions for warehouse operators. 30+ industry-leading brands including Carrefour, Decathlon, Gap, and Uniqlo trust Exotec to improve their operations and profitably navigate rapid shifts in business models and customer expectations.

For more information please visit https://www.exotec.com/

Name: Quantum Systems
Description:
Quantum Systems is the powerhouse of intelligent unmanned systems.
As a leading dual‑use and defence technology company, we deliver multi‑domain solutions - on land, in the air, at sea, and in software - for government agencies and commercial customers worldwide. 

Quantum Systems is Europe's highest valued dual-use scale up and a global leader in the development of intelligent unmanned systems. We supply mission-grade ISR, autonomy, and integrated mission solutions to military, government, and commercial customers. Quantum Systems products combine proven hardware, AI, and interoperable software to deliver persistent, reliable intelligence where it matters most.

Name: 1X
Description:
1X is a leader in humanoid robotics, creating humanoid robots for the home as a first step in developing general purpose robots

1X’s mission is to create an abundance of labor through safe, intelligent humanoids that work alongside people.

Name: Ocean Infinity
Description:
Ocean Infinity is a pioneer of large scale marine robotic operations deploying robotic technologies to capture ocean data and deliver maritime solutions whilst minimising our environmental footprint. We are building the world’s largest fleet of marine robotic vehicles, offering the safest and most sustainable operations the industry has ever seen. Our purpose is to innovate using robotics and technology to transform industries, enabling people and the planet to thrive. 

Our core belief that the seemingly impossible can be achieved with innovative, creative thinkers and cutting-edge technology has remained at the heart of the company. Ocean Infinity is a place where creativity and innovation is demanded and allowed to flourish. The team is constantly developing the most advanced technology and data handling capabilities in order to remain at the forefront of the industry.

Name: Humanoid
Description:
In a world where artificial intelligence opens up new horizons, our faith in its potential unveils a new outlook where, together, humans and machines build a new future filled with knowledge, inspiration, and incredible discoveries.

The development of a functional humanoid robot underpins an era of abundance and well-being where poverty will disappear, and people will be able to choose what they want to do.

We can imagine millions of bipedal robots doing more work than all human labour does today freeing people from the servitude of some repetitive and boring tasks that nobody likes to perform.

We believe that we have enough abundance to take care of everyone who is displaced. Eventually, providing a universal basic income will lead to the true evolution of our civilization.

Labor shortages loom, as the demands on our built environment rise. With the world’s workforce increasingly moving away from undesirable tasks, the manufacturing, construction, and logistics industries critical to our daily lives are left exposed.

By deploying our general-purpose humanoid robots in environments deemed hazardous or monotonous, we envision a future where human well-being is safeguarded while closing the gaps in critical global labour needs.

Name: Evobi Automations Pvt Ltd
Description:
Evobi Automations Pvt. Ltd. is a leading automation company headquartered in Bengaluru, with over 12 years of experience in technology and manufacturing. We operate through two specialized business units:

Rednerds – Your go-to partner for manufacturing needs, specializing in ODM (Original Design Manufacturing) and OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturing). We provide high-quality design and production services, turning concepts into reality.

Yudu Robotics – Focused on product innovation, we design and develop cutting-edge hardware and software solutions with a core emphasis on Embedded Robotics and IoT. Our goal is to integrate intelligent technology into transformative products in Education

Name: Origin
Description:
Origin is building the World's First General Purpose Construction Robot.

Nothing defines an era more clearly than the way a society builds. Yet, construction remains the only major industry where productivity has declined over the last century. While the rest of the world has become mechanised and lightning-fast, construction is still slow, hazardous, and heavily manual.

We are here to change that.

We are on a multi-decade journey to enable humanity to build anything, anywhere, autonomously, without ever compromising on craftsmanship.
 
Origin is working with leading Trade Contractors and General Contractors in New York and will expand across the United States in the future. If you want to define the future of automation in Construction, please get in touch.

Join the most talent-dense robotics team in the world. We are looking for passionate engineers who want to shape how the future is built. If you are ready to reinvent how humanity builds, apply here: https://apply.workable.com/10x-3/

Name: FieldAI
Description:
FieldAI is the general-purpose brain making robots autonomous in complex, risky, real-world environments. We combine data-driven and physics-based methods to create the Field Foundation Model (FFM), the first risk-aware, physics-first foundation model for general-purpose robots across any robot type.

The FFM works through a virtuous data flywheel that receives input, assesses uncertainty, establishes risk, and takes action based on that risk. Each deployment increases reliability and continuously enhances the capabilities of the global fleet, all without pre-trained data, GPS, maps, or pre-programmed routes.

Our mission is to deploy autonomous robots at scale in any unstructured environment.

Name: Anzo Controls
Description:
At Anzo Controls, we’re passionate about driving change and innovation in everything we do. We empower our employees to take on challenging roles and assignments, offering them an elevated level of responsibility in an agile and dynamic working environment. Our people are at the heart of what makes us who we are, and together, we create exceptional products and experiences.

In short, we don’t just think outside the box — we think like there is no box.

Be part of an organization that's transforming the motion control industry in India.

As a leading manufacturer of electronics and software products for industrial sectors, we build best-in-class solutions in the following domains:

Robotics, SCARA, Delta & Gantry Systems
Motion Control and Drives
Automated Guided Vehicles (AGVs)
Motorized Driven Rollers
Servo Drives
HMIs
Barcode Scanner Applications

We enable the manufacturing sector to harness the full potential of our offerings, providing OEMs with competitive, advanced solutions for the market.

With expertise in Embedded Systems, IT, Controls, and Mechanical Engineering, we deliver end-to-end solutions for factory automation and customized projects.

Name: BEW
Description:
BEW is a dynamic company driven by a passion for engineering, innovation, and the transformative power of technology. Headquartered in the heart of technological advancements, we specialize in delivering cutting-edge solutions that seamlessly integrate Artificial Intelligence (AI), Robotics, and Engineering across diverse sectors.

#ElectricalEngineering 
#MechanicalEngineering 
#CivilEngineering 

Join us on a journey of innovation, engineering excellence, and the limitless possibilities that arise when creativity meets technology.

Name: Advanced Navigation
Description:
Advanced Navigation is a global leader in navigation and autonomous systems. By leveraging capabilities in software-enhanced hardware, every solution delivers unrivaled capabilities and exceptional performance across land, air, sea and space applications where GPS is unreliable. 

Made possible with extensive research, testing and vertically integrated manufacturing, the company has progressed into deep technology fields, including robotics, inertial, photonic and quantum sensing, artificial intelligence, underwater acoustics, and GPS antennas and receivers. Customers choose Advanced Navigation for rapid product delivery and unmatched technical field expertise.

Headquartered in Sydney, Australia, with research and production facilities nationwide and offices globally. Advanced Navigation is an Australian manufacturer exporting worldwide. #JoinTheAutonomyRevolution

Name: 10x
Description: AI powered Super Humanoids for Physical World.

Name: PickNik Robotics
Description:
PickNik accelerates robotics automation, helping companies simplify, accelerate, and deliver unstructured robotics solutions to optimize operations, foster efficiency, and innovate faster.

MoveIt Pro is PickNik's flagship product, a commercial-grade platform for accelerating the development of unstructured robotic arm applications, reducing engineering time and support costs by up to 75%. The platform for quickly building robotic arm applications, with out-of-the-box reference applications, a powerful runtime engine, and easy user interfaces that enables advanced, customizable robotic solutions for all industries.

Name: Blue Robotics
Description: Blue Robotics makes low-cost, high-performance components for marine robotics to enable the next generation of ocean exploration! Based in Torrance, CA, we are a young and innovative company with a diverse and international customer base.

Name: Ocean Robotics Planet (formerly ROV Planet)
Description:
The Ocean Robotics Planet Magazine is the most focused publication on Unmanned Underwater Vehicles (ROV, AUV, USV) in the Subsea Construction, Defence, Science, Offshore Oil&Gas, and Offshore Renewable Industries.

www.OceanRoboticsPlanet.com

Name: Voliro
Description:
Voliro is developing advanced flying robots to perform Inspection and Maintenance tasks more safely, cost-effectively and faster than conventional methods.

With its tiltable rotor system, Voliro expands the abilities of service drones from “fly and see” to “fly, see and touch”. Voliro T, our flying robot, can be equipped with a variety of payloads, such as Non-Destructive Testing (NDT) or Lightning Protection System (LPS) sensors. The unique 360° drone design of Voliro T allows inspection of curved and sloped surfaces. No other flying inspection robot boasts these capabilities.

Voliro aims to provide innovative UAVs with unique capabilities. We promote the concept of omnidirectional aerial vehicles, where position and orientation of the flying platform are completely independent. This ability is crucial to achieve robust and reliable interaction with the environment.

Name: Engineered Arts
Description:
World leaders in humanoid robots for entertainment, information, education and research.
Engineered Arts Ltd produce a range of humanoids including, Ameca our flagship communication humanoid, Ameca Desktop,  RoboThespian the original robot actor and Mesmer, stunning ultra-realistic robots.

Name: Liquid Robotics
Description: Liquid Robotics, a wholly owned subsidiary of The Boeing Company, designs and manufactures the Wave Glider, the world’s first wave and solar powered uncrewed ocean robot. Based in Herndon, Virginia, and with an Engineering & Test Facility in Hawai’i, Liquid Robotics works with global partners to address many of the planet’s greatest challenges, by transforming how to assess, monitor, and protect the ocean. Since 2007, Liquid Robotics has conducted 100s of Wave Glider missions around the globe, from the Arctic to Antarctica, for customers in the defense, commercial, and science markets.

Name: Nexxis
Description:
Hi we’re Nexxis,

We’re about so much more than just hiring and selling top quality technical equipment.

Our flexible, solutions-driven approach helps our customers make smarter and more profitable business decisions of their own.  When they choose Nexxis as their technical equipment supplier, they enter into a value-driven partnership which enables them to achieve better outcomes for their business and their own clients too.

At the heart of our operation is the supply of an extensive range of leading-edge inspection, testing and measuring equipment for a variety of applications including offshore oil rigs, subsea installations, engines, machinery, mining operations, infrastructure projects and manufacturing processes.   All these products are available for hire, lease or purchase.

We know from experience that industrial projects are fluid and that requirements change all the time.  We also know that our customers’ circumstances are constantly changing and that they need rapid and practical responses.  That’s where our ‘flexible adaptability’ gives our customers a distinct competitive advantage.

Our whole aim is to reduce project downtime so when you buy, lease or hire any of our technical equipment, you enjoy innovative and flexible contract options, the very best and most advanced equipment on the market, highly skilled and experienced technical support and the benefit of our downtime mitigation guarantee.

As a business we keep up with rapidly evolving technological developments and bring you the most advanced equipment in the world with a continually expanding our range to ensure we have the right equipment to suit your needs.

We’re with you all the way. Comprehensive training on all of our products, either at our Perth offices or on-site, ensure that you maximise the benefit of your investment, plus our team of highly skilled and field-trained technicians are available 24/7 for emergency support.

Name: GrayMatter Robotics
Description:
We’re solving the most difficult surface treatment and finishing challenges high-mix, high-variability manufacturers face with our advanced robotics and physics-informed AI technologies. We are transforming the future of manufacturing with robotics and our proprietary GMR-AI, designed into smart robotic cells that assist humans in tedious and ergonomically challenging tasks. Our mission is to enhance human productivity and improve the quality of life by letting robots empower people.

Based in Los Angeles, California, GrayMatter Robotics has been voted one of the top 50 Best Places to Work in LA by BuiltIn.  We also won the RBR50 Award in 2022 as one of the top 50 innovators in the robotics market.

Name: Pyka
Description:
Pyka’s goal is to provide society with a new form of safe, clean, and cost-effective transportation enabled by autonomous electric aviation. 

To get there, we’re taking a different approach than most. We're applying our technologies to every industry where autonomous electric aircraft can be useful, starting with the highest value and most dangerous jobs. In doing so, we're building game-changing products manufactured at scale, while perfecting the safety, reliability, and capabilities of our autonomy engine and electric propulsion systems. 

We design, develop and manufacture an ecosystem of technologies including proprietary flight control software, avionics, high power density motors, motor controllers, batteries, and custom carbon-fiber composite airframes.

Today, we supply autonomous electric aircraft for cargo transport and crop protection to real-world customers across four separate continents and have secured industry-first regulatory approvals from the FAA. 

Our cargo aircraft enables remote connectivity, enhances express delivery networks, and ensures fast and reliable shipping of critical supplies to areas in need. Our crop protection aircraft offers agricultural services providers and farmers an autonomous tool to make aerial application safer, more precise, and less harmful to surrounding environments. Both vehicles are highly economical to operate, easy to deploy, and significantly reduce C02 emissions in their respective industries.  

What we work on at Pyka makes people’s lives better now and brings the future of electric aviation one step closer each day.

Name: Ambi Robotics
Description: We're empowering people to handle more.

Name: RIVR
Description: RIVR is a robotics company pioneering Physical AI for doorstep delivery. We combine neural networks with innovative wheeled-legged robots to automate the most labor-intensive part of delivery, bringing packages and food directly to consumers’ doorsteps, while mastering real-world challenges like climbing stairs, navigating cluttered spaces, and opening gates and doors. With customers already requiring tens of thousands of robots, RIVR is on track to generate more daily data than YouTube within four years, unlocking a powerful data flywheel for General Physical AI.

Name: The Construct Robotics Institute
Description:
Where Your Robotics Career Happens

The Construct is your platform for becoming a Robotics Developer from scratch. Explore ROS & Robotics learning resources on our website: https://www.theconstructsim.com/.

Name: CynLr
Description: CynLr is a visual object intelligence platform that enables industrial robotic arms to see, understand and manipulate any object in random unstructured environments.

Name: Energy Robotics
Description:
Energy Robotics is the AI platform that bridges the gap between human led and fully autonomous operations. 

Oil & Gas companies across the globe partner with us to remove the complexity from their operations, using our platform to orchestrate different types of robots and drones to collect data across their facilities. 

Our platform collects all of this data, conducts analysis and enrichment with our industry specific LLM before feeding it back to operational team members. 

What does this mean? 

Improved efficiency, less outages, 24/7 asset monitoring, lengthened asset life cycles and much more. 

Visit our website to learn about the power of Energy Robotics Enterprise AI Platform...

Name: EyeROV (IROV TECHNOLOGIES PRIVATE LIMITED)
Description:
EyeROV (IROV Technologies Pvt. Ltd.) vision is to be the technology leader and solution provider in underwater critical infrastructure inspections and surveys. EyeROV TUNA, our first product - a smart and efficient micro-ROV(Remotely Operated Vehicle)/underwater drone was our first step towards this vision. 

The company is founded by IIT Alumnus Johns T Mathai and Kannappa Palaniappan P. Johns did his Master from IIT Delhi in Electrical Engineering(2013) and Kannappa Palaniappan P in Ocean Engineering from IIT Madras(2014). Johns then worked in Samsung R&D Bangalore and Grey Orange Robotics. Kannappa has Industrial experience working in Saint Gobain and  National Institute of Ocean Technology (NIOT). The company also has reputed advisors from ex-DRDO, ex-Navy and Marine Industry. The company is now incubated at Maker Village an initiative of Govt of India and Govt of Kerala.

Name: Bluewhite
Description:
The Bluewhite mission is to build resilient farms by maximizing the grower’s productivity and reducing their operational cost. We equip existing fleets with autonomous technology, an easy-to-use platform, and end-to-end service; supporting their journey to a more profitable and sustainable farm. 
Together, we will Grow Forward.

Name: Alphadroid
Description:
Reimagine how your business operates with Alphadroid. 

We build robotic solutions and conversational AI that adapt to your needs, lighten the load, and make your business sharper, quicker, and better at what it does. 

Our robotic solutions don’t replace your team; they work with them to deliver better results. 

Hospitals, airports, hotels, retailers, and restaurants use Alphadroid to cut wait times, reduce operating costs, and deliver smoother, faster service. 

Our smart robotic solutions handle everyday tasks so your people can focus on what matters most: 
- Alpha Smart: zips food and goods securely 
- Alpha Signature: clears plates and keeps things tidy 
- Hey Alpha: answers customer queries in any language 
- Alpha Envoro: manages and sorts waste efficiently 
- Alpha Shine: keeps spaces spotless in no time 

Work smarter and move faster with Alphadroid 

Book a product walkthrough: https://meetings-eu1.hubspot.com/alphadroid

Name: Rekise Marine
Description: Autonomous platforms and sustainable infrastructure for the next generation maritime economy.

Name: Micropolis Robotics
Description:
We’re changing the way people think of mobility.

We know for a fact that the future is autonomous.
Micropolis, through years of research & development, is positioned to make functional transportation safer, cleaner, and more accessible.

In order to achieve this goal, we embarked on a journey to reimagine transportation.

We are a catalyst in the paradigm shift towards sustainable mobility.
Micropolis delivers mobility-as-a-service to dense urban environments with end-to-end management, maintenance, and upgrades for our fleet of vehicles.

Strategic partnerships accelerate innovation.
Micropolis joined forces with Dubai Police in 2020, and together, ushered in a new era for autonomous rovers.

Much has been learned on the road to re-discovery.
Roads are inherently unsafe and can be stressful.
Our testing focuses on real-life scenarios as we move towards launching our first fleet.

Name: Bedrock Robotics
Description: Bedrock Robotics brings advanced autonomy to the built world, helping the construction industry build at the pace today's society demands. Our technology upgrades existing heavy equipment, enabling truly autonomous operation with expert level quality and superhuman safety. At a time when we need to build faster than ever—from housing to data centers to factories and energy infrastructure—autonomous construction isn't just an innovation, it's an economic necessity.

Name: Built Robotics
Description: Built Robotics’ mission is to build the robots that build the world. By automating repetitive tasks in the $300 billion solar industry, Built is bridging the workforce gap to accelerate our transition to clean energy.

Name: Robotnik Automation
Description:
Robotnik is a company with an extensive and innovative experience, specialized in mobile service robotics.

Our main specialities are:

   - Mobile robot platform development and manufacturing. 
   - Mobile service robotics R&D and engineering projects.

We offer high-quality services for national and international reach and are certified in:

    ISO 9001:08 - Design, manufacturing and commercialization of products and systems based on robotics technology.

    UNE 166002:06 - R&D management in the development of projects in the field of robotics.

    INNOVATIVE SME

Name: Trossen Robotics
Description:
Our drive is a passion for helping innovators innovate.

Trossen Robotics is a leading provider of affordable and versatile robotic platforms designed to accelerate innovation in research and industry. Specializing in advanced robotic arms and AI-driven hardware solutions, Trossen Robotics equips organizations—from global institutions like Google DeepMind and Stanford to innovative startups—with powerful tools for machine learning, data collection, and automation. Committed to fostering collaboration, Trossen Robotics supports customers through customized solutions, comprehensive resources, and international partnerships, empowering users to achieve groundbreaking advancements in robotics and artificial intelligence.

Name: Sunseeker Robotics
Description:
Sunseeker was born with the motivation to merge lawn care solutions with cutting-edge technologies. We believe effortless experience should be given to everyone and continuously push the boundaries of what lawn care technology can achieve.

As an innovator, we deliver technologically advanced products to our customers. Our product portfolio encompasses a diverse range of intelligent robots and commercially-focused equipment. These offerings stand as a testament to our relentless pursuit of technological advancement and excellence.

We stand for the love of lawns and the tech that makes them shine. This synergy birthed Sunseeker, uniting homeowners and landscapers with robots and equipment that empower them to craft, innovate, and harness the essence of their outdoor spaces in a fulfilling and rewarding way.

Mission
Bring intelligent, high-tech and eco-friendly outdoor equipment into every lawn, creating a more wonderful outdoor life for every household

Vision
Lead the way in revolutionizing Lawn care with robots and Quality-focused equipment

Core Value 
Honesty | Pragmatism | Innovation

Name: mimic
Description: Physical AI to scale your most tedious tasks from manufacturing to logistics. Our robots intuitively learn new skills from you and operate autonomously in any environment.

Name: FERNRIDE
Description:
At FERNRIDE, we believe in a future where robotics unlocks human potential to create a better world. Yet today, humans still perform repetitive, risky tasks to keep our world running. These jobs, essential yet unattractive, should be performed by robots, which still fall short. We are on a mission to change that and transform critical industries through human-centric automation. 

FERNRIDE delivers a comprehensive ground autonomy platform to offer scalable automation solutions across industries. They span from container terminals and yard operations via defence logistics to open-road trucking. By combining AI-powered autonomy with human oversight and modular, vehicle-agnostic hardware, we enable businesses and defence organizations to harness the full potential of automation, driving efficiency and safety.

FERNRIDE was founded in 2019 following 10 years of research at the Technical University of Munich and currently has over 150 employees. We are setting the standard for autonomous logistics and advancing Europe’s technological sovereignty by delivering the first-ever fully certified autonomous trucking system in Europe in 2025. 

Physical AI is the biggest transformational technology of our time. Backed by €75 million in venture funding from top-tier investors and trusted by blue chip customers, such as HHLA, Volkswagen, and DB Schenker, we are on a mission to build Europe’s leader in Physical AI, on track to lead the world in next-generation industrial automation.

Name: Matic Robots
Description:
Matic is reimagining the next generation of fully autonomous, helpful, and elegant home robots using AI and computer vision. Level-5 autonomy in your home.

Our flagship product, Matic, is a remarkably smart, proactive, and secure floor-cleaning robot that uses photo-realistic vision with precise 3D mapping to provide a home with perpetually clean floors.

Name: Indy Autonomous Challenge
Description: The IAC organizes fully autonomous racecar competitions among university teams from around the world and works to further automated vehicle technology. The IAC is aiming to establish a hub for performance automation in Indiana by harnessing the power of competition to attract the best and the brightest minds from around the globe.

Name: Atomic Machines
Description: Atomic Machines is on Earth to radically advance humanity's command of matter.  Our MC-1 manufacturing technology embodies this mission at the micro-scale, both blowing open the design space ("MEMS 2.0") and doing so in a way that enables extremely rapid iteration and scaling to high-volume manufacturing ("thought to thing time").  Our first device product establishes a new price-performance class highly relevant to the AI data center space.

Name: Avular
Description: Avular accelerates the creation of mobile robot applications that will shape a brighter future for all. By developing the core technology for autonomous robots and building our own drones and robots from the ground up, we developed the perfect tools to start your mobile robotic solution. By simplifying the implementation of robot development in practice we significantly reduce our partners' time-to-market and costs.

Name: Nomagic
Description:
Nomagic delivers AI powered robotic picking solutions that transform how warehouses operate. Our advanced vision systems and machine learning algorithms enable robots to handle millions of different items with speed and precision, helping e-commerce, retail and logistics leaders achieve new levels of efficiency, accuracy and scalability.

From piece-picking automation to seamless integration with warehouse management systems (WMS), Nomagic’s technology is designed to reduce operational costs, solve labour shortages and meet ever growing customer expectations.

We work with leading brands and fulfilment providers worldwide to optimise their order fulfilment, returns processing and inventory management, all while delivering a faster, more reliable supply chain.

Specialties: AI robotics, warehouse automation, robotic picking, computer vision, machine learning, fulfilment optimisation, e-commerce logistics, retail supply chain solutions.

Mission: To make warehouses smarter, more flexible and more sustainable through cutting-edge robotics and AI.

Name: Novarc Technologies Inc.
Description: Novarc Technologies is a full-stack robotics company specializing in the design and manufacturing of cobots and AI systems for robotic welding applications.

Name: Exail Technologies (EPA: EXA ; OTCQX: EXALF)
Description: Exail Technologies is an industrial company specialized in advanced technologies in the fields of autonomous robotics, navigation, aerospace and photonics. Based on a strong entrepreneurial culture, Exail Technologies ensures performance, reliability and safety for its civilian and military customers operating in harsh environments. From the bottom of the ocean to the farthest reaches of space, the group expands its customers' capabilities through its components, products and systems. Exail Technologies generates revenues in nearly 80 countries.

Name: Inser Robótica
Description:
Inser Robótica is an industrial automation engineering company that supports its customers in the definition, development and implementation of automation projects that are viable, efficient and adapted to real production environments.

Our differentiating value lies in combining strong technical judgment, deep industrial experience and a practical business-oriented approach, transforming operational needs into automation solutions that work reliably, stand the test of time and deliver efficiency from day one.

We approach each project from a global perspective, taking care of engineering, system integration and commissioning, with a strong focus on reducing risk, ensuring system reliability and enabling future scalability.

We work as a long-term technology partner, fully involved in our customers’ projects, so that automation becomes a real driver of operational improvement rather than an end in itself.

Name: INETEC
Description:
We are INETEC – a high-tech Croatian company that has been designing and manufacturing robots, instruments, software, controllers and probes for the nuclear, aerospace and medical industries on the global market for 30 years. 
Our scope of work also includes inspections of vital components of nuclear power plants. Our products and services meet the highest standards such as ISO 9001, ISO 14001, OHSAS 18001, ISO/IEC 17025 and other.
One of the most exciting projects we are involved in is ITER, an international project dedicated to the development of a thermonuclear reactor, or fusion. 
We are also proud of our NERO project – an innovative robotic system for neurosurgical procedures.

In addition, we have taken part in numerous international development, research and education projects in cooperation with faculties, the International Atomic Energy Agency, etc.

Since 1991, we have been running the NDE School and Training Center, and in 2020, we founded the INETEC Academy, which we use to conduct professional training sessions for our employees, interns and students. 
We also support new generations of engineers and experts, and we organize paid summer student internships and award prizes to top students each academic year.

Our team consists of people who keep realizing their potential in the field of robotics, automation, mechanical engineering and software design, as well as electrical engineering and mechatronics. 
Each of our employees gets to work with highly qualified experts in the field  and is offered a culture where one receives support in turning their innovative ideas into market products and solutions, as well as career development plans.

Our guiding motto is ‘Creating a reliable future’, and we believe that investing in a secure future is now more important than ever. Join our vision!

Name: HG Robotics
Description:
HG Robotics is a pioneering Robotics and Drone Technology company dedicated to leveraging advanced, locally-developed technology to help people and industries achieve higher productivity, safety, and sustainability. 

Our core strength is built on Autonomous Mobile Robotics developed by a Multidisciplinary Team of engineers and specialists. We combine sophisticated Machine Intelligence with Human Insight to create systems that are smart, reliable, and deeply aligned with user needs. 

Our Core Expertise and Heritage:
Our history is rooted in developing customized, complex unmanned vehicle systems across various challenging environments: 
Aerial: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) and drones. 
Ground: Autonomous ground robotics and land-based systems. 
Marine & Subsea: Autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) and remotely operated vehicles (ROVs). 

Strategic Focus on AgTech
Evolving from the foundational expertise of HiveGround Co., Ltd. (founded in 2011), we strategically transformed into the startup HG Robotics (HGR) in 2016. We channeled our robust robotics expertise toward high-impact commercial solutions, leading us to focus on agriculture. Today, we are exclusively concentrated on our flagship product line, TIGERDRONE.

TIGERDRONE: Complete Agriculture Drone Solution
We provide a complete agricultural drone solution—from planning and flying to sensing, analyzing, and action—to empower modern farmers, increase efficiency, and promote sustainable practices.

Funding History
Our growth has been accelerated by strong support from strategic investors across multiple rounds:
Seed (2016): NVEST Venture
Series A (2018): SCG and PTT
Series B (2021): True, CP, and PTT
Series B+ (2023): CU Enterprise, Taihos (Japan), and DC Cruz (Philippines)
Additional support from individual investors across all rounds.

Name: Antt AI
Description:
Antt AI is pioneering the era of Physical AI by building intelligent, autonomous agents for the real world.
We design and deploy semi-humanoid robots that automate tasks across hospitality, retail, and last-mile delivery—enhancing customer experience and operational efficiency.

Our robots combine advanced computer vision, real-time decision-making, and robust hardware integration to navigate and interact in complex environments. Powered by our proprietary AI agent stack, Antt AI is bridging the gap between digital intelligence and physical action.

We're on a mission to reimagine service, mobility, and labor through embodied AI.

Name: Robotizr
Description:
Software programming of industrial machinery (e.g. robotic arms) is a complex task that requires a qualified technical figure. For manufacturing companies owning  industrial machinery, this means hiring or training technical personnel or adopting external consultants: in both scenarios, they end up facing high waiting times and costs. Our solution is aimed at simplifying and democratizing this process.
Our product is composed of 1) a plug&play electronic device (Robotizr Edge) to be connected to the machinery, and 2) a web application (Robotizr Manager) to be used for programming. The web interface relies on a no-code visual system that does not require the knowledge of programming languages, enabling everybody to implement industrial automation.

Name: TACTUN
Description:
TACTUN provides a comprehensive solution for smart machinery manufacturers, allowing them to create advanced machinery without needing programming skills. We offer two key products: a powerful hardware platform and a software application builder. Our hardware platform combines FPGA, CPU, and NVIDIA Jetson, handling AI inference, sensor data acquisition, and actuator control—all through no-code configuration.  
Our cloud-based software platform allows manufacturers to design interfaces, define operations, and deploy custom AI models, letting them focus on innovation while TACTUN manages the complex technical aspects.

Every complex physical system can be made much faster and easier. 

Proud participant in Plug and Play’s Accelerator – Armenia, 2024 Cohort

Name: TetherIA

Ground: Autonomous ground robotics and land-based systems.

Funding History (HG Robotics Co., Ltd.):

Seed (2016): NVEST Venture
Series A (2018): SCG and PTT
Series B (2021): True, CP, and PTT
Series B+ (2023): CU Enterprise, Taihos (Japan), and DC Cruz (Philippines)
Additional support from individual investors across all rounds.

At ARAPL RaaS (Robotics-as-a-Service), we are redefining the future of intralogistics with intelligent, autonomous, and affordable robotic solutions delivered on a flexible, pay-as-you-go model. ARAPL RaaS is a bold new initiative from ARAPL, built on the powerful concept of "Robots as a Service"—providing customers with cutting-edge automation on demand, typically billed by the hour.

Our flagship products include Autonomous Forklifts, Reach Trucks, and Pallet Jacks, supported by a full-stack mobile robotics platform purpose-built to streamline material movement across warehouses, factories, ports, and last-mile operations.

Our next-gen robots—Atlas, Maia, Hercules, Zeus, and Daedalus—are engineered with ambient intelligence, open platform compatibility, and seamless system integration. Powered by proprietary controllers, layered AI/ML, and fully in-house hardware, they deliver unmatched flexibility and data-rich performance at scale.

Headquartered in India with a growing global presence, ARAPL RaaS is committed to making high-performance robotics accessible to businesses of every size. We’re not just building machines—we’re enabling a smarter, more connected, and autonomous supply chain ecosystem.

Join us as we revolutionize the $150B global intralogistics market—through innovation, precision, and purpose.

Name: Marso Robotics
Description:
Autonomous robots designed and built for the field, not the lab.

We tackle the hardest manual tasks in logistics, the ones that resist traditional automation. Our systems deploy fast, without WMS integration, and operate reliably in brownfield warehouses where 93% of flows remain manual.

Our founding team built and deployed industrial-grade robotics systems worldwide, at the scale of thousands of robots and hundreds of millions in revenue at Exotec, Parrot, and Decathlon. 

At Marso, we combine this operational heritage with the latest advances in embodied AI. Our proprietary control stack combines transformer-based action models, RL-trained behaviors, and teleoperation fallback, enabling autonomy at industrial levels of precision.

Supported by industry operators across AI research, robotics, and large-scale logistics, we are quietly building the next layer of embodied intelligence for industry.

Name: NUST Robotics & AI
Description:
NUST Robotics & Artificial Intelligence (NUST RAI) is a tech enterprise which aims to promote the culture of robotics and artificial intelligence in Pakistan. Based in the National University of Sciences and Technology-Pakistan Navy Engineering College (NUST PNEC), the team’s objective is to explore the power of autonomous software and its integration with advanced electro-mechanical systems. We wish to spread awareness amongst the students of Pakistan and beyond to explore this field, as we spear-head the wave of robotics in Pakistan.

Our journey began in 2021, when this team participated in RoboFiesta 4.0 at HITEC University, Taxila, Pakistan. Our first competition, yet we returned home with the runners-up cup in the fire-fighting module. 

Fast forward a few months into 2022, the team attempted its first stand-alone project: CY, a humanoid robot designed for future human interactions. The goal of the project was to manufacture an interactive and reliable robotic prototype with efficient information delivery, making CY intelligent, entertaining and versatile.  Fully completed and operational, CY showed the ability to be customized according to its audience, taking it to higher levels of efficiency. 

As of now, NUST RAI is still basking in the glory of its highly anticipated and successful humanoid project which was unveiled in September 2023. The MARC-1 humanoid project is a testament to the value of hard work, consistency, and the end goal of NUST RAI: to become the pioneer of cutting-edge research and development in the field of Robotics and AI in Pakistan. The MARC-1 project was the first of its kind in the country.

Today, the technical and business divisions at NUST RAI are attempting another first: an international competition with another unprecedented robotic prototype. This time, we plan on transcending the standard of undergraduate excellence. 

Stay tuned. It is an exciting time to be alive :)

Facebook: /nustroboticsai

Name: efeuCampus Bruchsal GmbH, innovation centre for urban, autonomous logistics and transport
Description: efeuCampus Bruchsal GmbH intends to establish the lighthouse project efeuCampus (eco-friendly experimental urban logistics campus) on the site of the former Dragonerkaserne ("efeuQuartier") as part of the RegioWin research project "Innovation centre for autonomous urban freight logistics" funded by the state of Baden-Württemberg. This lighthouse project is to become a permanent source of inspiration for the business location Bruchsal for the development of solutions for last mile freight logistics and autonomous vehicle technology. In the efeuQuartier, a residential quarter of the future is to be created in the medium term as a "habitable laboratory", in which technologies for energy-efficient building and living in the spirit of the energy turnaround will be tested and presented. In this reference quarter, which is unique in Europe, goods of all kinds are to be moved with innovative vehicles, emission-free, generationally appropriate and driverless. The sustainable reduction of motorised delivery traffic on the so-called last mile is intended to reduce energy consumption and CO2 emissions and to decisively improve the quality of life in the neighbourhood. The experience gathered in the efeuCampus Innovation Centre is to be evaluated and published for a wide range of applications. For this communicative interface function internally and externally, the efeuAkademie was founded as a sub-project.

Physical Robotics based in Moss, Norway is pioneering the future of robotics by designing and developing advanced humanoid robots that enhance human lives and revolutionise industries. Our dedication to innovation, quality, and ethical practices drives us to shape a future where humanoid robots will magnify the efficiency of humans, let humans reach higher targets, go further in space..etc. Our ultimate objective is to use humanoid robotics as a tool for societal advancement.
 
We represent the transition of advancement from Generative AI and Deep Reinforcement Learning to a more focused field of robotics. Besides the cognitive aspects of intelligence, such as understanding, reasoning, and decision-making,  our Physical Artificial Intelligence deals with learning the movement intelligence, force and touch interaction - that humans are very good at - to equip the robots.

Our Robot π with an exceptionally advanced concept will be the key for data collection and training for Physical Artificial Intelligence as well as being the perfect body to apply those learned skills in everyday tasks.

Description:
COLETEK is a rare breed — a hands-on Australian R&D lab with global reach that designs and builds original products, platforms, and prototypes in-house for clients. Founded by Luke Cole in 2001, we've delivered 150+ cutting-edge projects from mission-critical and safety systems to agriculture, autonomous systems, medtech, environmental robotics, consumer products, and more.

From edge AI and embedded firmware to autonomous robotics and smart mechatronics, we work across the full stack — combining hardware, software, mechanical, and AI under one roof. We’re not service brokers. We’re builders, engineers, and problem-solvers.

– COLETEK.IO: Full-stack product/project management

– AISmartRider.com: Wearable haptic AI for equestrians

Why Clients Work With Us:

 Everything built in-house – no outsourcing
 Proven MVP process – cost-aware, lean, scalable

About the Founder:

Luke Cole built his first robot at age 9. By 17, he was working alongside Australia’s top robotics minds. He helped build the world’s first autonomous tractor, created GPS-free indoor tracking systems, and developed AI IoT platforms used in industry, environmental, mission-critical and safety systems.

“Robotics is more than engineering — it’s a mirror for life”
— Luke Cole

Name: Pheratech Systems
Description:
Pheratech builds decentralized swarm intelligence for autonomous systems.

We design edge-based autonomy where every unit processes its own sensor data, plans locally, and communicates over a mesh network. No central controller. No single point of failure. Our approach enables multi-agent coordination in low-connectivity, high-complexity environments.

The core autonomy stack supports real-time decision-making, dynamic tasking, and cooperative behaviors across drones, ground vehicles, and maritime platforms. It works out of the box and scales across missions — perimeter security, infrastructure inspection, ISR, convoy support, and more.

Pheratech is venture-backed and advised by senior leaders from defense, homeland security, and national labs. We’re already deploying systems and expanding into commercial and dual-use markets.

We’re hiring across autonomy, systems, and operations.

Name: RoboStar Centre for Software Engineering for Robotics
Description:
RoboStar is a centre of excellence dedicated to research and technology transfer in the area of Software Engineering for Robotics.

Robostar is one of the largest research groups in the world, bringing together a diverse membership of researchers working in robotics under a single umbrella. Its membership comprises UK researchers from the universities of York, Sheffield, Surrey, King’s College, and from Thales.

Our research and development focuses on model-based software engineering: modelling, simulation, testing, and verification, covering control software, physical models of the platform and scenarios, environment assumptions, and human behaviour.

Name: LOGIBIOTECH
Description:
LOGIBIOTECH è stata fondata nel 2019 con l’obiettivo di sviluppare soluzioni avanzate di robotica e RPA per il settore sanitario. 
Grazie alla combinazione di dispositivi robotizzati, software personalizzati, algoritmi di AI e machine learning, LOGIBIOTECH è un partner ideale per tutte le organizzazioni che hanno l’esigenza di ottimizzare i processi di manipolazione dei campioni, garantendo tracciabilità e rintracciabilità, custodia e archiviazione in modo completo, efficiente e sicuro.
LOGIBIOTECH ha sviluppato il suo primo sistema integrato per rispondere alle esigenze di un ambito sempre più strategico per la salvaguardia dei campioni, quello dell’anatomia patologica e sono in fase di sviluppo nuove soluzioni sempre per il settore sanitario.
LOGIBIOTECH vuole far crescere la sua rete di collaborazioni B2B ed è un partner ideale per aziende interessate all’ingegnerizzazione di applicazioni con le ultime tecnologie disponibili sul mercato e a commercializzazioni di successo.
LOGIBIOTECH fa parte del Gruppo CGI (CGI Holding Srl).


LOGIBIOTECH was founded in 2019 with the aim of developing advanced solutions in robotics and RPA for the healthcare sector.
Thanks to a combination of robotic devices, custom software, AI algorithms and machine learning, LOGIBIOTECH is the perfect partner for all organizations that need to optimize their sample handling processes, ensuring complete, efficient and secure tracking, tracing, custody and storage.
LOGIBIOTECH has created its first integrated system of solutions to respond to the needs of pathological anatomy, a strategic sector for the management and custody of samples and new solutions are being developed for the healthcare sector.
We want to expand our network of B2B collaborations and are the ideal partner for companies interested in engineering applications with the latest technologies available on the market, as well as in successful marketing.
LOGIBIOTECH is part of the CGI Group (CGI Holding Srl).

Name: ARAS
Description:
ARAS Research group originated in 1997 and is proud of its 20+ years of brilliant background, and its contributions to the advancement of academic education and research in the field of Robotics. The main asset of the research group is its human resources devoted all their time and effort to the advancement of science and technology. This includes the internationally-recognized faculty members, staff, and talented and motivated students enrolled at different undergraduate and graduate levels. Benefiting from this asset in addition to well-equipped laboratories, workshops, and educational facilities in a dynamic and pleasant environment provides the required means to accomplish research at the leading edge of science and technology. One of our main objectives is to use these potentials to extend our educational and industrial collaborations at both national and international levels. In order to accomplish that, our mission is to enhance the breadth and enrich the quality of our education and research in a dynamic environment.

Professor S. Ali. A. Moosavian (Department of Mechanical Engineering), Professor Hamid D. Taghirad (Department of Electrical Engineering) & Professor Ali Ghaffari (Department of Mechanical Engineering)

Name: MZX Robotics
Description:
Évek óta foglalkozunk a digitális transzformáció problematikáival, különös mértékben fókuszálva a STEAM képzési vonatkozásokra, a jövő generációi tudatos formálásának céljával. Egyedülálló edukációs célú robotikai termékportfolió és képzési segédletek piaci képviselete és támogatása.

Sok tucatnyi oktatási intézménnyel végzett közös munka mellett 2018-ban elnyertük a német-magyar iparkamara szakképzési díját, a Mechatronikai Szakgimnáziummal - a kooperációs kategóriában - közösen végzett munkánkkal, mely a leginnovatívabb oktatási projekt díját érdemelte ki.

Eddigi munkánk koronája, hogy 2021 második felében 420-nál is több alapfokú hazai oktatási intézményben jelennek meg humanoid robotjaink, a digitális kompetencia képzés eddigi talán legjelentősebb és leghatékonyabb lépéseként, immáron a közoktatás részévé téve a digitális felzárkóztatást. Nyiss velünk a robotikára!

Az oktatási célú robotikai termékeink mellett szolgáltató, és ipari robotok kereskedelmével is foglalkozunk, melyek az elöregedő társadalmakból és a munkarőhiányból fakadó nehézségekre kínálhatnak megoldásokat.

Name: FRAMEBOTIX
Description:
About Us
Welcome to FRAMEBOTIX!

We are a passionate team of engineers and professionals with decades of experience in developing autonomous robots integrated with advanced AI. Our journey encompasses numerous successful projects, each contributing to our profound expertise and innovative approach in robotics.

At Frambotix, our mission is to revolutionize the way robots assist humans. Our robots are meticulously designed to autonomously gather data, derive optimal paths, and manufacture parts according to specific designs, all without the need for manual programming. This cutting-edge technology is poised to address some of the world's most pressing challenges, particularly the global housing crisis, which affects not only the United States and North America but also the entire world.

Recognizing the transformative potential of our technology, we have established Frambotix as a specialized entity dedicated to leveraging our innovations to enhance quality of life and drive positive change globally. Unlike traditional models of leasing and financing factories, we own our factory outright and focus solely on selling the manufacturing cost.

Our unique pricing model is based on the square/linear foot price of production, which varies depending on the number of homes produced annually: the higher the number, the lower the cost. This approach allows us to scale our production facilities efficiently and offers a promising solution to the worldwide housing crisis.

Thank you for visiting our website and learning about our journey and mission. Together, we can create a brighter future with innovative robotics and a commitment to making a difference.

Description:
Team Abhiyaan is an enthusiastic group of multi-disciplinary students of IIT Madras, committed to building intelligent, robust, precise and safe ground navigation systems. As a competition team of 45 members, we participate in an international robotics competition called IGVC (Intelligent Ground Vehicle Competition) held at Oakland University, Michigan, USA. Participants are given a problem statement using which robots have to navigate autonomously through laned obstacles in Auto Nav challenge of IGVC.
With the vision of creating autonomous navigation systems to negate human risk, we aim to designing a six-seater golf cart to operate autonomously in closed and restricted environments like institutes, airports and gated communities.
Currently Abhiyaan is also working on a project called MACRo (Martian Autonomous Core Sampling Rover) in collaboration with Extra-Terrestrial Manufacturing (ExTeM), which aims to build an autonomous rover capable of collecting core samples from Martian surfaces. The rover will be built to withstand the intense Martian conditions and we intend to drill 1 foot for more in-depth research and analysis, whereas current rovers can only drill 2.75 inches.
Our goal is to strive for and progress toward the vision of developing autonomous systems that can help solve many real-world problems and assist humans in their daily activities.

Name: Human-Robot Interaction Pioneers
Description:
The 21st annual Human-Robot Interaction Pioneers Workshop will be held on March 16th, 2026 in conjunction with the 2026 ACM/IEEE International Conference on Human-Robot Interaction. 

HRI Pioneers seek to foster creativity and collaboration surrounding key challenges in human-robot interaction and empower students early in their academic careers. Each year, the workshop brings together a cohort of the world's top student researchers and provides the opportunity for students to present and discuss their work with distinguished student peers and senior scholars in the field.

Pioneers is a premier forum for students in HRI; thus, we invite students at any stage of their academic career to consider applying. To facilitate attendance, we aim to provide some support for travel and lodging expenses to accepted Pioneers. However, this is contingent on available funding and it is likely that not all Pioneers will be able to receive support. As our funding sources are separate for participants from US-based institutions and those from outside the US, the available support might differ between these two categories. Additionally, we can no longer guarantee funding for all Pioneer attendees. We therefore encourage all US-based and non-US-based applicants to also apply for external travel grants, including those available through the main conference, as well as student volunteer positions, for those who require this support.

Dalla gestione e manutenzione di infrastrutture critiche al monitoraggio delle colture agricole, i nostri sistemi innovativi lavorano 24/7 senza necessità di intervento umano.

Chi siamo : Siamo una startup innovativa, nata dall’esperienza decennale dei nostri fondatori nel campo della robotica autonoma. Il nostro obiettivo è chiaro: trasformare la robotica in una soluzione modulare e autonoma per settori come energia, trasporti, agricoltura e logistica.

Cosa facciamo : Progettiamo e realizziamo piattaforme robotiche dotate di intelligenza artificiale per operare in ambienti complessi. Le nostre soluzioni permettono di monitorare e gestire infrastrutture critiche e sensibili, come linee elettriche, aeroporti, cantieri e grandi impianti industriali. Inoltre, siamo pionieri nell'automazione agricola, offrendo sistemi robotici per la gestione completa di vigneti e altre coltivazioni.

Perché scegliere RaFla

Efficienza h24: I nostri robot operano in maniera autonoma, anche in ambienti inaccessibili all’uomo.
Sicurezza e riduzione dei costi: Eliminiamo i rischi umani e riduciamo i costi operativi.
Tecnologie avanzate: Grazie a sensori di ultima generazione e algoritmi di intelligenza artificiale, le nostre soluzioni forniscono dati utili per la manutenzione predittiva.
Versatilità: I nostri sistemi robotici possono essere utilizzati in diversi settori, dall'energia all'agricoltura.
Il nostro futuro Stiamo rivoluzionando il settore con soluzioni che integrano tecnologie 4.0 e 5.0 per garantire maggiore sostenibilità ed efficienza operativa. Con un focus su innovazione e tecnologia, immaginiamo un mondo dove le infrastrutture e le colture si autogestiscono, riducendo l'impatto umano e aumentando la produttività.

Unisciti a noi nella rivoluzione della robotica autonoma!

What to include in your submission
Covering letter
Your covering letter should include:

Your full name (and pseudonym if applicable)
Your postal address, telephone number, and email address (if you have one)

Synopsis and manuscript

A synopsis is a brief overview, which can vary in length depending on the nature of your book, but generally it should not exceed 500 words. You will also need to include a copy of your full manuscript.

Description:
Vyom is building the platform to power the future of autonomous robots and drones. 

We are creating a software-defined ecosystem to empowers the robotics industry with unmatched adaptability, flexibility, and scalability, to operate and orchestrate their fleets.

We are building: 

Vyom IQ: Command centre in the cloud to operate, orchestrate, monitor and aggregate data from your robot and drone fleets. It unifies planning, teleoperation, observability, data capture and fleet health monitoring – into a single pane of glass. 

Vyom GCS (Ground Control Station): For more granular mission planning and real-time operation of drones and bots, either autonomously or with human-in-the-loop. 

Vyom AI Pilots: Truly autonomous AI pilots for your drones and robots to ensure they can navigate, optimize flight paths, avoid obstacles and complete missions, on their own if needed.

We call this complete platform, the Vyom OS: A hardware agnostic operating system which ensures easy & optimal operations in the complete value chain, from edge devices in the field to centralized data store.

Description:
Danu Robotics manufactures recycle-able waste sorting robots and computer imaging systems, controlled by AI. 
Our series of robotic systems are designed specifically and only for picking dry mixed recyclable materials, capable of retrofitting into existing manual waste sorting facilities. Optimised for Material Recovery Facility (MRF) operational efficiency, our robot arms use modular and plug and play hardware - high wear component like grippers can be replaced in minutes by on-site operators ensuring minimal downtime and the potential for 24/7 operations. Our computer vision software identifies and outlines objects ensuring highly accurate picking and high purity of separated recyclable materials. 
Danu's demo facility opens in April at the National Robotarium in Edinburgh, appointments are by invite only however we will explore educational opportunities for any organisation also invested the drive to a circular economy and the role recycling plays.

Name: Innobrothers LLC
Description:
InnoBrothers delivers engineering-driven creative designs, builds 3D-printable worlds around products, and compounds value through a growing global community.

1. Engineering-Driven Creative Designs for the 3D Printing Ecosystem
We set out to solve one of the biggest gap in the 3D printing world and it is not hardware, it is the lack of engineering-grade, creatively designed, real-world–ready products. InnoBrothers exists to fill this gap. We design functional, creative, and system-level 3D-printable solutions, developed with real engineering logic and supported by standard industrial components (fasteners, bearings, electronics, mechanical interfaces). Our designs are not “things to print,”they are things to use, assemble, evolve, and build upon.

 2. Turning products into expandable ecosystems
InnoBrothers doesn’t compete with hardware manufacturers, we amplify them. For companies, we create 3D-printable worlds around their existing products: fixtures, mounts, accessories, enclosures, extensions, educational setups, and use-case-specific systems. These worlds transform standalone products into living ecosystems that grow with their users.

3. We’re building a global maker community and a Hub
Designs grow faster when people own them, InnoBrothers is not a file marketplace, it is a community-powered platform. Designers, makers, and engineers don’t just consume designs; they contribute, adapt, and expand them. Each contribution strengthens the ecosystem, multiplies use cases, and creates long-term defensibility through network effects.

Let's Build This Together 
We’re creating a living ecosystem of independent creators who design, share, and improve functional 3D solutions. Every user becomes a potential producer that can utilize our ecosystem.

partners@innobrothers.com

Name: MakerLab Research Institute
Description:
The perfect world is only one generation away. It is really that simple: one generation with the right knowledge, skills, and determination is all it takes to fix things and create a better tomorrow for all of us. 
MakerLab was founded by a team of STEM enthusiasts to help create that generation, and we are instilling in our students the courage to forge ahead, think for themselves, and dare to do things differently. We refer to them as The First Generation.
The first generation to make radical changes and dedicate themselves to correcting past mistakes, the first to invent zero-footprint products and services and eliminate pollution, the first to restore the oceans to their former glory. The first generation with the courage and resolution it takes to stand up, reject conventions, and invent better ways of doing things. The first generation with the advanced technical knowledge and creative skills required to make all their dreams and visions come true.
MakerLab knows exactly what The First Generation needs to set the world ablaze. We equip them with up-to-date mechanical and engineering skills, teach advanced computer and coding tech, contemporary science, and just as valuable, we teach them the importance of thinking in unique ways, being courageous and doing something nobody else has ever done before. 
Our teaching methods are based on the individual level of knowledge and skills in each child, and our curriculum is adaptable - we adjust to the children's pace, not the other way round. Because we think it is crucial that, above everything else, learning new skills should be fun and exciting, and never feel overwhelming or difficult. We want our students to leave the MakerLab workshops with a smile on their faces, and an eagerness to return again.

Name: FLYFARM
Description:
About Us

FlyFarm is pioneering a new frontier in sustainable bio-manufacturing by transforming insects into miniature biofactories.

Harnessing the power of Black Soldier Fly (BSF) technology, we design and deploy advanced, modular systems that convert organic waste into high-value proteins, oils, and functional biomolecules. Our proprietary automation, climate control, and AI-driven production technologies enable scalable, cost-effective BSF operations, even in challenging environments.

Our full-stack insect technology platform is evolving beyond waste valorisation and feed applications, towards next-generation biologics manufacturing. By leveraging genetically enhanced BSF strains, we enable decentralised production of therapeutic peptides and other bioactive compounds with broad applications in both animal and human health.

With operations across Asia Pacific and Australia, and active collaborations with global processors, FlyFarm is building a new model for circular, site-specific biomanufacturing — turning organic waste into valuable, health-focused outputs.

Name: iSpaRo
Description:
iSpaRo'25 1-4 December 2025, Sendai, Japan
The International Conference on Space Robotics (iSpaRo) is a new conference aiming to provide a framework for scientists and industry experts to engage in fruitful discussions and share their insights on the emerging topic of Space Robotics. In the last decade, the advances in in-orbit servicing and satellite operations, the renewed interest in the Moon, recent discoveries in the exploration of Mars and Near-Earth Asteroids (NEAs), together with the emergence of new ventures aimed at the prospection, extraction, and utilization of space resources have sparked a new wave of research and commercial opportunities. Traditional and new space companies are pushing the limits of in-space activities with robotics as the disruptive technology and enabler of the next advances in this new space era. 
iSpaRo seeks to fill the gap between established robotics conferences and non-per-reviewed aerospace conferences by allowing the research community and industry professionals to engage in rigorous discussions and share their latest findings in the field. The conference aims to push the boundaries of the field of Space Robotics by exploring new research and development opportunities. The conference will feature speakers from both industry and academia, providing attendees with valuable insights and perspectives on the latest advancements in the field. The iSpaRo conference also aims to create a network of researchers and industry professionals on this topic, including the new generations, fostering future collaborations and innovations.

Our flagship project, the OpenFlexure Microscope, was first published in 2016 and has been built or used in over 50 countries on all seven continents. OpenFlexure is a vibrant and growing community of makers, designers, teachers, hobbyists and medical professionals, working to make microscopy more accessible.

Costing under £300 in parts, the OpenFlexure Microscope is a fully automated, digital microscope, capable of imaging parasites in blood, cancerous cells in biopsies, or damaged cells in smears. We've also been adopted by groups such as AfricaOSH as a platform for education and training, while local manufacturing keeps control in each community where the device is used.

Lab Automators is a vibrant community originating in the Boston area dedicated to advancing the field of lab automation technology. We orchestrate premier  biotech events, happy hours and product launches, creating a platform for networking and discussion among professionals in the sector. Each event, whether a seasonal premiere or an episode showcasing automated lab builds, brings together a diverse group of vendors and practitioners tasked with envisioning the future of automated lab setups. By doing so, we not only promote the exploration of lab automation technology but also provide a space for engineers and scientists alike to connect face-to-face.

Our events are more than just a showcase of technology; they are a hub for meaningful dialogue. For instance, during our events, attendees have the chance to identify and discuss common pain points in lab automation, fostering a collaborative environment to tackle these issues. The discussion extends to the importance of data management in cloud environments, aiming to bridge the gap between data acquisition on the ground and data management in the cloud. The dream, as expressed by the community, is to make transitioning between these realms as easy as flipping a switch, with short implementation times. The enormous turnout at their events, as noted in one of their meetings at the Liberty Hotel in Cambridge, underscores the value and impact that Lab Automators is bringing to the scientific community in the regions our chapters are in.

Name: EvoLogics GmbH
Description:
EvoLogics GmbH is a German-based high-tech enterprise. The company was founded in 2000 by a group of leading international scientists and R&D experts aimed to develop innovative key technologies for maritime and offshore industries through interdisciplinary cooperation between engineering and life sciences.

EvoLogics GmbH designs and manufactures underwater information and communication systems based on bionic concepts, combining cutting edge engineering with the best ideas found in nature. The advanced product features have become enabling technologies for deep water exploration and production. 

EvoLogics’ products offer highly reliable, flexible and cost-effective solutions for multiple underwater communication, positioning, navigation and monitoring applications. The team strives for innovation and invests its vast experience into developing, manufacturing and supporting  products that deliver an excellent performance and solve the most challenging tasks.

EvoLogics' developments are based on the patented S2C (Sweep Spread Carrier) technology - the most reliable acoustic telemetry available for subsea and offshore applications that provides an independent bidirectional data link along with acoustic positioning, broadcasting and full networking capabilities. The company’s solutions can simultaneously facilitate optimal operation and navigation of subsea robots. They enable retrieving information from various subsea sensors and other subsystems and allow to control complex processes by combining communication with high-accuracy positioning and multiple monitoring options.

S2C systems have been carefully designed for operations in harsh underwater environments and enhanced with special algorithms for signal processing and data management. The company’s extensive experience with sensor integration allows it to provide customers with turn-key solutions ranging from initial deployment up to recovering the equipment.

Name: RoBoa
Description:
RoBoa - The robotic revolution to save disaster
victims and inspect confined spaces!

Disaster relief efforts, for example, after earthquakes, lack technological solutions to quickly find and save victims trapped under debris.
In industrial settings, human entry for inspection and maintenance is dangerous, while existing solutions struggle in winding, cluttered, sharp, or slippery environments. 

RoBoa overcomes these limitations with its cutting-edge, snake-like robot and goes where no humans or other robots can go!
The modular robot combines growing-based locomotion with remarkable maneuverability, environment perception, and the possibility to supply liquids. 

RoBoa’s robotic innovation will transform disaster relief missions to save lives and inspect industrial pipes, sewers, and tanks safely.

We are happy to discuss your potential application - contact us for more information!

As battlefield conditions evolve, technology must evolve with them. Robotics enable operations to be carried out more safely, minimizing risks to human life.

Our solutions:

Linza – multiuse logistical drone, equipped with a gyro-stabilised camera with digital and hybrid zoom and can carry up to 2 kg over a distance of 10 km.

Zoom – EW-resistant reconnaissance Drone, can stay airborne for up to 40 minutes, with a tactical range of 12 km. 

Buria – an autonomous remote weapon station designed for accurate, stand‑off engagement using the Mk 19 / RDS40 grenade launchers or the Browning M2.

PRI incorporates MAX into a complete AV retrofit kit called TONY. TONY AV kits bring rapid and full autonomy to a wide variety of vehicles for passenger and goods transit. The Misty robot provides researchers, innovators and educators with an easy-to-use and creative tool to explore social robotics, ranging from quick and simplistic prototypes to advanced human-robot interactions. By leveraging the power of the social robotics community, Misty users can share their experiences and quickly gain traction in their development and research.

Misty Robotics are creating new opportunities for the research and innovation industry to further understand how social robots will be useful in our society. Misty Robotics believes in the role and positive impact that robots will have in our lives, and is part of the early revolution together with researchers and innovators to define the future role of robots in society.

Name: Kyber Labs
Description:
Want a hand? Wait list is here!
https://airtable.com/appbuPsRZGM5IYJuz/pag3tQl0Z0zzSHyBm/form

Kyber Labs is building robotic manipulation platforms that expand what automation can achieve, starting with the high mix, low volume work that has resisted traditional automation. We believe the true value of humanoid robots lies in their hands and what they can do in the world. Whether fixed at a workstation, on a rail, wheels, or legs, dexterous manipulation is a key unlock for general purpose robotics.

At the core of our platform are hands designed from the ground up for embodied AI. Mechanical compliance and full backdrivability make interactions safe and adaptable, while super low cost makes large scale data collection practical. Even for seemingly simpler tasks, we intentionally apply general purpose hardware because solving them this way ensures the system is future-proof, data rich, and ready to scale into far more complex applications tomorrow.

Our Focus:
● Stepwise path: Starting with targeted industrial problems in manufacturing and assembly, and steadily working up to broader general purpose capabilities.
● Manipulation-first design: Robust, low cost hands with torque control, tactile sensing, and high mechanical compliance to conform naturally to objects.
● Ecosystem building: We can’t build general-purpose robots alone, so we’re partnering with researchers and innovators to provide accessible hardware and tools to accelerate AI driven manipulation.

Name: TUM Autonomous Vehicle Systems Lab
Description:
Welcome to the Autonomous Vehicle Systems (AVS) lab, where the future of intelligent transportation comes to life. At AVS, we are dedicated to advancing the field of autonomous vehicles through pioneering research and innovative education. Our lab explores the core aspects of autonomous systems, emphasizing the development of groundbreaking algorithms for dynamic trajectory planning, behavior prediction, adaptive control, and continuous learning.

Research and Development:
We are on a mission to create the next generation of autonomous vehicles. We are pushing the boundaries of what's possible with a wide range of applications, from conventional autonomous road vehicles like cars and trucks to specialized machines designed for challenging terrains and compact autonomous robots for service tasks. Our goal is to enhance the interaction between vehicles and their environment, ensuring efficient, safe, and reliable operations.

Trustworthy Autonomy:
In an era where safety and efficiency are paramount, our lab is on the cutting edge of creating trustworthy autonomous systems. We are on a mission to develop vehicles that can interact seamlessly with humans and other vehicles. By integrating adaptive dynamic path planning with behavior planners, our algorithms can navigate uncertainties in complex, multi-agent environments, paving the way for robust autonomous operations.

Applied Software Development:
At AVS, theory meets practice in our state-of-the-art simulation environments, where algorithms undergo rigorous testing in both 2D and 3D settings. They leverage detailed models of sensors, vehicle dynamics, and environmental interactions to mirror real-world conditions closely. This approach not only enhances our understanding of algorithm performance but also bridges the gap between simulated testing and real-world application.

#swarms #UnderwaterExploration #AUVs #drones #Multiple_AUVs #Acoustics
