Ryberg’s Omnia UV-C Disinfection Robot sets a new benchmark in autonomous disinfection. It eliminates pathogens with powerful UV-C light and collects real-time data for smarter infection control. Name: iBot Club

By offering indispensable guidance through workshops and tutorials along with equipment to all the interested students, iBot club helps students express their skills, knowledge and creativity through conceptualising and designing state of the art prototypes. iBot club is not leaving any stone unturned in the process of becoming a self-sustained student robotics hub.
The iBot Club is home to many of Insti’s robotics enthusiasts who spend a great chunk of their time designing and prototyping robots which can perform any required task. Name: Heibjerg
Description:
The Heibjerg robot is a concept for removing weeds in gravel or stone-paved areas in an autonomous, safe, and efficient manner.

Automating weeding in gravel and stone-paved areas has many benefits. It reduces the time spent on manual weeding which is associated with pain-inducing working positions and is a time-consuming task. Due to the high cost of labor, a robotic solution has the potential to reduce the cost associated with weed removal or free up time to do more important work.

Using a robot is an energy-efficient solution for weed removal. It is capable of spot treatment, not wasting energy on already clean areas. Using hot water, instead of herbicides, also reduces the environmental impact of weed removal. Water-based weeding also eliminates the risk of fires, known from traditional weed burning.

𝐎𝐮𝐫 𝐭𝐞𝐚𝐦

𝐎𝐮𝐫 𝐦𝐢𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐨𝐧
We want to develop a versatile platform for future extraterrestrial exploration on the moon.  We envision an easily deployable robotic system capable of withstanding significant impacts and navigating challenging terrains. Our robot will be able to fall from great heights, making deployments cheaper and more successful. The robot adapts to various mission objectives, for example the exploration of lunar caves. These caves are believed to harbor valuable resources like ice and provide protection from radiation, dust, meteorites and temperature fluctuations. If we want to stay at the moon, we first must explore the lunar caves, which could be ideal for future human habitats.

Name: DigiAgro
Description:
Om natuur en landbouw meer met elkaar in balans te krijgen staan de land- en tuinbouwsector voor een verduurzamingstransitie naar een natuurvriendelijke kringloopvorm. Dat betekent onder meer het voorkomen van verspilling van meststoffen, water, voedingsstoffen en andere hulpmiddelen. In combinatie met het personeelstekort vraagt dit om de inzet van robots. Een consortium van bedrijven en Noord-Nederlandse kennisinstellingen, onder leiding van TNO, richt zich daarom op de ontwikkeling, levering en gebruik van agrobots in de land- en tuinbouw. In 2027 zijn de prototypes klaar.

Het DigiAgro consortium bestaat uit de bedrijven ABDrone, Batenburg-Beenen, Croptimal, Demcon, GroeNoord, Track32 en de kennisinstellingen TNO (projectleiding), Hanze, NHL Stenden, RUG en Saxion.

DigiAgro wordt financieel mogelijk gemaakt door een subsidie vanuit het Just Transition Fund (Europese Unie, Ministerie van Economische Zaken en Samenwerkingsverband Noord-Nederland (SNN)).

Name: RoboJackets
Description:
The RoboJackets is a group of Georgia Tech students, faculty, and alumni that aims to enhance the understanding of the field of robotics and its applications in depth of knowledge as well as to increase of the number of students exposed to it. We plan to carry out our mission through projects that correspond to the organization’s tenets of promotion, education and advancement.

We strive to be a multifaceted organization that allows students to experience various aspects of robotics. Both depth and breadth are encouraged as this allows the students to work more collaboratively in a group environment as well as have the abilities to pursue projects on their own. Hence our mission is effectively three-fold.

Mission
Promotion:
Through presentation of the existing uses of robotics in both industry and the home, the areas of current research and the possibilities for further application we hope to show the practicality and versatility of the robotics applications as well as the viability of the pursuit of education and careers in its development.

Education:
Through training workshops, competitive robotics, and student designed experimentation, the RoboJackets encourage the application and integration of concepts learned in their regular coursework as well as the understanding of how other disciplines contribute to the success of a project.

Advancement:
Through development projects and student experimentation, the RoboJackets hope to expand both our organizational knowledge base, as well as that of the students involved and the robotics community.

Description:
LEVA is an all-terrain, autonomous legged robot, being developed by our team of 12 engineers and industrial designers from ETH Zürich, ZHAW and ZHdK.
Our goal is to design a robot that carries standardised containers with heavy payloads in them, over stairs, unpaved land and all kinds of challenging terrain.

We are a focus project at ETH Zürich, supported by the Robotic Systems Lab. Follow us for updates about our project, and don't miss the Focus Rollout on the 28th May 2024, where we will present a fully functional prototype.

We aim to foster a dynamic community that propels rapid advancement by linking promising, top-tier individuals to their prospective roles, thereby shaping a future of progress through target-oriented talent acquisition.


We are not just another recruiting company. We're a dynamic, innovative firm that values the unique skills of our team.

Name: Robotics for Space Exploration
Description:
Robotics for Space Exploration (RSX) is a design team at the University of Toronto. It was co-founded in May of 2013 by passionate roboticists, space enthusiasts, and undergraduate engineering students. The recent explosion in interest in space exploration is what triggered the formation of RSX. With organizations working on Mars-bound spacecraft, teams around the world racing to win the Google Lunar X Prize, undergraduate students launching nano-satellites, and companies aspiring to mine asteroids, there has been a shift in the age-old paradigm that only a limited number of highly specialized organizations can partake in space exploration.

Needless to say, space exploration is an exciting and challenging venture. The field of space engineering is growing at a rapid pace and we hope that RSX will play a part in bolstering the interest in space and space engineering, and develop the next generation of skilled Canadian engineers who will be at the forefront of the space industry. By designing, building, and testing robots designed for planetary and lunar exploration, RSX competes in space engineering competitions around the world. At heart, our true passion is manned space exploration, and we hope to build robotic systems to facilitate it.

Name: Ark Robotics
Description:
Ark Robotics is a technology companybuilding core infrastructure for mass autonomous robotics and collaborative autonomy.

Our products allow the operation and real-time coordination of thousands of robots in one-to-many mode, creating asymmetrical advantage across air, ground, and maritime domains. We build software-first, system-agnostic solutions designed for rapid deployment and scale.

Ark Robotics exists to protect the people of Earth. Our goal is to build physical superintelligence for robotics and achieve the highest possible human leverage. We are building a future where robots operate at scale across every sector of the economy — freeing human minds to imagine, create, and innovate. Humans and robots, not humans or robots.

Name: Source Robotics
Description:
Our main mission is to bridge the gap between robotic education, research, and industry with accessible and open-source solutions.
We accomplish this by developing high-performance robotics hardware such as robotic arms, motor controllers, and grippers. Additionally, we actively support and build communities and raise awareness about robotics through engaging content and examples on social media.

Our main products are the Spectral Micro BLDC controller, the PAROL6 robotic arm, and the SSG-48 adaptive electric gripper.

For any questions, feel free to send us a mail at info@source-robotics.com.

Maxtronics, part of Maxvision Technologies, is a french technology company dedicated to developing advanced robotic systems and intelligent solutions that improve people’s life. 


Our main humanoid robot is NAO.

Our mission is to make robotics accessible, useful, ethical, and impactful, supporting progress in education, healthcare, and research through innovation and collaboration.

Name: Devol Robots
Description: Devol Robots is building THE action layer for the physical world: physics-aware embodied AI that enables any robots to see, feel, and predict physical interaction in robotic manipulation. Our AI adapts to new scenarios on the fly and understands objectives instantly from high-level cues—then executes with compliant, human-safe precision across any robot, from robotic arm to bimanual and humanoid systems. Patent-backed and deployment-ready, Devol replaces brittle scripts with goal-seeking autonomy that’s reliable at scale and stunningly capable—turning intent into action, anywhere.

Name: Hyperspawn Robotics
Description:
Hyperspawn is redefining mobility, enabling infinite possibilities for work, exploration, and interaction.

We develop intelligent humanoid robots with the ability to receive and mimic real-time, full-body human motion capture data with unprecedented dexterity for an immersive physical presence, anywhere in the world.

Imagine being able to 'teleport' your skills and presence to any location, instantly.

Description:
Xyronix Labs is a forward-thinking technology company dedicated to innovation in AI, IoT, robotics, and data science. We aim to bridge the gap between cutting-edge technology and real-world applications, creating solutions that enhance lives and businesses. One of our flagship products, the Fire Early Warning and Detection System, ensures safety with advanced AI and IoT capabilities, offering early fire detection, precise localization, and efficient suppression.

Our scalable and reliable solutions cater to diverse industries, including residential, commercial, and industrial sectors. By leveraging intelligent systems powered by neural networks and robust cloud-based platforms, we strive to drive efficiency, safety, and sustainable growth. Guided by the vision of a smarter and safer tomorrow, we focus on creating impactful technologies that embody our motto, Future is Here.

Name: AgRibot Project
Description: Funded by the European Research Executive Agency (REA), under GA 101183158, AgRibot is a project focused on advancing robotics and AR/XR in agriculture. Its key aims include improving working conditions, reducing the environmental footprint, addressing labor shortages, and boosting sustainability and competitiveness. Aligned with strategic initiatives such as the Farm-to-Fork Strategy, the Common Agricultural Policy post-2022, and the EU's digital ambitions, AgRibot seeks to drive positive change for a fairer, safer, and more resilient agricultural sector.

Name: McMaster Mars Rover Team
Description:
The McMaster Mars Rover Team (MMRT) is a student-run, interdisciplinary organization representing McMaster University in the Canadian International Rover Challenge (CIRC). Each year, we design, build, and test a functional Mars Rover capable of performing a variety of tasks in simulated extraterrestrial environments. 

MMRT consists of approximately 80 members from both engineering and non-engineering disciplines. By applying theoretical knowledge to real-world engineering challenges, our members gain hands-on experience in mechanical design, electrical systems, software development, scientific analyses, and project management.  

Our leadership structure fosters mentorship, collaboration, and professional development, empowering students through teamwork, problem solving, and hands-on challenges, where building a rover is the journey to building future leaders.

The team is currently on a 2 year design cycle with the aim of competing on the international stage at The University Rover Challenge (URC) by Mars Society in Utah, America.

Name: Bay Area Robotics Network
Description: BARN is a community created specifically for robotics founders and leaders in the Bay Area who are actively engaged in the building and growth stages of their companies. Name: POSITEC France
Description:
Fondée en 1994, Positec est un groupe familial de dimension internationale spécialisé dans les outils à batterie pour l’outillage électroportatif et le jardin motorisé. Le Chiffre d’Affaires du groupe est de l’ordre de 1 Md $$. Leader européen des robots de tonte pour jardin avec sa marque WORX en GSB, des innovations de rupture viennent d’être annoncées à sa marque KRESS (réservée aux réseaux professionnels).
La marque Kress a acquis une solide réputation dans l’ingénierie allemande de pointe, grâce à la conception et à la fabrication de moteurs électriques depuis plus de 50 ans. Aujourd’hui, Kress s’engage à assurer un avenir propre au secteur des espaces verts avec un objectif « zéro émission ». Sa technologie de batterie unique permet enfin la transition outil thermique à l’outil sur batterie, en apportant une valeur ajoutée décisive sur tous les plans : environnement, rentabilité et performances.
 Positec France est une des 11 filiales du groupe.

Our mission is to bridge the gap between traditional facilities and the future of autonomous, intelligent workflows—without the need for costly infrastructure modifications.

Conventionally, robots operate on top-down systems, relying on intricate algorithms to execute specific tasks. However, Dax operates on a revolutionary platform, DaxOS ("Dax Operating System"). Name: luhbots
Description:
Wir wurden 2012 als studentisches Robotik-Team der Leibniz Universität Hannover gegründet 

Von 2015 bis 2024 nahmen wir an der RoboCup @Work Liga teil, wo wir 2015 und 2016 Weltmeister wurden 

Seit 2019 arbeiten wir auch daran, in der RoboCup Soccer Small Size League (SSL) anzutreten, was wir 2022 erfolgreich geschafft haben: Wir konnten in Bangkok den dritten Platz erreichen! Im Jahr darauf erreichten wir in Bordeaux sogar den zweiten Platz. Im Jahr 2024 haben wir uns entschieden, in die höhere Division innerhalb der Liga zu wechseln, um unsere Roboter weiter zu verbessern. So war der RoboCup in Eindhoven voller neuer Erkenntnisse und Erfahrungen! Wir haben definitiv vor, auch in den nächsten Jahren weiter teilzunehmen. Nächster Halt (nach der Entwicklung unserer zweiten Robotergeneration): Südkorea im Jahr 2026 

Unsere Leidenschaft ist es, praktisches Robotikwissen und Teamfähigkeiten allen interessierten Studierenden zugänglich zu machen, während wir gemeinsam als Team lernen und wachsen

Name: Tiburon Subsea, Inc
Description:
At TSI, we use proprietary technology to increase the efficiency and reliability of subsea data gathering. That’s how we’re building the new Data Infrastructure Platform for the Blue Economy. Conventional subsea data collection methods are slow, scarce, expensive, unreliable, and unable to meet the rapidly increasing demand for accurate subsea intelligence. At TSI, our mission is to build a platform that overcomes these barriers.

The inability to access subsea survey equipment undermines efforts to monitor and understand our ocean’s ecosystems in areas like the transition to renewable off. Conventional subsea data collection methods are slow, scarce, expensive, unreliable, and unable to meet the rapidly increasing demand for accurate subsea intelligence. Our mission is to build a platform that overcomes these barriers. The inability to access subsea survey equipment undermines efforts to monitor and understand our ocean’s ecosystems in areas like the transition to renewable offshore energy, coastal fishery protection, climate change remediation, underwater methane leakage, and national security.

That’s why we’re building and deploying a global fleet of AI-empowered HAUVs equipped with swarm technology and the latest in subsea survey payloads. Our fully vectored HAUVs represent a massive leap forward in shallow water and low-logistics capabilities. Not only will these vehicles provide operators with cost and time efficiencies resulting from their increased maneuverability,  speed, and multi-vehicle swarm approach, but they will also offer a degree of reliability and redundancy never seen before in the subsea space on account of our robust vehicle design and fleet structure. Our HAUVs will feature built-in payloads of High-Resolution MBES, MAS or HF Dual Frequency SSS, Current-Profiling, Magnetometer, HD Camera, HD Video, CTD, and Scattering Fluorescence Sensors, in addition to incorporating custom client payloads and optional Sub-Bottom Profilers.

Name: MOTUS Robotics
Description:
MOTUS Robotics develops robotic-based O&M applications, with a special focus on improving mobile robotic systems through innovations in AI-driven perception, cognition, and decision-making. 

Its primary mission is to deploy autonomous solutions for the inspection and maintenance of critical infrastructures, especially in challenging environments:

1)  Aerial Robotics (UAVs): drones equipped with AI and sensors for inspecting high-altitude structures like wind turbines and powerlines.

2) Surface Robotics (USVs): autonomous surface vehicles designed to monitor and maintain assets such as floating platforms or coastal installations.

3) Underwater Robotics (AUVs and ROVs): robots for subsea operations like inspecting pipelines, anchors, and mooring lines.

The lab specializes in machine learning and computer vision techniques and develops mobile robots capable of functioning in harsh conditions, including extreme weather, turbulent seas, and complex industrial setups.

Name: Gausium Österreich
Description:
Gausium (Gaussian Robotics Holdings Limited) wurde 2013 in Shanghai gegründet und gilt als Pionier der autonomen Reinigung. Das Technologieunternehmen entwickelt und produziert hochwertige, industrielle Roboterlösungen mit branchenführender Navigation, cloudbasierter Verwaltung und Wartung für alle Anwendungsbereiche der gewerblichen Bodenreinigung. 
Gausium-Roboter verfügen dank hochentwickelter Sensorik und ausgeklügelten Algorithmen der Navigationssoftware über die Fähigkeit selbstständig zu lokalisieren sowie zu kartieren und das auch in dynamischer Umgebung. Innerhalb der Branche gilt Gausium als Technologieführer. Bereits 2017 präsentierte der chinesische Marktführer den ersten autonom arbeitenden Reinigungsroboter – mittlerweile bietet Gausium in mehr als sechzig Ländern der Welt als einziger Anbieter eine ganze Produkt-Palette an KI-gestützten Boden-Reinigungsrobotern an. 

Seit 2023 werden Gausium-Roboter über den neuen Geschäftsbereich der Denzel-Gruppe – Denzel Robotics – exklusiv nach Österreich importiert und über ein autorisiertes Partnernetz vertrieben. Gausium setzt damit auf die bewährten Qualitäten des kompetenten Denzel Robotics-Fachteams, das im Bereich Service-Robotik in Österreich neue Maßstäbe setzt.

Name: NEOSYAD
Description:
NEOSYAD develops products for the use of adipose tissue in plastic and reconstructive surgery (lipofilling), as well as in regenerative medicine.

Our range of patented AdiMate/Adipure devices is revolutionizing the practice of lipofilling for plastic surgery, by automating the preparation of adipose tissue while optimizing graft effectiveness. 
 
Lipofilling is a surgical process in which adipose tissue (fat) is transferred from one area of the body to another, on the same person and in the same operating time (autologous grafting).
This technique is very common and generally necessary for breast reconstruction after breast cancer, as it enables "natural" reconstruction with highly satisfactory results. 
It is also frequently used in cosmetic surgery for gluteal augmentation (buttocks), as well as for breast augmentation, with or without implants. In these indications alone, lipofilling accounts for over 1.5 million procedures per year worldwide. 

NEOSYAD's products are based on the scientific expertise of its founders and the experience of its teams in developing innovative medical devices. 

NEOSYAD's automated systems incorporate software and protocols specifically developed for surgical applications, based on the most recent scientific work on autografts and adipose tissue biology, as well as on several years of NEOSYAD's own R&D.

Surgery ; Adipose tissue ; Cellular biology ; Tissue engineering ; Regenerative medicine

Description:
RICE (Robots and Intelligent Systems for Citizens and the Environment) is a laboratory of the Department of Computer Science, Bioengineering, and Systems Engineering (DIBRIS) at the University of Genoa, founded in 2022.

RICE aims to develop intelligent robotic solutions closely aligned with the needs of society. Its objective is not only to create new technologies centered around humans and their needs but also to disseminate the findings of researchers exploring these aspects worldwide.

In this perspective, RICE focuses on two main areas of research: the development of “intelligent” humanoid social robots to assist and aid people, and the advancement of terrestrial and aerial autonomous robots for environmental monitoring and search and rescue operations after catastrophic events.

In the awareness that RICE, despite its humble nature, is the main food for human beings on Earth.

Description:
Founded in early 2025, CosmicBrain AI is building the data translation and intelligence layer for robotics. We remove the biggest bottleneck in real-world robotics adoption by transforming messy, unstructured robot data into deployable intelligence.

As robotics hardware matures, progress is now limited by software and data. Enterprises and robot manufacturers collect massive volumes of video and sensor data but lack the infrastructure to convert it into model-ready datasets.

CosmicBrain’s AI-driven platform translates raw robotic data at scale and powers a hardware-agnostic marketplace for skill-optimized Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models—enabling intelligence to be deployed across any robot without changing existing hardware.

CosmicBrain turns robots into intelligent systems through software.

Name: Rose City Robotics
Description:
Rose City Robotics is a machine vision lab building software-first inspection systems for high-mix manufacturing.

Our core product is RoseVision CAD Studio — a CAD/STEP-driven workflow that “cold starts” defect detection models from design files, so teams can stand up inspection logic without waiting for a large labeled defect dataset. Built for on-prem deployment and strict data/security policies.

We also work on creating high-fidelity virtual worlds for training physical AI systems (synthetic environments and simulation pipelines that let teams validate perception + inspection logic before full-scale deployment).

What we’re building:
* CAD/STEP → inspection logic for cosmetic + functional defects
* Inspection workflows that stay stable across variants + engineering revisions
* Practical deployment: on-prem, factory-friendly integration (cameras, edge compute, pass/fail outputs)
* High-fidelity simulation/synthetic data pipelines to train and validate physical AI systems

Name: byte robotics GmbH
Description:
Automatic Generation of Optimal Paths

Robots are among the most flexible machine tools available today for industrial use. While conventional machine tools are usually highly specialised structures for individual application scenarios, industrial robots (with the right tool) can be used for an extremely wide range of application scenarios.

Focus
However, path generation during programming of industrial robot applications consumes a lot of time and the quality of the paths depends significantly on the experience of the offline programmer. The focus of byte robotics software "AuRora" is therefore the decoupling of path quality and individual skills by:

*    Automatic sequencing of workflows
*    Automatic path generation of collision-free infeed movements
*    Control-based multi-criteria optimization
*    Integration of our research results into industry-relevant offline programming


German/ Deutsch:

Automatische Generierung optimaler Pfade
Roboter gehören zu den flexibelsten Werkzeugmaschinen, die heutzutage für den industriellen Einsatz zur Verfügung stehen. Während herkömmliche Werkzeugmaschinen in der Regel hochspezialisierte Konstruktionen für einzelne Anwendungsszenarien darstellen, sind Industrieroboter für eine äußerst große Bandbreite an Einsatzszenarien nutzbar.

Fokus
Die Pfaderzeugung während der Programmierung von industriellen Roboteranwendungen verschlingt jedoch viel Zeit und die Qualität der Pfade hängt maßgeblich von der Erfahrung des Offline-Programmierers ab. Schwerpunkt von byte robotics ist die Software "Aurora" mit der Entkopplung von Pfadqualität und individuellen Fähigkeiten Einzelner durch:

*Automatische Sequenzierung von Arbeitsabläufen
*Automatische Pfadgenerierung von kollisionsfreien Zustellbewegungen
*Automatische Ausnutzung von Werkzeugfreiheitsgeraden
*Integration in bestehende Offline-Programmierungen

Un drone ou un robot sont des vecteurs parfaits pour des applications telles que les prises de vues aériennes, la cartographie, le contrôle dimensionnel, la surveillance industrielle, les missions de sécurité ou de sûreté.

Name: Computational Robotics Lab - ETH Zürich
Description: We develop theoretical foundations to shape the way future generations of robots are made, how they operate in complex environments, and how they interact with us. In this quest, we define Computational Robotics as the fusion of simulation, algorithms and data. In brief, we formalize advanced simulation models to provide robots with an innate understanding of the laws of physics. Leveraging these models, we devise practical algorithms for motion planning, motion control and computational design problems. Whenever possible, we exploit data to efficiently learn solution spaces, to create faithful digital twins through real-to-sim methodologies, and to enable humans to teach robots new skills.

Name: Telekinesis
Description:
Telekinesis is building Europe’s leading Physical AI cloud platform, giving any robot—industrial, mobile, humanoid, quadruped, or drone—the ability to perceive, reason, and act with human-like intelligence.

Unlike traditional automation, Telekinesis empowers non-experts on the factory floor to deploy and teach robots in minutes—without writing a single line of code. Focused on automotive, aerospace, and metal manufacturing, the platform enables automation of tasks still performed manually today, particularly in end-of-line manufacturing such as assembly validation, labeling, cable harnessing, laser engraving, and final quality inspection.

At the heart of the system is a universal AI brain that adapts to any robot and task, delivering unmatched flexibility and rapid deployment. This is made possible by breakthrough Physical AI technologies such as Visual Robot Programming, where robots learn directly from video demonstrations; LLM Plan, which transforms natural language prompts into executable behavior; and AutoPlan, which autonomously generates motion plans for complex tasks. Together, these innovations turn robotics into an intuitive tool that anyone can use.

Telekinesis is a spin-off from the Intelligent Autonomous Systems Lab at TU Darmstadt, led by Prof. Jan Peters, a pioneer in robotics and machine learning. Backed by leading investors and industry veterans—including the former MDs of KUKA and Universal Robots (UR/WE)—Telekinesis is transforming two decades of academic innovation into real-world industrial impact.

We’re making robots usable—by anyone.

Name: Rainier Labs
Description:
At Rainier Labs, we're reimagining robotics to transform how the world works, learns, and lives. Our journey begins with Terrier, a versatile, all-American robot dog designed to adapt to a wide range of applications. Designed, engineered, and built in California, Terrier is powered by cutting-edge technology—a robust RGBD camera paired with the NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano processor—making it the ideal companion for those pushing the boundaries of innovation.

Whether you’re a researcher exploring robotics, an engineer performing factory inspections, or a construction manager creating 3D models of your sites, Terrier is equipped to excel. Its dynamic adaptability and precision engineering also make it an invaluable tool for patrolling as a vigilant security guard, ensuring safety, or functioning as a smart home assistant, ready to make everyday tasks easier.

But Terrier is only the beginning. At Rainier Labs, we envision a future where robots seamlessly integrate into our lives, handling repetitive, dangerous, or mundane tasks so humans can focus on creativity and innovation. From education and research labs to manufacturing, construction, and elder care, our robots are designed to solve real-world challenges with intelligence and agility. Our products embody the perfect synergy of advanced AI, meticulous design, and the relentless pursuit of innovation.

Beyond Terrier, we’re developing platforms that combine natural language interaction, autonomous navigation, and manipulation capabilities to redefine what’s possible in robotics. Our mission is to democratize cutting-edge robotics, offering tools and solutions that are accessible, impactful, and transformative.

Join us on this exciting journey as we push the boundaries of robotics, creating intelligent machines that work alongside humanity to build a smarter, more efficient, and more connected future.

Name: HandsOn Robotics
Description: The operation of commercial kitchens presents our customers with enormous problems when hiring staff. Today, finding a sufficient workforce for manual tasks such as loading and unloading dishwashing lines, portioning, or serving is challenging. The labor shortage and ever-increasing costs make the automation of manual processes unavoidable. We are developing lean automation solutions that take over manual tasks in the commercial kitchen back-end that are way smaller and faster than everything available. We have strong customer interest and first, paid contracts. Name: The Hybrid Intelligence Centre
Description:
Hybrid Intelligence (HI) is the combination of human and machine intelligence, expanding human intellect instead of replacing it. HI takes human expertise and intentionality into account when making meaningful decisions and perform appropriate actions, together with ethical, legal and societal values. Our goal is to design Hybrid Intelligent systems, an approach to Artificial Intelligence that puts humans at the centre, changing the course of the ongoing AI revolution.

By providing intelligent artificial collaborators that interact with people we strengthen our human capacity for learning, reasoning, decision making and problem solving. This interaction has the potential to amplify both human and machine intelligence by combining their complementary strengths. Hybrid Intelligence requires meaningful interaction between artificial intelligent agents and humans to negotiate and align goals, intentions and implications of actions. Developing HI needs fundamentally new solutions to core research problems in AI: current AI technology surpasses humans in many pattern recognition and machine learning tasks, it falls short on general world knowledge, common sense reasoning, and human capabilities such as collaboration,  adaptivity, responsibility and explainability (CARE).

The Hybrid Intelligence Centre is a collaboration of top AI researchers from the VU Amsterdam, the University of Amsterdam, the TU Delft, and the Universities of Groningen, Leiden, and Utrecht, in areas such as machine learning, knowledge representation, natural language understanding & generation, information retrieval, multi-agent systems,  psychology, multimodal interaction, social robotics, AI & law and ethics of technology. The HI centre will create a national and international focus point for research on all aspects of Hybrid Intelligent systems.

Name: FORSocialRobots
Description:
Unsere Vision im Bereich der sozialen Robotik besteht darin, dass Mensch und Roboter gemeinsam in flexiblen Teams in allen Bereichen des täglichen Lebens zielführend zusammenarbeiten und neue Roboterapplikationen in kürzester Zeit realisiert werden können. Das Projekt FORSocialRobots soll daher in sechs relevanten Anwendungsfeldern robotischer Applikationen – Überwachung (A1), Logistik (A2), Produktion (A3), Service (A4), Seniorenheim (A5) und Demenzzentrum (A6) –  und in fünf wissenschaftlichen Teilprojekten – Architektur sozialer Fähigkeiten (TP1), sozial situative Kommunikation (TP2), sozial adaptive und proaktive Interaktion (TP3), Simulation und Validierung sozial kognitiver Roboter im Digitalen Zwilling (TP4) und Mensch-Roboter-Interaktion im Arbeitskontext (TP5) – einen entscheidenden Beitrag zur Verbesserung der sozialen Fähigkeiten von Robotern leisten, damit deren Wirksamkeit und Akzeptanz bei den potenziellen Nutzern steigern sowie völlig neue technische Entwicklungen unterstützen.

Das Projektvorhaben ist auf drei Jahre angelegt. Das Projektkonsortium besteht aus Forschungseinrichtungen, Einrichtungen der angewandten Forschung, Unternehmen und Anwendern, welche gemeinsam sowohl das Know-how besitzen, Roboter physisch und softwareseitig zu erforschen als auch geeignete Anwendungsfälle exemplarisch umzusetzen und dabei ethische, rechtliche und soziale Implikationen (ELSI) zu beachten. 

Beteiligte Forschungsinstitute:
- Lehrstuhl für Fertigungsautomatisierung und Produktionssystematik (FAPS) der Friedrich-Alexander-Universität Erlangen-Nürnberg (FAU)
- Lehrstuhl für Psychologie im Arbeitsleben (PiA) der FAU
- Fraunhofer IIS
- Fraunhofer IGCV

Our vision is to create a network of robotics learning platforms across the country, fostering innovation among the next generation of Malawian leaders. We collaborate with schools, universities, NGOs, and the government to provide access to robotics resources, training, and mentorship. Our mission is to inspire and empower young people to pursue STEM careers, contributing to a more technologically proficient and innovative Malawi.

Name: Event-Driven Perception for Robotics - IIT
Description:
We exploit the event-driven sensing and neuromorphic processing to endow robots with improved energetic and behavioral autonomy
Bio-inspiration to exploit neural computational primitives implemented on neuromorphic hardware is our focal point. We also explore engineered methodologies for event-driven processing with hard real-time constraints.

Name: Gausium Österreich
Description:
Gausium (Gaussian Robotics Holdings Limited) wurde 2013 in Shanghai gegründet und gilt als Pionier der autonomen Reinigung. Das Technologieunternehmen entwickelt und produziert hochwertige, industrielle Roboterlösungen mit branchenführender Navigation, cloudbasierter Verwaltung und Wartung für alle Anwendungsbereiche der gewerblichen Bodenreinigung. 
Gausium-Roboter verfügen dank hochentwickelter Sensorik und ausgeklügelten Algorithmen der Navigationssoftware über die Fähigkeit selbstständig zu lokalisieren sowie zu kartieren und das auch in dynamischer Umgebung. Innerhalb der Branche gilt Gausium als Technologieführer. Bereits 2017 präsentierte der chinesische Marktführer den ersten autonom arbeitenden Reinigungsroboter – mittlerweile bietet Gausium in mehr als sechzig Ländern der Welt als einziger Anbieter eine ganze Produkt-Palette an KI-gestützten Boden-Reinigungsrobotern an. 

Seit 2023 werden Gausium-Roboter über den neuen Geschäftsbereich der Denzel-Gruppe – Denzel Robotics – exklusiv nach Österreich importiert und über ein autorisiertes Partnernetz vertrieben. Gausium setzt damit auf die bewährten Qualitäten des kompetenten Denzel Robotics-Fachteams, das im Bereich Service-Robotik in Österreich neue Maßstäbe setzt.

Name: Strutt
Description:
At Strutt, we believe that technology should serve real people, and solve real problems. We’re world-leading experts in smart products - advanced sensing, autonomous driving, and hardware design, but we’re the sort who question everything. We found ourselves wondering why we would dedicate our lives and talent to a distant future, or even a shallow present.

That’s why we started Strutt - to use technology to solve problems that matter, not in the future but now.

Name: Kurtin Robotics Company

Founded in 2019, the company focuses on designing, programming, and integrating robotic systems for businesses, with a mission to bring jobs back to America through innovative automation. Their services include creating functional robot cells, offering user-friendly solutions, and providing hands-on support to ensure the success of their projects. The company values integrity, honesty, and loyalty, standing behind their products and guaranteeing implementation that meets or exceeds expectations.

Welcome to TechnoRobotics!
Our robots team up with you to raise safety, quality, and efficiency.

The robotic revolution is upon us, and TechnoRobotics LTD from Israel is at the forefront of this technological shift. Our state-of-the-art “All-in-One” solutions for intelligent manufacturing bring together advanced equipment such as collaborative robots, smart technologies, and customised services, enabling businesses to uplift their processing margins while adhering to higher social and eco-friendly standards.

We are excited to share with you what sets us apart — our people.
Our story began in 2018 when the group of scientists and entrepreneurs joined their efforts to develop a practical alternative to conventional large — scale automation.
From day one, we have prioritized engaging top talent who have unique skillsets, experiences, and multicultural backgrounds. This commitment has enabled us to grow beyond our initial focus on technology to TeamRobotics solutions accessible to small to medium-size enterprises.

Name: Millibar Robotics
Description:
Millibar continuously develops innovative solutions to solve robotic and material handling challenges.  With the release of its Manual Tool Changers for Collaborative Robots, Millibar extends the versatility of an already versatile technology by making robot redeployment extremely fast and easy for any operator. As collaborative robots are employed further up the production line, they are being tasked with handling an expanding array of product shapes and surfaces. To meet these challenges, Millibar has developed off-the-shelf components for controlling vacuum, pneumatic and adaptive grippers that provide a turnkey solution for non-expert robot users.  Our material handling expertise eliminates end-of-arm tooling guesswork and helps companies deploy their robots with minimal engineering effort.

Combining its line of tool changers and collaborative robot accessories with its extensive line of venturi vacuum pumps, suction cups and foam grippers, Millibar is uniquely positioned to be your one-stop source for robotic, factory automation, material handling and packaging solutions.

Now, we are bringing this expertise to Brazil. With cutting-edge technology and a professional approach, we help growers boost productivity, reduce costs, and achieve new levels of efficiency in the field.

Name: imrox
Description:
imrox – Ihr spezialisierter Komplettanbieter für Robotersysteme in Kliniken und Pflegeeinrichtungen
Unser Fokus ist die Automatisierung der Transportprozesse in Kliniken durch bewährte Roboter namhafter Hersteller. 
-Wir haben die beste Robotertechnik und Software
-Wir installieren die Infrastruktur im Gebäude und kümmern uns um die IT-Systeme
-Wir nehmen die Menschen beim Integrationsprozess mit durch Mitgestalten, Schulen und professionellem Support
-Wir schauen uns die Prozesse sehr genau an und sorgen dafür, dass diese dann perfekt funktionieren

imrox ist der erste Komplettanbieter, der sich auf nicht-medizinische Prozesse in Kliniken und Pflegeeinrichtungen konzentriert. Unsere innovativen Robotersysteme optimieren die kritische Ver- und Entsorgung von Wäsche, Speisen, Verbrauchsmaterialien, Medikamenten, Laborproben und mehr – bis hin zur "Last Mile Delivery". Zusätzlich bieten wir Assistenz- und Serviceroboter, die den Betrieb auf Station und in der Pflege maßgeblich unterstützen.

Von der Planung über die Integration in bestehende Gebäudeinfrastrukturen bis hin zur Betriebsunterstützung – wir liefern betriebsbereite und bewährte Systeme inklusive aller erforderlichen Dienstleistungen. Auch bei der Automatisierung Ihrer Prozesse stehen wir Ihnen aktiv zur Seite.

Als Systempartner mit umfassendem Know-how strukturieren und verantworten wir Projekte gewerkeübergreifend und ganzheitlich. Dank unserer Kooperationen mit führenden Roboterherstellern können wir für jede Anforderung die passende Lösung bieten. Unser Portfolio wird durch ergänzende Systeme, Geräte und Softwarelösungen abgerundet, um Ihre Arbeitsabläufe effizienter zu gestalten.

Name: JARVIS
Description: JARVIS, part of the Horizon Europe HORIZON-CL4-2023-DIGITAL-EMERGING-01-0 initiative, is a 4-year project dedicated to crafting a versatile toolkit for AI-driven multimodal interaction. We are a consortium of 16 partners collaborating from various European countries to develop a reusable set of tools that enable AI-driven multimodal means of interaction. Our focus includes interfaces for both physical and remote information exchange, robot control and programming as well as the integration of social skills into a diverse array of robots. JARVIS aims to facilitate seamless user-centric interactions, extending human capabilities for intricate tasks. The project will not only showcase the scalability of its applications but also demonstrate the ability to achieve economies at scale.

Name: AlterEcho (Techstars '25)
Description:
AlterEcho develops remote embodiment robotics enabling life science professionals to perform aseptic work from outside cleanrooms or hazardous zones. Using VR headsets and motion controllers, operators control robot arms in sealed chambers with full dexterity and 3D vision - safely distant from contamination and biohazards.

Unlike laboratory hoods, glove boxes and isolators exposing humans and limiting throughput, AlterEcho eliminates human presence in critical processes, achieving true Class A (EU-GMP Annex 1) sterility while maintaining flexibility for complex tasks. Our platform integrates robotics with AI assistance, supporting delicate procedures with complete control and traceability.

For laboratory hoods, AlterEcho offers plug-and-play upgrades to remote operation. In pharmaceutical manufacturing, we replace glove-based isolators with robotics chambers keeping staff outside sterile zones. Our vision: compact robotics containers piloted by skilled professionals and enhanced by adaptive AI.

This defines life sciences' future: relying on human mind, not body. Physically demanding tasks become precision robot piloting - eliminating fatigue and injury, making roles accessible to a more diverse workforce as markets evolve.

AlterEcho serves pharmaceutical manufacturers in aseptic processing including injectables and Class A cleanroom workflows. Our technology benefits specialty labs in radiopharma, infectious disease, and vaccine R&D where biohazard handling is critical. In clinical settings, our system enables remote sterile compounding and ATMP handling, even during outbreaks or staffing shortages.

AlterEcho delivers zero contamination risk, fewer rejected batches, smaller space requirements, staffing flexibility, and a path to intelligent autonomisation. We transform cleanrooms from bottlenecks into remotely operated, AI-enhanced environments for these critical industries.

Name: Active & Interactive Robotics Lab
Description:
We conduct research on human-robot interaction (HRI) from a multidisciplinary perspective, adopting methodologies from robot control, machine learning, and human factors.

We are interested in understanding how humans interact with robots, and how to improve robot control with respect to diverse interaction scenarios. We analyze the interaction from physical aspects (e.g. maintaining physical safety and user ergonomics) and social aspects (e.g. perception, acceptance, ethics). In particular, we are interested in using these aspects within the control of the robots so that they can perform their tasks actively and efficiently, while being socially acceptable.

Our ultimate goal is to have robots seamlessly and intelligently interact with people in diverse environments including but not limited to manufacturing, home (e.g. cooking, cleaning), and long-term care institutions. The robots we utilize consist mainly of collaborative robot arms, wheeled mobile robots, and humanoid robots.

Name: Centre of Excellence on Biologically Inspired Robots & Drones | Indian Institute of Technology Delhi
Description:
About Us
The Center of Excellence on Biologically Inspired Robots and Drones (BIRD) at the Indian Institute of Technology Delhi (IIT Delhi) is a multidisciplinary initiative focused on advancing robotics and aerial systems inspired by nature.

BIRD aims to explore and engineer intelligent, adaptive, and efficient robotic platforms by drawing inspiration from biological systems — from insects and birds to human motion. Our research spans across areas such as soft robotics, swarm intelligence, bio-mimetic drones, flapping-wing flight, autonomous navigation, and environmental interaction.

We bring together researchers, students, industry partners, and policy thinkers to develop cutting-edge technologies with real-world impact — ranging from defense and agriculture to healthcare and disaster response.

Our Focus Areas
 Biologically Inspired Locomotion
 Aerial Robotics and Drones
 Soft Robotics and Grippers
 Swarm Robotics and Coordination
 Vision, Control, and Autonomy
 Human-Robot and Environment Interaction

Who We Are
BIRD is led by faculty and researchers at IIT Delhi, supported by a vibrant community of collaborators from academia, industry, and government. We welcome partnerships, student interest, and innovation-driven collaborations.

Connect with us to learn more, collaborate, or get involved.

Name: Analog Physics Inc.
Description:
Analog Physics Inc.’s QAINET delivers cognitive autonomy to robots and swarms across air, land, sea and space (2026).

Today’s UAS programs are fragmented across proprietary stacks, slowing integration while hardware and additive manufacturing race ahead. QAINET bridges this gap with a deterministic, explainable OS that self-organizes drone and robotic swarms through cloud or edge deployment—delivering real-time speed, agility, and control.

Accessible via an AWS-like marketplace, QAINET enables rapid deployment, interoperability, and sustainment across the U.S. military, allies, and industry partners. 

Beyond defense, QAINET delivers endless applications in infrastructure and industry — transforming exponential complexity into linear scalability across all domains of autonomy.

Breakthrough: Recognized as a Top Quantum Computing Startup for Quantum Error ‘Prevention’ (QEP), Analog Physics’ Quantum Subsumption Architecture overcomes the robotics barrier of combinatorial explosion, enabling 1,000+ concurrent swarm behaviors in real-time with sub-millisecond decisioning.

QAINET operationalizes quantum at the edge—a hybrid quantum-cloud OS powered by QEP and Vulkan GPUs. Its deterministic, decentralized decisioning and explainable logic create trustworthy, self-organizing swarms that require no training. Inspired by nature’s collective systems, QAINET embodies “That Ant Died” resilience—maintaining mission continuity without retraining or central control.

QAINET—for defense, public safety, infrastructure, smart cities, agriculture, and industry—accessible on AWS Marketplace in 2026.

Name: Gobotty
Description:
Moeite om je logistieke processen efficiënt te laten verlopen? Worden repetitieve taken nog steeds handmatig uitgevoerd ondanks het tekort aan personeel? Wist je dat de aanschaf van een heftruck duurder is dan de inzet van een AMR robot?

Ontdek hoe robotisering met Gobotty veel voorkomende problemen binnen de logistiek en productie oplost. Bij Gobotty zijn we ervan overtuigd dat robotisering niet slechts een product is; het is een innovatieve dienst, die bedrijven vooruit brengt door hen te voorzien van op maat gemaakte robotiseringsoplossingen op basis van een abonnement.

Een Gobotty is te personaliseren op basis van jouw logistieke vraagstuk. Denk hierbij aan toepassingen met rollenbanen, transportbanden, smartmovers, karakuri en klantspecifieke oplossingen.

Of het nu gaat om het transport van pallets, dozen, kratten en stukgoederen, de aanvoer van karton bij inpakstraten, de afvoer van overtollig materiaal en reststromen, of het aan- en afvoeren van tooling en bewerkte producten – voor elk logistiek proces is een module beschikbaar.

Het unieke aspect van Gobotty is dat bedrijven, een complete oplossing huren in plaats van alleen een robot aan te schaffen. Deze aanpak wordt gepresenteerd als “Robotisering as a service”.

Daarnaast zijn onze robots modulair, flexibel en geschikt voor verschillende toepassingen, waardoor ze passend zijn voor elke fase van een organisatie. Gobotty is meer dan alleen een product; het is een innovatieve dienst die bedrijven vooruit helpt door op maat gemaakte robotiseringsoplossingen te bieden.

Our Technology

ORB – A compact, tactical drone with passive stabilization for agile manoeuvring, low energy use, and live acoustic imaging.

 Defense & Security – Maritime ISR, mine detection, naval monitoring.

Name: Farm Robotics Challenge
Description:
The Farm Robotics Challenge is the nation’s premier collegiate competition advancing robotics and artificial intelligence for agriculture. Powered by UCANR Innovate and the AI Institute for Next Generation Food Systems (AIFS), our annual event empowers student teams from across the country to tackle real, pressing problems facing growers — from autonomous navigation and precision mapping to harvesting, pest control, and more.

Each team partners with a grower, confronts an on-farm challenge, and designs an innovative solution using robotics and AI. Projects span the full agricultural cycle, focusing on technical excellence, design creativity, system complexity, societal benefit, and commercial viability.

Why compete? The Farm Robotics Challenge is not just a contest — it’s a launchpad for future leaders in ag tech. Gain hands-on experience, expand professional networks, and showcase your achievements to leading experts and employers. Finalists compete for significant prizes and earn travel stipends to attend the FIRA USA ag tech conference in California, opening doors to new connections and opportunities.

Whether you’re a student innovator, educator, startup, or sponsor, join us to help shape the future of sustainable agriculture. Registration for the 2026 competition is open. Let’s transform today’s prototypes into tomorrow’s market-ready solutions — and build a smarter, healthier food system together.

Name: AIRA - Artificial Intelligence. Real Abilities
Description:
AIRA puts Robots to Work. We train Physical AI models on task specific data and deploy mobile, general-purpose Robots in real world manufacturing settings, supervising remotely, and supporting on site. We aim to solve the productivity challenge working directly with manufacturing customers, one task at a time.

Physical AI is not just about humanoids, it can be any AI system equipped with sensors and actuators: the sensors allow it to observe the world, and the actuators allow it to interact with and modify the world, covering several robotic form factors. It holds the promise of freeing human workers from physical tasks that are dangerous, laborious, or tedious. While several fields of AI have advanced significantly thanks to data and compute scaling in the recent decade, Physical AI only inches forward due to real world data scarcity and several other barriers. 

Unlike digital AI, where pre-training data can be scraped from the internet, Physical AI requires data from dynamic, real-world environments. This makes clean, deployment-specific, information-rich data the most important aspect of Physical AI training. AIRA’s mission is to accelerate the adoption of Robots in productive environments through a data-rich and scalable Physical AI Training Framework, granting robots Real Abilities and deploying them in real-world applications.

Name: Artificial Intelligence, Biomechatronics, and Collaborative Robotics
Description:
Biomechatronics is a multidisciplinary field that combines principles of biology and mechatronics (electrical, electronics, and mechanical engineering). It also encompasses the fields of robotics and neuroscience. This discipline aims at designing and controlling advanced robotic systems that interact with the human body. This field includes a wide range of applications, including the design and control of wearable and implantable devices, prosthetics, exoskeletons, and assistive robots.

Collaborative Robotics is a branch of Biomechatronics that explores the interaction and collaboration between humans and robots. This includes the design of robotic systems that can work alongside humans in shared environments, as well as the study of human-robot interaction and the development of new control strategies to enable effective collaboration. The evolution of the field may be seen as a gradual approach to human-robot interaction, human-robot cooperation, and human-robot teaming. Human-robot interaction is the study of how humans and robots interact, as well as how to develop robots that can adapt to human behavior. Human-robot cooperation expands on this by creating new approaches and technologies that allow robots to collaborate with people in shared environments. The field of human-robot teaming goes one step further, by studying how to create teams of humans and robots that can work together effectively and efficiently to achieve common goals.

This research group focuses on the intersection of Biomechatronics and Collaborative Robotics. The objective is to develop advanced technologies that enhance human capabilities and enable more efficient and effective collaboration between humans and robots. Haptics, Virtual Reality (VR), Augmented Reality (AR), Mixed Reality (MR), and Extended Reality (ER) technologies are integrated to create immersive and interactive experiences for human-robot collaboration to enhance human capabilities.

Nosso objetivo principal é capacitar e qualificar engenheiros, tecnólogos, técnicos e entusiastas para se destacarem em um campo de negócios em rápido crescimento.

Este projeto envolve diversas áreas de conhecimento, tais como:

Inteligência Artificial (IA): Desenvolvendo algoritmos avançados de aprendizado de máquina e visão computacional para permitir a autonomia dos veículos.

Engenharia de Veículos: Explorando a integração de sensores, sistemas de controle e hardware específico para veículos autônomos.

Tecnologia de Sensores: Utilizando uma variedade de sensores, como câmeras, lidar, radares e ultrassom, para permitir a percepção ambiental dos veículos.

Software de Controle: Desenvolvendo software de controle sofisticado que permite que os veículos tomem decisões seguras e eficazes em tempo real.

Junte-se a nós nesta jornada de aprendizado, inovação e colaboração, à medida que trabalhamos juntos para moldar o futuro da condução autônoma e desenvolver as habilidades necessárias para prosperar nesse emocionante campo em constante evolução.

Name: Innovationsprojekt pd|z ETH Zürich
Description: The innovation project is a compulsory course in the third semester of the bachelor’s program in Mechanical Engineering at ETH Zurich. During the project, students work in small groups to develop an autonomous robot designed to solve a specific challenge. The task changes from year to year but always presents students with challenging technical problems to solve. The aim of the innovation project is to give students hands-on experience with agile product development methodology. In the final week of the semester, students present the systems they have built and pit them against each other in the public finale in the main building of ETH Zurich.

Name: Olivabot
Description:
Olivabot is building bio-inspired robots to automate olive harvesting in traditional groves.

Millions of olive trees across Southern Europe are grown on steep, rocky, and irregular terrain — making them incompatible with current mechanized harvesters. As labor shortages worsen, more farmers are forced to abandon or under-harvest their groves, threatening both yields and the cultural heritage of traditional olive oil production.

We’re developing terrain-adaptive, AI-powered harvesting robots that can navigate these complex environments — climbing, detecting, and picking olives with precision. Designed for small-to-medium farms, our robots reduce labor needs while preserving the quality and sustainability of hand-picked harvests.

We operate on a Robot-as-a-Service (RaaS) model: farmers pay per harvest, with no upfront cost. Our pricing aligns with farmer success, making advanced automation accessible without risky investment.

Why now?
Advances in robotics, computer vision, and lightweight design make it possible to bring automation into places that machines have never gone before. And the urgency is real — traditional groves are being left behind in the global shift to ag-tech. We're here to change that.

Backed by the Amsterdam Academic Angel Fund and supported by Demonstrator Lab (Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam), we are currently focused on R&D and early field validation across Italy, Greece, Spain, and Portugal.

We’re not building robots for factories. We’re building them for the hills.

The team had a humble beginning with only a handful of student body forming the student body. Today the team boasts a network of 30+ alumni spread across different continents along with more than 35+ active members engaged within the different modules and domains of the team.

We move beyond proof-of-concepts: our approach is pilot → operationalize → scale, with turnkey POC services, integration, training, and ongoing support to ensure measurable business outcomes.

What we deliver - 
•	Faster workflows and reduced manual load - automating repetitive delivery, reception, and cleaning tasks.
•	Consistent service levels and improved safety with autonomous navigation and collision avoidance.
•	Measurable ROI: reduced operating costs, improved throughput, and higher customer satisfaction.
•	Flexible deployment models - from single-site pilots to multi-site fleet rollouts.

How we work - 
We collaborate with operations leaders to define KPIs, implement pilot programs, and model the ROI for each deployment. Our team handles hardware, software integration, on-site commissioning, and lifecycle support so organizations can scale robotics with confidence.
Mission: To empower human workers by deploying intelligent robots that handle repetitive, dangerous, or time-consuming tasks, freeing people to focus on meaningful, creative, and strategic work.

Name: Sudriabotik
Description: L'association Sudriabotik de l'ESME Sudria (loi 1901) à vu le jour en janvier 2001, remplaçant le club robotique créé en 1996. Son but est simple, permettre à des élèves dynamiques et motivés de notre école d'ingénieur de se réunir et de réaliser un robot pour participer à la Coupe de France de Robotique. Chaque année, tous les membres utilisent leur énergie et leur temps libre pour concevoir et réaliser ce projet : chacun partage son savoir avec les autres et le travail effectué permet de mettre en pratique les cours vus à l'école mais aussi de mieux comprendre notre futur métier d'ingénieur et même de découvrir des domaines hors de nos formations. L'association comporte cinq départements : mécanique, électronique, informatique, communication et stratégie. Chaque chef de département répartit le travail entre les différents membres de l'équipe selon les motivations et les compétences. Le but est d'être le plus efficace possible : la réalisation de notre robot s'effectue en effet en parallèle avec nos études et les disponibilités de chacun varient.

Name: Robotique CIV
Description:
Le club de robotique du CIV à Sophia est composé de 4 équipes. L'une participe au FIRST Robotics Competition (FRC) et les trois autres participent au FIRST Tech Challenge (FTC). 

Les différents évènements organisés par FIRST ont pour objectif d’inciter les jeunes à s'engager dans les sciences, la technologie, l'ingénierie et les maths (STEM) pour inspirer et former la prochaine génération d'innovateurs et de leaders. Les équipes gagnent toujours en expérience car chaque nouvelle saison présente un nouveau défi, de nouvelles contraintes. Ainsi, nos nombreuses participations au FTC nous ont fourni un savoir-faire et les connaissances nécessaires à la création d'une équipe FRC en 2023. 

Outre la mécanique et l'informatique, nous assurons une communication active sur les réseaux  sociaux et avec les entreprises afin de promouvoir notre activité. Nos partenaires nous accompagnent sur de nombreux aspects: ils nous apportent leurs connaissances, leurs employés nous aident en mécanique et programmation (initiative d’ARM), et nous fournissent en matériel.

Pour nous, la participation à ces compétitions est également un moyen de diffuser nos valeurs. Nous souhaitons promouvoir la parité dans les milieux ingénieurs et le monde de l'entreprise. De par son installation dans un lycée international, le club berce dans un écosystème multiculturel. Les nombreuses compétitions internationales auxquelles nous avons participé ont enrichi cet aspect. En somme, la robotique va de pair avec la curiosité: curiosité à l’égard du club, de l’ingénierie, de l’autre, et de l’avenir.

Toutes ces valeurs forment  l'esprit FIRST: l’entraide et la coopération, les vecteurs de notre réussite.

Nous remercions chaleureusement notre donateur ARM, et nos partenaires FIRST, SAP, Community Foundation Ireland, la ville de Valbonne Sophia Antipolis, et nos mentors pour le soutien qu’il nous apporte.

Pour nous contacter : robotique.civ@gmail.com

Name: Zabidou
Description:
Industrial feed lines in mining, food production, and recycling require more than simple object identification. They need a comprehensive, real-time understanding of material properties. When subtle differences in composition, texture, or density determine a product's fate—whether accepted, rejected, or rerouted—the stakes are enormous. This multi-billion dollar global industry sees direct benefits from deeper material insights: significant process savings, higher yields, reduced waste, and meaningful circular economy wins.

Yet conventional robotic vision systems lack this crucial sensitivity. They can see, but cannot feel. In these industries, feel is often everything.

"Zabidou has developed a novel laser sensing system that generates over 100,000 points per second—each one imprinted with the feeling of touch on every pixel in the environment." - Dr Cibby Pulikkaseril

This breakthrough enables unprecedented precision. Machines can now distinguish between visually similar materials that behave differently, advancing smarter, leaner, and more sustainable operations.

Comprising over 30 passionate students, our mission is to become world-class engineers by designing, building, and programming advanced robots and intelligent systems. Each year, we represent DTU at top national and international competitions such as the World Robot Contest (China), CANSAT (USA), ABU Robocon, Technoxian, and Smart India Hackathon.
We tackle real-world engineering challenges in robotics, AI, and automation-ranging from autonomous navigation and sensor fusion to AI-driven control and precision actuation. Our hands-on projects and competition experience help us develop solutions that advance technology and create social impact.

Name: Robotic Summit
Description:
Veranstaltung für Anwendungsbezogene medizinische Robotik mit dem Ziel, Wissen zu vermitteln und die Vernetzung von Medizin und Industrie zu fördern.

Zielsetzung der Veranstaltung:
1. Vernetzung: Plattform für den branchenübergreifenden Austausch zwischen Medizin und Industrie.
2. Wissensvermittlung: Aufklärung über Innovationen durch Fachvorträge, Panels und Live-Demos.
3. Neuheiten: Präsentation der neuesten Entwicklungen in der medizinischen Robotik.
4. Langfristige Vision: Etablierung als führendes Forum für Medical Robotics, alle zwei Jahre mit Branchenführern und Visionären.

Der Robotic Summit 2025 (RS25) bringt Expertinnen und Experten aus Medizin und Industrie zusammen, um die Potenziale der anwendungsbezogenen medizinischen Robotik zu präsentieren und zu vernetzen.
Ursprünglich als kleines Symposium des Brüderkrankenhauses Paderborn gestartet, entwickelt sich der PRS zu einer führenden Plattform, die den Austausch über Innovationen und Einsatzmöglichkeiten robotischer Systeme in der Medizin fördert.
Der Hauptfokus der Veranstaltung liegt auf der Aufklärung über Innovationen in der medizinischen Robotik und deren praktischen Nutzen für Patienten sowie für die Effizienz und Wirtschaftlichkeit von Krankenhäusern. Ergänzend werden auch Lösungen für die Pflege und angrenzende Bereiche vorgestellt, um ein ganzheitliches Bild moderner Robotik im Gesundheitswesen zu bieten.

Datacept operates across diverse industries with complex logistics needs – from indoor-heavy sectors like food & beverage and manufacturing, to rugged outdoor domains like building materials, chemicals, and automotive.

Name: Sestosenso
Description: Sestosenso is a European project that develops technologies for next generations of collaborative robots capable of self-adapting to different time-varying operational conditions and capable of safe and smooth adaptation from autonomous to interactive when human intervention is required either for collaboration or training/teaching. The project proposes a new sensing technology from the hardware and up to the cognitive perception and control levels, based on networks of embedded proximity and tactile sensors on the robot body, providing a unified proxy-tactile perception of the environment, required to control the robot actions and interactions, safely and autonomously. Within the project, the same technologies are also applied to wearable devices (like exoskeletons) to provide the user with better spatial awareness and to enforce safety in critical human-robot interactive tasks.

Name: IUB BongoMarine
Description:
Independent University Bangladesh (IUB) BongoMarine is a visionary and collaborative team that strives to develop underwater robotics and technology. Our mission is to unlock the potential of autonomous systems in marine environments by pushing the boundaries of ocean exploration. Composed of IUB students who share a passion for AUV (Automated Underwater Vehicles) technology, our team collaborates to generate innovative ideas, develop advanced technologies, and lead the charge in marine innovation.

BongoMarine consists of four specialized sub-teams, each focusing on different aspects of the project. The Fabrication Team is responsible for building and assembling the physical components, ensuring that designs are accurately transformed into real-world products. The AI & Machine Learning Team develops intelligent algorithms and models that enhance decision-making and automation within the system. The Electronics and Hardware Team designs and implements the electronic circuits and systems that power the AUV. The PR and Design Team oversees visual branding and communication strategies, creating engaging materials to showcase BongoMarine's innovations across social media and other channels. Each team plays an integral role in driving the project forward and ensuring its long-term success.

In 2024, the IUB BongoMarine Alpha, the first-generation AUV team made a significant mark by reaching the finals of the prestigious Singapore Autonomous Underwater Vehicle Challenge (SAUVC), competing against 72 teams from around the world. 

We aspire to make underwater technology more accessible to people, share knowledge, and inspire a new generation of innovators in this field right here in Bangladesh. Let’s grow together into new territory, where we can unlock inspiring possibilities in ocean engineering.

As IUB BongoMarine continues to advance, we encourage you to follow us and stay connected on this journey!

Name: Elecsyor
Description:
Elecsyor conçoit et développe une nouvelle génération de moteurs électrostatiques fibrillaires destinés à la robotique portable, aux dispositifs médicaux et aux textiles techniques.
Issus de plusieurs années de recherche à l’EPFL, ces moteurs sous forme de fibres reproduisent le comportement d’un muscle naturel : souples, légers, silencieux et intégrables dans des structures textiles.

La technologie d’Elecsyor repose sur une architecture coaxiale innovante exploitant les forces électrostatiques pour produire un mouvement linéaire continu et réversible. Cette approche combine performance mécanique, faible consommation énergétique et compatibilité avec les environnements flexibles.

Basée à Lyon, Elecsyor a pour mission de rendre la robotique plus humaine et plus accessible, en développant des muscles artificiels modulaires capables de s’adapter à une large gamme d’applications : exosquelettes, dispositifs de rééducation, interfaces haptiques ou robotique souple.

#DeepTech #SoftRobotics #ArtificialMuscles #ElectrostaticActuation #WearableRobotics #MedTech

Name: HOPR Capstone 2025-26
Description: The Hopping Observation Platform for Reconnaissance (HOPR) is a lunar jumping robot developed to investigate permanently shadowed regions and collect imagery of potential ice deposits near the Moon’s poles. Lunar ice represents a vital in-situ resource for future missions, offering potential sources of water, oxygen, and propellant. HOPR’s primary objective is to capture high-resolution images and terrain data to support future exploration and resource utilization HOPR utilizes a compact, spring-powered hopping mechanism that enables mobility across uneven terrain in low gravity. Its lightweight structure, efficient power system, and elastic energy storage system allow it to operate effectively within hazardous environments traditional rovers cannot reach. By combining a jumping mobility approach with an on-board data collection system, HOPR demonstrates a practical approach to reconnaissance in extreme conditions. The platform serves as a proof of concept for small, agile robotic explorers capable of enhancing lunar surface studies and supporting the development of sustainable exploration infrastructure.

Relevant Research problems include “Cyber-physical systems,” “Cooperative and distributed control of multi-agent systems,” “Testing, evaluation, analysis, control, and decision making for large-scale complex systems,” “Robotic swarms,” “Reliable and fault-tolerant hybrid control systems,” “Distributed optimization and decision making for coordination of multi-agent systems,” “Human-Machine Interactions,” and “Hybrid modeling and control.” We particularly focus on the applications of the developed technologies to different domains, including "Smart Transportation", “Smart Agriculture,” “Teaming of Autonomous Vehicles,” “Smart Grids,” “Space Exploration,” “Security and Reliability of Complex Systems,” “Aerial Robotics,” and “Flight Control Systems.”

Name: GrapeHawk
Description:
GrapeHawk développe des solutions de robotique aérienne et de télédétection pour accélérer la transformation digitale de l’agriculture et de la foresterie. Nous combinons drones (VTOL et multirotors), capteurs avancés (RGB, multispectral, SWIR, LiDAR et radar/DInSAR selon les missions) et IA embarquée (edge AI) pour produire des informations directement actionnables sur le terrain.
Notre approche fusionne données drones, satellites et capteurs IoT afin de suivre finement le stress hydrique, la vigueur, la variabilité intra-parcellaire et certains risques agronomiques, puis de générer des recommandations opérationnelles : priorisation des zones à inspecter, optimisation des passages, cartographies décisionnelles, alertes et rapports. Nous proposons des prestations de cartographie, de diagnostic et d’aide à la décision, ainsi que des solutions intégrées (plateforme + capteurs + opérations) adaptées aux exploitations, coopératives et acteurs institutionnels.
En complément, GrapeHawk opère des missions de pulvérisation de précision (traitements ciblés) sur la base des cartes et seuils agronomiques, afin de passer de l’analyse à l’intervention, avec traçabilité des opérations. Nous concevons également des systèmes drone-capteurs orientés performance, avec une forte exigence sur la sécurité, l’autonomie et la qualité de données. Présente en France et au Brésil, l’équipe réunit ingénierie, IA, traitement d’images et opérations pour déployer des solutions fiables, reproductibles et industrialisables.

Name: SpinDrone
Description:
The mission is to create the worlds most efficient VTOL aircraft. By simulation and iterative
prototyping, the solution was found: An airplane that transforms it‘s wings
into one big rotor for efficient and lightweight take-off and landing.

1. Real live experiments proved a ~40% efficiency-gain against multi-rotor
concepts. Looking at jet-theory it’s no surprise: twice the rotor radius -->
half the power demand.

2. Every part of the aircraft is used 100% all the time. Therefore weight is
saved, which increases payload capacity.

3. Free choice of engine technology: Due to the constant RPM it is possible to use combustion engines for large scale & long endurance applications.

Description:
En Tecno Robotic IA ayudamos a que los robots funcionen en la vida real. Somos integradores de robótica: seleccionamos a los mejores fabricantes internacionales, configuramos sus soluciones, las adaptamos a las necesidades del cliente y ofrecemos soporte local en España y la UE.

Nos enfocamos en tres mercados prioritarios:

Agricultura: robots de deshierbe autónomo (láser, mecánico, eléctrico), pulverización selectiva con visión, asistencia de cosecha (UGV de acarreo), drones para datos y tratamientos.

Logística y almacenamiento: AMR para picking/cross-dock, sistemas Goods-to-Person (AutoStore, Exotec), soluciones específicas para la cadena de frío.

Seguridad e inspección: robots cuadrúpedos integrados con sistemas de videovigilancia y PA, inspección industrial en entornos críticos.

 Lo que ofrecemos:

Integración llave en mano (robot + software + soporte).

Pilotos con métricas claras de ROI (<24 meses).

Servicio técnico en España con repuestos críticos en 48–72h.

Cumplimiento de normativa europea: CE, ISO, GDPR, AESA/EASA.

 Creemos en la automatización con propósito: reducir costes, mejorar la productividad y hacer más sostenibles los procesos agrícolas, logísticos e industriales.

Name: Syntheva Robotics
Description:
At Syntheva we're building the most affordable yet functional humanoid robots for home use.
The common understanding of a humanoid robot makes people believe that it's an extremely complex and extremely expensive piece of technology. While the "extremely complex" part is quite accurate, the "extremely expensive" part does not have to be true.
We design our robots from the ground up with the scalability and manufacturability in mind, meaning that our robots are far cheaper to manufacture than any competitive solution.
We think that in the near future anyone should be able to buy their home companion robot on a budget, just as anyone can buy a laptop or a smartphone.

Name: RoboCI
Description:
RoboCI is a next-generation DevOps platform purpose-built for robotics teams. We bridge the gap between software engineering best practices and the unique challenges of robotics development, making it faster, safer, and more scalable to build, test, and deploy robots.

Our AI-powered platform automatically generates CI/CD pipelines for ROS-based projects, runs simulations in the cloud, and connects seamlessly to real hardware for hardware-in-the-loop testing. By reducing setup time, eliminating broken builds, and ensuring consistent deployments, we enable robotics developers to focus on innovation instead of infrastructure headaches.

Products & Services

RoboCI Platform – AI-assisted ROS CI/CD generator with support for ROS 2, Gazebo, Webots, Jetson, and Raspberry Pi.

Cloud Simulation – Run automated simulations without local setup.

Hardware Deployment – Streamlined cross-compilation and deployment to popular robotics hardware.

Custom DevOps Solutions – Tailored CI/CD workflows for hardware vendors and robotics enterprises.

At RoboCI, we believe robotics innovation should be about building the future, not fighting your build system.

We believe in nurturing entrepreneurship in robotics. Our platform provides opportunities to explore techno-entrepreneurship, fostering innovation and the development of robotics products. 

As a founding member of the Asian Robotics Society Union (ARSU) www.asian-robotics.org and the World Robot Corporation Organization (WRCO), our mission is to foster global collaboration in robotics driving innovation and development for society.

Through education, training, and community outreach, we aim to inspire interest in robotics among the younger generation. 

Join us at RSS as we work towards our vision of being the premium platform for promoting friendship and robotics in Singapore and beyond. Together, let’s nurture practice, education, training, innovation, and techno-entrepreneurship among individuals with passion for robotics.
