Name: Robotics Industry Monthly
Description:
Robotics Industry Monthly is a global B2B e-news platform delivering the latest news, innovations, insights, and emerging trends in industrial robotics, intelligent automation, AI and machine learning, and autonomous systems—including robotic arms, AGVs, AMRs, mobile platforms, cobots, vision-guided robotics, humanoids, and commercial drones. 

Our editorial coverage spans R&D, advanced control systems, systems integration, engineering, production, and global distribution—with a strong focus on robotics for smart manufacturing, intelligent warehousing, precision assembly, materials handling, and autonomous inspection. We also cover safety-certified solutions (e.g., ISO 10218, ANSI/RIA) that drive efficiency, reliability, and human-machine collaboration. We serve a range of verticals, including automotive, aerospace, electronics, logistics, and advanced materials.

Published by Components Source, powered by Embassy Global, LLC — the world’s first and only 100% woman-founded, owned, and led publishing and consultancy network (WBE/WOSB/DOBE certified) serving the high-tech manufacturing and engineering sectors — Robotics Industry Monthly connects a global network of industry peers through expert-driven content designed to educate, inform, and empower. 

As part of a growing portfolio of 11 specialized e-news sites, Robotics Industry Monthly contributes to a living data ecosystem that informs key industry stakeholders, public and private sector leaders, and academia. 

Together, our publications advance awareness, advocacy, and sustainable innovation across the global supply chain.

Our sister publications include:

•	Industrial Automation Monthly

•	Electronics Industry Monthly
•	Semiconductor Industry Monthly

Visit our site today to join the conversation.

Description:
We are a team from the sons of the Higher Technological Institute in the Tenth of Ramadan - Faculty of Engineering, Mechatronics Department.
After seeing the Egyptian market and observing it in terms of large industrial exhibitions and events, we noticed the extent of the Egyptian market's need for industrial robots.
We decided, after research and scrutiny, to work on establishing a company to design, manufacture and operate all kinds of industrial robots that the Egyptian market lacks.
Last November, we manufactured our first robot, which is an arm
It performs an automatic welding process for metals.
This robot has many uses, including car production lines, military factories, metal industries, air tanks, and others.
At this stage, we are developing the robot, as it is in the stage of developing the operating system to become the best performance, and then it will be ready for testing
Inside the Egyptian factories.
Our first and last goal is to renaissance the Egyptian industry.
We are very keen on that so that our vision is compatible with Egypt's Industrial Vision 2030.

Rooted in collaboration and curiosity, our club thrives on the belief that technology, especially Robotics, is best learned by doing.

We actively work on Combat Robotics including Robo Rovers, Robo Sumo, Robo Soccer, and Robo Wars, as well as a variety of impactful technical projects. Throughout the academic year, we organize workshops, events, and competitions designed to enrich the hands-on learning experience of students across CBIT.

Our club provides essential mentorship, access to tools, components, and workspace, and encourages knowledge sharing among all members - whether you're a beginner or an experienced enthusiast. We meet regularly to discuss ongoing projects, manage resources, and plan upcoming events and innovations.

There are no prerequisites to join only a willingness to learn, contribute, and grow. At the Robotics and Innovation Club, we don’t just build robots we build the future.

By collecting real-time data and providing actionable insights, Rostek's OEE services empower manufacturers to identify bottlenecks, reduce downtime, enhance productivity, and maximize their operational efficiency.

Furthermore, Rostek specializes in digital transformation services tailored specifically for manufacturers. With a strong track record of delivering high-quality robotics and digital transformation solutions, Rostek has established itself as a trusted partner for manufacturers seeking to enhance their operational capabilities, increase productivity, and stay ahead in today's competitive market.

Name: Stride Robotics
Description: Stride Robotics began with a simple question: why have powered prosthetics failed to reach the people who need them most? Existing devices are too heavy, short-lived, and limited—leaving amputees with decades-old passive solutions. Drawing on nearly a decade of research in artificial muscle and high-performance robotics, we set out to change that. Today, our team is building the first powered prosthetic knee lighter than the limb it replaces—restoring strength, endurance, and true mobility to people everywhere.

Name: SmartDrones
Description:
SmartDrones builds full-stack autonomous inspection systems for critical infrastructure.

We design and develop an end-to-end Automatic Inspection System (AIS) that combines autonomous drones, proprietary autopilot firmware, simulation-based validation (deterministic SIL/HIL), AI-powered analysis, and dedicated web apps for inspection workflows and reporting. Our systems are engineered for safety-critical environments, where repeatability, determinism, and data integrity matter more than “just flying a drone”.

Business model: Drone-as-a-Service + SaaS. Clients can rent drones/pilots via SmartDrones, get access to aisCloud, and receive AI-generated PDF reports. Missions are planned in the cloud — the pilot’s role is simplified to launching a pre-validated mission (often one-click start), while AIS handles the end-to-end process: planning → execution → data validation → AI analysis → auditable reports.

Use cases: wind turbine blades, solar PV farms, telecom towers, and guarded facilities.

Name: SoftEnable
Description:
"Towards Soft Fixture-Based Manipulation Primitives Enabling Safe Robotic Manipulation in Hazardous Healthcare and Food Handling Applications" 

SoftEnable is a Horizon Europe project which proposes a new framework for robotic manipulation actions involving deformable delicate objects. It is based on the notion of soft fixture-enabled manipulation primitives. 

The project considers two primary use case applications: to aid workers in healthcare applications with procedures involving personal protective equipment and to provide assistance by means of collaborative robotics in the food processing industry

Name: WASP Research Arena Robotics
Description:
The Wallenberg Research Arena for Robotics.

WARA Robotics targets the industrial challenges of the future that are currently performed by human operators. To increase the level of autonomy, the expected outcomes in the next years are twofold. 
- On one hand, the goal is to increase the capabilities of the robots to carry out complex mobile manipulation tasks based on sensor inputs and AI to replan motion commands. 
- On the other hand, the vision is for the robots to share their environment with humans and collaborate with them, which brings challenges in the human-robot interaction. 

It is expected that the research that WARA Robotics generate will lead to an increased productivity by integrating robots in industrial applications that are currently performed fully manually due to the complexity of the interactions (e.g., fast food, retail, lab automation). 

WARA Robotics will provide hardware (collaborative manipulators and mobile manipulators, industrial manipulators), software (interfaces to the robots and the digital twin), support from the staff members and industrial partners. 

Being hosted at ABB, the WARA Robotics brings together expertise from the industrial partners in sharing both the know-how and the challenges in current industrial applications. 

The physical lab is currently accessible and available to the students which would then be able to access to the network of students, PIs, and industrial partners that are part of the WARA network. 

WARA Robotics can uniquely help fulfilling the WASP vision to create competence and research in artificial intelligence and autonomous systems for the benefit of Swedish society and the segments of industry mentioned above.

Name: Noitom Robotics
Description:
Company Introduction
Noitom Robotics (NR) is an innovative leader in the rapidly evolving field of embodied AI and humanoid robotics. The company is dedicated to overcoming the critical data bottlenecks limiting robotic intelligence, especially the challenging cross-embodiment transition. NR’s mission is to empower the robotics industry by delivering comprehensive and scalable data solutions, accelerating the development and deployment of intelligent robots across diverse environments.

Technical Highlights
NR specializes in state-of-the-art motion capture technology, providing high-precision, low-latency multimodal human data acquisition systems. The company's robust solutions include sophisticated body-mapping workflows, immersive teleoperation environments, digital twins, and advanced multimodal dataset generation, management, and governance processes. These systems are meticulously designed to seamlessly integrate with industry-standard platforms such as NVIDIA Isaac, Mojoco, ROS, and other widely-used frameworks, significantly streamlining the complex training pipeline critical for robotic autonomy.

Value to Industry
NR enables robotics companies and researchers to rapidly establish large-scale data factories for embodied intelligence, significantly improving the quality, quantity, and practical relevance of training datasets. By systematically addressing key challenges in human-to-robot mapping and simulation fidelity, NR dramatically reduces time-to-market for robotic innovations. Ultimately, Noitom Robotics empowers businesses to scale efficiently, enhance robot performance and reliability, and effectively bridge the gap between virtual simulations and real-world robotic deployment.

Name: Northsence
Description: Imagine a day that you sit at your home or office and stand present at another place on the other side of the globe, freely walking around, talking to the people, and observing whatever you want to observe. We took this as our vision “To enable professionals' presence anywhere in the world, at any time". Our mission starts with the construction industry since 3 out of 10 people at a construction project have to be physically at the site, but their daily tasks are limited to observation and communication. Also, construction is still a local industry, has a high injury rate and access to the global talent pool is not yet convenient. But most importantly, all the daily travels are costly for the budget and planet. By a web-operated robot, we are enabling remote and safer work of the organization roles at a construction project. This solution saves $47 Billion every year, globally, in the construction industry.

Name: IDEAS Lab
Description:
Welcome to the interdisciplinary research lab, IDEAS (Intelligent Design for Empathetic and Augmented Systems) at Purdue Computer Science. Our research includes the design and implementation of scientific algorithms leveraging the “human in the loop” and their applications to robotics, computer graphics, AI, virtual environments, augmented intelligence, pedestrian and crowd dynamics, and medical / healthcare research.

Our research also focuses on building embodied computational models of human behaviors, and developing component algorithms of an intelligent agent (from sensing, to decision-making, to actuating). Our long-term research goal is to create engaging, socially intelligent agents that can interact with humans in innovative ways through expressive multi-modal interaction.

Some recent work includes combining these methods with machine learning, computer vision, and physically-based modeling for Multi-Agent Dynamics, Heterogenous Robotics, Autonomous Driving, Affective Computing and Virtual Reality. In addition to publishing papers at the leading venues, we have a long history of developing software packages and transitioning our technology into industrial products.

Our approach combines:

Name: The Biohybrid and Organic Robotics Group
Description: Animals have long served as an inspiration for robotics. However, many of the mechanical properties, physical capabilities, and the behavioral flexibility seen in animals have yet to be achieved in robotic platforms. Towards addressing this gap, research in the CMU B.O.R.G. focuses on the use of organic materials as structures, actuators, sensors, and controllers towards the development of biohybrid and organic robots. The research group’s long-term goal is to develop completely organic, autonomous robots with programmable neural circuits. Such robotic systems will have future applications in medicine, search and rescue, and environmental monitoring.

Name: RobotSkull
Description:
RobotSkull is a company based in Vadodara, India that specializes in providing robotics parts, automation parts, and artificial intelligence boards to customers. The company was founded in 2015 with the aim of becoming a leading supplier of high-quality robotic and automation components.

Robotics parts refer to components that are used in the construction and assembly of robots. These include motors, sensors, actuators, and controllers, among others. Automation parts, on the other hand, are components that are used to automate processes and operations in various industries, such as manufacturing, agriculture, and healthcare. These can include conveyor belts, robotic arms, and programmable logic controllers (PLCs), among others.

AI boards are specialized circuit boards that are designed to perform AI computations. These boards are used in various applications, such as image recognition, natural language processing, and autonomous navigation.

Overall, Robotskull is a company that specializes in the production and distribution of high-quality robotics parts, automation parts, and AI boards. With its focus on innovation and cutting-edge technology, Robotskull is poised to play a leading role in the rapidly evolving field of robotics and automation.

Name: RASTIC
Description:
RASTIC is the Boston University engine for applied robotics innovation that benefits society.

It is our hub for education, skilled workforce development, entrepreneurial pursuit, industry partnership, and research translation.

Our leadership and impact are felt not just here at BU but around the New England region and across the Robotics and Autonomy sector at large.

RASTIC allows students to expand and hone their robotics skills, so they are ready to make an immediate impact in their classes and in the workplace. RASTIC is a robotics hub, providing an innovation space, a thriving community, and a forum for intimate tech talks, industry days, career fairs, research exchange, and networking, among companies, students, faculty, investors, and other industry stakeholders.

In RASTIC, students can design, build, and test all kinds of robots, from simple consumer ‘bots to GPU-fueled, AI-powered cutting-edge marvels. Aerial and ground-based robots can navigate dynamic landscapes, assisted by Hollywood-grade motion-capture technology. Students can custom-mold silicon to create the flexible robots that are rapidly emerging in medicine, Industry 4.0, and beyond.

Name: Triton Droids
Description: Triton Droids is a student-led organization focused on developing the first affordable, autonomous humanoid robot that can eventually help eliminate global poverty. Access to capital, technology, and cheap labor causes epic disparities in production efficiencies across regions of the world. This is the reason why a farmer in Australia earns 20 times more than a farmer in India or Africa with the same amount of land or why many American companies outsource production. By leveraging collaboration with UCSD faculty, UCSD’s unique engineering labs,industry partnerships, and the interdisciplinary innovative spirit of our student body, we aim to make cutting-edge humanoid technology accessible to all. Our mission is to create a world of abundance and drive equitable economic growth through innovation, while providing our members with unparalleled hands-on experience and opportunities to shape the future of robotics and global development.

We specialize in robotics and systems engineering to build platforms that deliver high-quality, human-centered services in an era of population decline. By focusing on labor-intensive service sectors such as hotel room cleaning and front-desk operations, we aim to automate complex, detail-oriented tasks through scalable, AI-powered robots.

Our robots are designed to understand and replicate human social norms, enabling them to perform tasks such as over 300 hotel inspection items with precision and consistency. Through the fusion of data, automation, and cultural sensitivity, we strive to redefine service industries and preserve meaningful traditions.

Omotenashi Robotics exists to deliver kindness through technology—and to build a future where hospitality is always within reach.

Name: IAS-19
Description:
The 19th international conference on Intelligent Autonomous Systems (IAS) will be held in Genoa, Italy, from 30 June to 4 July 2025. Since 1985, IAS conferences have been a benchmark for intelligent and autonomous robots, offering a unique opportunity to share research, ideas and discussions on the impact and potential of intelligent and autonomous systems.

This edition's motto, ‘Ethical, Responsible, and Inclusive Robotics’, reflects the conference's commitment to promoting the development of robot science and technology based on ethical, responsible, and inclusive principles, creating a more equitable future for all.

IAS will bring together researchers, practitioners and companies to explore the latest innovations in Robotics, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning and Cognitive Systems, offering a unique opportunity to discuss emerging challenges and new opportunities.

We invite all researchers and practitioners to attend and present their work, helping to make this edition a memorable moment of innovation and collaboration.

Our solutions:

BoBee – a cutting edge robotic solution capable of non-destructive smartphone dismantling. It enables efficient recovery of valuable components while reducing manual labor and maximizing repair potential, all in support of a more sustainable, circular economy.

Ayrıca yenilikçi, dünyayı tanıyan, yeteneklerine ve kendine güvenen insan kaynağı yetişmesine katkıda bulunmak da temel gaye ve hedefimizdir.
Snark isimli otonom tünel veya mağara içi haritalama ve görüntüleme robotu projemizle başlayacağımız yolculuğumuz, diğer otonom robotik teknolojiler ile devam edecektir.

robot soccer matches.

The team is tasked with the formation of students through hands-on practice on the programming of humanoid robots, modeling the soccer team as a multi-agent
system, dealing with complex problems in the field of Robotics and Artificial Intelligence, usually ending up in the realization of a Master Thesis project in the RoboCup
SPL setting.

SPQR Team has been involved in RoboCup
competitions since 1998 in different leagues and is currently taking part yearly in the SPL league tournament.

The team also took part in several instances of the Maker Faire - Rome Edition and Maker Faire - European Edition with a dedicated exposition space featuring a regulamentary RoboCup SPL field with live matches,
organizing tournaments with other teams from abroad.

SPQR team members published a total of 22 papers in RoboCup Symposia, in addition to other RoboCup-related
publications in International Journals and Conferences in AI and Robotics.

Our team is made up of students from multiple disciplines that are passionate about utilizing robotics to solve global problems in innovative ways.

Our flagship product EMBR (Ember Mitigation Bot Responder) is an autonomous robotic system designed to address the critical challenge of managing and extinguishing residual embers post-wildfire. EMBR leverages advanced sensing technologies and AI-driven analysis to navigate through affected terrains, accurately detect hidden hotspots, and efficiently execute extinguishing protocols. This innovative approach not only enhances firefighting efforts but also significantly reduces the risk of re-ignition, contributing to safer, more effective wildfire recovery operations.

Why we exist:
Physical AI is still in its infancy. Our mission is to power its emergence by making it so accessible that anyone can build and deploy it—removing six-figure infrastructure costs and months of bespoke engineering.

Who we serve:
• Established robotics and automation companies expanding or modernizing their fleets
• New entrants exploring AI-driven products without prohibitive upfront spend
• R&D teams needing a reproducible path from prototype to global rollout

Understand robot capabilities beyond specifications

Robot in 3 Days is based around the international high school FIRST Robotics Competition (FRC). There are currently over 80,000 students participating in FRC robotics each year from over 26 countries. FRC teams are given an annual robotics challenge to complete in 2 months. 

The purpose of our club is to inspire and inform high school teams by doing it in only 3 days! We host a 72-hour build event every January where we document the design, build, and testing processes. During and after the event, we share our robot to help teams learn from our experience.

Name: REMARO ITN
Description: REMARO attacks one of the most pressing problems of modern computing, the safety of AI, in the well-defined context of submarine robotics. The REMARO research fellows will develop the first-ever submarine robotics AI methods with quantified reliability, correctness specifications, models, tests, and analysis & verification methods. REMARO rests on two founding principles: (i) The submarine robot autonomy requires a comprehensive hybrid deliberative architecture, a robotic brain. (ii) Safety and reliability must be co-designed simultaneously with cognition, not separately, as an afterthought. This project has received funding from the European Union’s Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme under the Marie Skłodowska-Curie grant agreement No 956200.

They are used in various industries such as manufacturing, healthcare, agriculture, space exploration, and more recently, in everyday environments like homes and offices. RGT inc. is a full-stack robotics company behind the autonomous service robot brand SIRBOT. We specialize in AI-driven navigation, in-house software development, and integrated cloud-based control systems built to scale in real-world environments. Our lineup includes serving robots, cooking robots, and autonomous cart robots all built in-house to ensure performance consistency and system-level efficiency.

Our core competitive edge lies in advanced autonomy. RGT has independently developed key technologies such as transparent object recognition, impact-sensitive collision detection, real-time SLAM-based mapping, and adaptive path planning that responds to dynamic environments. These technologies enable high-precision navigation across complex indoor-outdoor spaces.

More than automation, RGT turns robots into data-driven business platforms. Our robots collect real-time customer movement, engagement, and service flow, which are analyzed to automate in-store marketing, generate operational insights, and support revenue growth strategies. We also develop proprietary integration systems that connect robots with POS, NFC table ordering, digital signage, call-and-alert devices, elevators, and more.

RGT robots are built to scale across diverse industries including retail, F&B, shopping, and logistics. Beyond machines, we engineer a sustainable ecosystem where robotics meets monetizable business value.

Name: FIELDBOT
Description:
ACCELERATING THE WORLD'S TRANSITION TO SUSTAINABLE ABUNDANT AGRICULTURE

Fieldbot's mission is to create highly profitable and sustainable farming methods that increase productivity, lower costs, mitigate nitrogen leaching and runoff, conserve water and energy, reduce agrochemical usage, eliminate pesticide spray drift, and lessen the environmental impact of row crop agriculture.

Fieldbot is an agriculture robotics company that provides electric power systems and autonomous farm machinery solutions for the global agriculture equipment market.

Today, there exists a unique opportunity to advance worldwide row crop agriculture using modern Fieldbot PRECISION UPGRADES that easily and cost-effectively retrofit onto conventional large-scale agriculture equipment.

New Fieldbot PRECISION UPGRADES universally add more reliability, capability, precision, and intelligence to any existing  “start-stop move” irrigation pivot.

These smartly repurposed ultra-high-speed "constant move" irrigation pivots, when newly used to more effectively apply costly crop protectants, e.g., pesticides, fungicides, herbicides, as all-electric, autonomous alternatives to conventional petroleum-based application methods such as ground sprayers and aircraft, provide a massive return on investment to the customer resulting from significantly higher crop yields at much lower production costs with no gas or diesel emissions.

“For commercial growers of high-value potato crops, the single greatest ‘pain point’ is the application of crop protectants. As for prescribing the right product-market fit to fulfill this unmet need, Fieldbot PRECISION UPGRADES are those rare must-have painkillers in a vast sea of nice-to-have AgTech vitamins.”

The total addressable market for Fieldbot PRECISION UPGRADES is estimated to be over $20 billion in modernizing the more than 600,000 slow, electromechanically driven “start-stop move” irrigation pivots watering over 100 row crop varieties in 100 countries worldwide.

Name: 知能技術株式会社 Chinougijutsu co. ltd.
Description:
We have been developing the transformation of existing construction machines into automatic work robots.
AI automatically moves construction machinery to perform construction.
Unlike conventional autonomous driving, our system does not require a computer or many sensors on the vehicle side, and AI remotely controls the construction machine while viewing images from cameras installed on the site.
This is just like a child playing with a radio-controlled car, in the same way that a radio-controlled car does not need a computer or sensors.
Therefore, existing construction machines can easily be turned into automated work robots.
AI can automatically do construction work. However, humans need to help AI with complex tasks.
The operator remotely controls the construction machine in collaboration with AI, just like playing a video game from home.
The reason we developed this system is that remote work is now the norm for office workers. However, construction workers must endure heat and cold to work on dangerous sites.
As a result, workers shy away from this work, creating a labor shortage.
We believe that this game-like AI system that can operate actual construction machinery will increase the number of young workers and create employment for families raising children at home and people with disabilities.
This system has already been introduced at civil engineering sites in Japan.

Description:
AIRBT represents the seamless integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) with Robotics Technology (RBT), symbolizing a company at the cutting edge of innovation. The name reflects the following key themes:

Autonomy: The company is focused on creating fully autonomous machines capable of performing tasks without human intervention, driven by AI.
Intelligence: AI provides the brain behind robotic systems, allowing them to think, learn, adapt, and improve over time.

Robust Technology: "RBT" highlights the company’s strength in building robust, reliable, and scalable robotic systems.

Innovative Fusion: The name showcases the fusion of AI and robotics, which can be used in a variety of industries—healthcare, manufacturing, logistics, or even smart home automation.

Business Application:
AIRBT can be positioned as a company specializing in:

Autonomous Robots: Robots that use AI to navigate environments, make decisions, and perform complex tasks—whether in industrial automation, service robotics, or logistics.

Embedded AI Systems: Embedding AI into machines for real-time decision-making and control systems in smart vehicles, drones, or robotic arms.

AI-Driven Process Automation: Offering solutions for streamlining processes across industries like manufacturing, healthcare, or retail by integrating robotic automation with AI-based decision-making algorithms.

Taglines:
"Empowering Robotics with Intelligence"
"Autonomous Innovation for Tomorrow"
"AI at the Core of Robotics"
"Building the Future with Smart Robotics"
"Revolutionizing Robotics through AI"

Future Products/Divisions:
AIRBT Health: Autonomous robots powered by AI for surgery, patient care, or diagnostics.
AIRBT Logistics: AI-driven robotic solutions for warehouses, material handling, and delivery drones.
AIRBT AI: A division solely focused on building advanced AI algorithms for use in robotic systems across industries.

Name: Quantum Quest
Description:
Quantum Quest develops multi-domain autonomous systems for drones operating underwater, on the surface, in the air and on land. Our work aims at making advanced control systems more accessible across all these environments. At the moment, our main focus is on underwater drones and surface autonomy.

Our technology is organized into three layers. MAVRIC is the autonomy operating system responsible for navigation, sensor fusion, mission execution and real-time decision processes. Mission Control Cores (MCCs) are modular hardware units that adapt the system to the requirements of a specific operational environment. Above this sits the application layer, which provides tools for defining missions, monitoring operations and managing fleets. These applications are also tailored to the domain in which a drone operates.

We concentrate on creating a consistent and adaptable architecture rather than on building complete drones. The aim is to provide a foundation that integrators and manufacturers can use across different types of platforms, supporting reliable operation in conditions where traditional control systems may be limited.

Our name is our thesis: a single, versatile body that can polymorph its function through software, mastering an ever-expanding library of skills. Our mission is to unbind human potential from physical constraints, amplifying the capacity of creators, scientists, and caregivers to solve the world's most important challenges. We are building the ultimate toolmaker, so humanity can become the ultimate maker of meaning.

This institute fosters an interdisciplinary approach, bringing together experts from various domains, including mechanical and electrical engineering, data and computer science, and cognitive science, to explore innovative solutions that enhance human capabilities, improve quality of life, and increase industrial efficiency. Through cutting-edge research, the institute focuses on developing intelligent robotic systems and human interfaces that can seamlessly integrate with human users, thereby optimizing human perfromance, rehabilitation processes, and enhancing user experience. By collaborating with industry partners and academic institutions, the institute aims to translate theoretical research into practical applications, ultimately contributing to the development of advanced robotic technologies that address pressing societal challenges. Our current main projects are:
1- Smart Prosthetic Hand
2- Fall Prevention
3-  Cognitive Rehabilitation with Virtual Reality
4- Telepresence with Augmented Reality
5- Cognitive Sensory Enhancement and Substitution
6- Modular and Service Robots

ربوکمپ بزرگترین بوتکمپ رباتیک ایران

"ربوکمپ" با هدف افزایش سطح علمی و فنی رباتیک و هوش مصنوعی در کشور، ارتقا سطح رقابتی مسابقات رباتیک و ایجاد نقشه راه برای دانشجویان و دانش آموزان در زمینه رباتیک در دو گروه سنی دانش آموزی و دانشجویی (بزرگسال) ایجاد شده است.

این بوتکمپ دارای دو سری کارگاه مهارت های پایه و کارگاه های تخصصی می باشد. کارگاههای پایه به مدت ۱۶ ساعت و به صورت پروژه محور برگزار میشوند و شامل مهارتهایی هستند که در حوزه رباتیک، به ویژه در مسابقات، صنعت و بازار کار بسیار کاربردی هستند. کارگاههای تخصصی در ۴ ساعت برگزار شده و شامل کارگاههای مختلف خصوصا در حوزه لیگهای مسابقات ربوکاپ هستند تا مسیر موفقیت تیم ها و مربیانی که قصد فعالیت در این لیگ ها را دارند تسهیل کند.

علاوه بر این، مجموعهای از کارگاههای تخصصی در حوزههای خاصی که کمتر شناخته شدهاند، برگزار میشود و آشنایی با این حوزه ها، سطح علمی و فنی افراد و تیم ها را بهبود میبخشد. کادر اجرایی و آموزشی این رویداد جمعی از شرکت کنندگان و کادر فنی فعلی لیگ های مختلف مسابقات ربوکاپ هستند.

En partenariat avec des fabricants reconnus tels que Service-botics, OrionStar Robotics, Pudu Robotics et Gausium, nous proposons des solutions robotiques adaptées aux contraintes opérationnelles de chaque site.

Nos gammes de robots couvrent plusieurs usages :

• Robots de nettoyage : balayage, aspiration et lavage autonomes pour l’entretien optimisé des espaces intérieurs et industriels.
• Robots de livraison : transport automatisé de charges légères ou lourdes, avec des technologies avancées de navigation et d’évitement d’obstacles.
• Robots d’accueil : interaction vocale, guidage intelligent et diffusion de contenus pour améliorer l’expérience visiteurs et clients.

Nos services incluent :

• Vente et leasing : solutions flexibles intégrant des options de financement et des contrats de maintenance.
• Installation personnalisée : étude préalable, cartographie des lieux, configuration des robots et formation des utilisateurs.
• Maintenance et SAV : maintenance préventive, support à distance et interventions sur site si nécessaire.

Notre mission est de faciliter la transition vers des solutions robotiques opérationnelles, tout en assurant un accompagnement complet et durable afin d’optimiser les performances et l’organisation de nos clients.

Name: phDRIM

To enhance the skills and competencies in Robotics and Intelligent  Machines distributed throughout the country, create fertile synergies  between disciplines, universities, research institutions, and  companies, and consolidate the excellent Italian positioning on the  international scene, a unifying cultural project is necessary.

This cultural project is carried out by the Ph.D. program of national  interest in Robotics and Intelligent Machines (DRIM).

DRIM brings together all the research and training areas aimed at  studying, developing, and constructing innovative robotic systems and intelligent machines. The objective is to form a large community of  students who develop their research themes in a shared context, integrated on a geographic, multidisciplinary, and interdisciplinary scale.

Meeting the tough requirements of the job it´s designed to do also make the Parrotfish USV a great alternative as a mobile power and communication hub on the surface for small ROVs and for carrying other type of payloads such as cameras, sensors, sampling equipment and sonars. It is especially suited to operate in marinas, harbors, archipelago and fjord environments with network coverage from land based cellular antennas.

Description:
Proximity Robotics & Automation GmbH is a German robotics startup that translates cutting-edge research into solutions for real industrial challenges.

By leveraging state-of-the-art foundational models, fine-tuned for specific applications, Proximity Robotics combines modern AI approaches with proven robotics techniques. These include classical control methods, Learning from Demonstration (LfD), and Reinforcement Learning (RL). Together, they enable the development of scalable, intelligent robotic systems for perception, control, and human-robot collaboration in real-world environments.

Name: SimpleAutomation, Inc
Description:
We are building a robot to automate repetitive manual work, starting with electronic assembly.

Our robot is general purpose, but not humanoid.
Two arms within an outer frame is 10x cheaper and simpler than humanoid — yet it retains the benefits of a general-purpose robot: mass scalability, wide range of data, and a massive total market.

Our goal: Deploy 100 million robots in the next 5–10 years.
Our plan:
Step 1: Automate electronics assembly — including our own robot production.
Step 2: Eliminate the need for the U.S. to offshore manufacturing.
Step 3: Tackle the rest of manual labor: food processing, textiles, jewelry, packaging, etc.

Strong early signals:
- Our release went viral at X and Reddit: https://x.com/ihorbeaver/status/1928154351383580800
- We are backed by Hugging Face (leading AI platform) and Naval Ravikant (early investor in Uber & Twitter) at a valuation higher than most post-YC startups.

We're looking for ambitious people who will change how the world works.

We intend to change that by offering a cost-effective, easy, and highly available solution to entice ship owners into more frequent hull cleanings. 

By removing and capturing fouling from surface our ROV offers an invasive species prevention and remediation solution.

We aim to push the boundaries of underwater vehicle design with cutting edge technology and a work ethic rivalling professionals.

SAUVC 2020 & 2025 – Qualified to compete at the Singapore AUV Challenge.

Teknofest 2024, Türkiye – Achieved the highest Concept Design Report (CDR) score among Indian teams with 78 points, showcasing our strong design and technical vision.

Name: McMaster RoboSub
Description: McMaster RoboSub Team is a technical team at McMaster University focused on designing, building, and testing an Autonomous Underwater Vehicle (AUV). This AUV will compete in the annual RoboSub competition hosted by RoboNation. The competition consists of challenging teams to build an AUV capable of completing complex underwater tasks which include but are not limited to, obstacle navigation, torpedo deployment, and sample collection. The McMaster RoboSub team provides students with a hands-on application of interdisciplinary engineering fields, including Electrical, Mechanical, and Software Engineering. The RoboSub team is actively seeking financial support, guidance, and sponsorships to help fund the team. The financial support will be quintessential to funding the development of the AUV and help to cover competition-related expenses. Guidance will help to ensure that our designs are correct and effective. The sponsorships will aid in gaining development and testing facilities. A partnership with our club would provide sponsors with visibility among a highly motivated and technically skilled group of students, while also fostering innovation and hands-on learning experiences for future engineers. We welcome any assistance that can help propel our team toward success in the RoboSub competition.

Name: Alsanor
Description:
For thousands of years, human progress has been measured by what we could lift, clean, move, and build with our own hands.
We created machines to help, but most of them stayed limited, trapped in factories, built for repetition, not understanding.
The world inside our homes, hotels, and hospitals still runs on fatigue.

Alsanor is building the next generation of machines, humanoid robots designed to live and work among us, sharing our spaces, our rhythm, and our understanding of the environment.
Robots that don’t just execute tasks but understand context: a hallway, a room, a tray, a smile.

Our goal is simple and audacious:
Put a capable robot in more than 100 000 locations within the next decade.

From housekeeping to healthcare, from homes to the highest towers, we are giving buildings their own nervous system of motion, perception, memory, and intent.
When every building can clean, carry, and prepare on its own, humanity gets to focus on what only we can do: imagine, connect, and experience life to the fullest.

We see a future where humans can finally be unburdened, and where robots take over the weight, the noise, and the repetition.
Every sensor, every joint, every line of code moves us closer to a world where intelligence is not confined to screens but walks beside us.

This is not science fiction. This is the next infrastructure of civilization.

We are building it now.

Willkommen bei FUCO-CARE ROBOTICS – Ihrem Partner für innovative Reinigungs- und Service-Roboter.

Unser Produktportfolio umfasst eine Vielzahl von Reinigungsrobotern in unterschiedlichen Größen, die gezielt auf die individuellen Bedürfnisse jeder Branche zugeschnitten sind. Darüber hinaus bieten wir Begleit- und Service-Roboter an, die insbesondere in Bereichen wie Fitnessstudios, Hotels, Büros oder im Einzelhandel wertvolle Unterstützung leisten.
Die Geschäftsstrategie von FUCO-CARE ROBOTICS basiert darauf, die zeitintensive manuelle Reinigungsarbeit durch unsere fortschrittlichen Roboter zu reduzieren. Dadurch profitieren unsere Kunden nicht nur von gesteigerter Effizienz und Produktivität, sondern auch von einer erheblichen Kostenersparnis. Angesichts des Fachkräftemangels ist unser Angebot besonders relevant: Viele Unternehmen sehen sich mit der Herausforderung konfrontiert, qualifiziertes Personal im Bereich Reinigung und Dienstleistung zu finden.
FUCO-CARE ROBOTICS wurde ins Leben gerufen, um drängende Probleme in verschiedenen Branchen anzugehen – Probleme verursacht durch ineffiziente manuelle Prozesse. Unser Ziel ist es, unseren Kunden eine leistungsstarke Alternative zu bieten und aktiv zur Entwicklung innovativer Automatisierungstechnologien beizutragen.
Durch den Einsatz unserer Roboter können Sie als Kunde nicht nur Zeit sparen – sie erfahren auch einen echten Mehrwert dank maßgeschneiderter Konfigurationen für jede Branche. Damit schaffen wir einen einzigartigen Kundennutzen und positionieren uns als Vorreiter in der Reinigungs- und Service-Roboter-Branche in Deutschland.

Name: IntRoLab
Description:
IntRoLab est un laboratoire de recherche qui étudie, développe, intègre et utilise des méthodologies en mécatronique et en intelligence artificielle pour la conception de systèmes autonomes et intelligents. Les activités touchent différents champs de recherche portant sur la conception matérielle et logicielle de systèmes robotisés, embarqués et intelligents, ainsi qu'à l'évaluation de ces systèmes dans leur milieu d'opération. Les recherches menées au laboratoire sont qualifiées de pragmatiques, dans le sens qu'ils s'attaquent aux défis pour amener les robots et les systèmes intelligents à être utilisés en pratique dans le but d'améliorer la qualité de vie de la société, et du même coup découvrir les mystères liés à la création d'une intelligence sur des machines. Nos champs d'application sont les robots de services, les robots tout-terrains, les robots interactifs, la robotique d'assistance, la télésanté robotisée, l'automobile et les robots chirurgicaux.

IntRoLab is a research laboratory pursuing the goal of studying, developing, integrate and using mechatronics and artificial intelligence methodologies for the design of autonomous and intelligent systems. Research activities involve software and hardware design of mobile robots, embedded systems and autonomous agents, and in-situ evaluation. Research conducted at the lab is pragmatic, oriented toward overcoming the challenges associated with making robots and intelligent systems usable in real life situations to improve quality of life, and also discover how to give intelligence to machines. Application areas are service robots, all-terrain robots, interactive robots, assistive robotics, telehealth robotics, automobile and surgical robots.

Name: Turul Robotics
Description:
At Turul Robotics, we deliver complete solutions that combine robotic flexibility with CNC precision. We help manufacturers achieve the next level of automation through multi-axis robotic machining, hybrid manufacturing, friction stir welding, and a myriad of other processes — all powered by Siemens NX CAM Robotics and Siemens SINUMERIK controls.

Our expertise lies in the most complex CNC robotic applications, including 6 to 8-axis robotic milling, additive + subtractive hybrid manufacturing, and CAM to Controller digital twin simulation. Each system we design is optimized in NX to ensure collision-free motion, toolpath accuracy, and perfect synchronization between robots and controller.

As authorized partners and resellers of Siemens Digital Industries Software, Siemens SINUMERIK hardware, and robotic systems from KUKA, Comau, and MABI, we provide complete, turnkey solutions — from simulation and design to installation, commissioning, and training.

Our approach bridges the gap between industrial robotics and CNC machining, empowering manufacturers in aerospace, marine, energy, automotive, and advanced composites to produce parts faster, more flexibly, and with uncompromising precision.

The name Turul is a nod to our founder’s roots — the mythical bird of the old Hungarian tribes, symbolizing vision, strength, and innovation. Like the Turul guiding explorers toward new horizons, Turul Robotics guides customers into the future of intelligent manufacturing.

Mission:
To make complex robotic manufacturing simple, powerful, and accessible — enabling manufacturers of all sizes to innovate with precision, flexibility, and confidence.

Name: Xory
Description:
https://youtu.be/78Y5ukojlSM

Xory:

Building neural networks that deliver real-time processing under 500 milliseconds defines our innovation. These systems leverage C++, Rust, and embedded systems to maximize deterministic execution, combined with parallel processing and multithreading for ultra-fast inference across distributed pipelines.

 Developing edge neural network data centers that provide ultra-low latency computing powers the future of infrastructure. Our modular clusters integrate NoSQL databases, memory-disaggregated storage, and microsecond-scale messaging queues, sustaining petabyte-scale throughput across thousands of nodes.

 Designing hydrogen-powered drones with autonomous flight and neural-based navigation redefines mobility. These fleets integrate real-time sensor fusion, LiDAR-driven topological mapping, and GPU-centric reinforcement learning, all orchestrated through Rust-based concurrency models to guarantee fail-safe aerial maneuvers.

 Delivering air hydrogen mobility technology creates zero-emission transportation for a connected planet. This system employs multi-agent reinforcement algorithms, event-driven schedulers, and containerized orchestration for predictable, high-frequency control loops.

 Constructing zero-inflation cryptocurrency systems ensures global financial stability and digital sovereignty. Transaction execution relies on Byzantine fault-tolerant consensus, C-based cryptographic primitives, and hardware-accelerated hash pipelines, sustaining sub-second settlement across a planetary-scale ledger.

 Training large-scale neural networks on high-performance compute clusters advances the boundaries of machine intelligence. Each model spans thousands of petabytes, optimized via CUDA kernels, SIMD vectorization, and asynchronous I/O pipelines for deterministic scalability.

 Scaling distributed neural pipelines across massive storage frameworks unlocks limitless learning capacity.

디든 로보틱스는 2024년 3월에 설립된 로보틱스 스타트업으로, 카이스트 휴보랩의 박해원 교수님 연구실 출신의 연구진 중 뜻이 모인 4명의 팀원들이 창업하였습니다. 저희는 조선업 등 고소 작업이 필요한 환경에서 위험을 줄이고 자동화율을 높이는 승월 로봇 기술을 개발하여 현장의 안전과 산업적 가치를 증대시키는 것을 목표로 하고 있습니다.

30kg급 하중을 적재하고 단차 등 장애물을 극복할 수 있고 벽면 및 천장을 돌아다닐 수 있는 로봇 DIDEN30 을 개발하고 있고, 용접 자동화 공정에 투입될 목적으로 제작중입니다. 구성원들의 지속적인 동기부여와 발전을 위한 업무 분담 및 다양한 보상 제도에 대해 고민합니다. 초기 스타트업으로서 열정 넘치는 구성원들과 함께 빠른 속도로 배우고 성장합니다.

Co spojuje všechny firmy? Úklid. Nejčastěji mokrý úklid. My úspěšně nahrazujeme mytí povrchů pomocí autonomních efektivnějších technik suchého zametání a vysávání. Něvěříte? Poptejte u nás demo a vše v praxi nezávazně dokážeme přímo u vás!

Unlike traditional schools, here kids will learn to love what they are doing, relevant to the ever-evolving world, and rooted in cutting-edge technology. Most importantly, we foster ambition.

Our courses for 7-10 year-olds cover the basics of mechatronics, while those for 11-13 year-olds focus on entrepreneurship with robotics. Both aim to ignite creativity and bring technology to life. We introduce programming, Arduino and sensors, app development, psychological marketing strategies, autonomous robots, and successful entrepreneurial concepts, drawing inspiration from world-renowned entrepreneurs.

Our teaching patterns include daily brainstorming sessions to spark creative ideas and problem-solving skills, hands-on learning with essential equipment, and research activities to inspire self-directed learning on favorite topics. Every activity nurtures an entrepreneurial mindset.

We are passionate about robotics, AI, and other technologies that shape the world, and we are dedicated to creating young minds who are future-leading entrepreneurs with a lifelong passion for innovation.

Our current project is the "YeliBot," a robot learning companion for kids. This bot helps develop communication skills, fosters passionate learning, and aids mental health by managing screen time. Join us as we teach and inspire the next generation of innovators.

Description:
(Le français suit)
We are Kelpie Robotics, an ambitious engineering design team from the University of Ottawa. Nestled within the innovative John McIntyre Team Space, our mission is to cultivate hands-on learning experiences for students passionate about making a tangible impact on the world.

As annual contenders in the MATEROV competition, we specialize in constructing underwater robots—ROVs designed to tackle pressing global challenges like climate change, renewable energy, and ocean conservation. 

Proud participants in the 2022 and 2023 World Championships in Long Beach, California, and Longmont, Colorado, we're now setting our sights on the 2025 World Championships in Alpena, Michigan. Our presence at the University of Ottawa isn't just about participation; it's about making a mark. Our leadership roles embody our entrepreneurial spirit—each position, from CEO, COO to CTO, represents our commitment to innovation and excellence.

Influenced by the spirit of MATEROV, we foster entrepreneurial skills that distinguish us from the crowd. Furthermore, we integrate a comprehensive ESG analysis into our workflow, underscoring our dedication to responsible engineering and sustainable development.

Join us as we dive deeper. #watergame
--
Nous sommes Kelpie Robotics, une équipe de conception en génie de l’Université d’Ottawa basée à l’espace de collaboration John McEntyre. Notre mission : offrir aux étudiants des expériences pratiques à impact réel.

Chaque année, nous participons au concours MATE ROV, en construisant des robots sous-marins pour répondre à des enjeux mondiaux comme le climat, l’énergie durable et la préservation des océans.

Après avoir fièrement représenté l’Université aux championnats du monde en 2022 et 2023, nous visons maintenant Alpena 2025. Du PDG au CTO, notre leadership incarne innovation, excellence et esprit entrepreneurial.

Portés par l’esprit MATEROV, nous intégrons une analyse ESG pour une ingénierie responsable.

Plongez avec nous.

Name: Nexterity, Inc.
Description:
OUR PRODUCT

Nexterity has developed a hands-free robotic tool for the (un)bolting of industrial pipeline infrastructure. (Un)bolting of pipelines is very common and required for routine maintenance and construction of all piping infrastructure. Today, this is primarily accomplished with hand tools/wrenches or electrically powered impact wrenches. These tools require a human user to be in close proximity to a pipe and its bolted joint.

SAFETY

(Un)bolting interface activities are safely managed by a human-in-the-loop via remote control. With our hands-free tool, humans and their hands are able to distance themselves from the pipe and its joint for safety. This distance not only allows for additional safety in the bolting process but also clears enough space to allow a nimble robotic tool to better position itself in front of the pipe joint. With this additional clearance, the robotic tool can interface with multiple bolts at a time.

SPEED

This dexterity and access enables the tool to provide a speed advantage of up to 8x in the (un)bolting process versus the current manual alternative. 

QUALITY

When interfacing with multiple bolts at a time via machine, there is significant reduction of human error in the bolting process. This allows our technology to deliver equal if not better quality than human labor, without requiring the physical exertion. Our tool allows for torque setting within +/- 10 ft*lbs of a target torque and provides the consistency and reliability of a programmed machine.

Wij streven ernaar om een toonaangevende speler te worden in de wereld van service robots, door duurzame, schaalbare en mensgerichte robotica-oplossingen te bieden die bedrijven helpen groeien.

Volg ons en ontdek hoe INNO-WAY Robotics uw bedrijf kan transformeren met de kracht van geavanceerde robotica!

Description:
Our mission is to explore the uncharted potential of autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) and revolutionize underwater research and engineering. As one of the few university student teams focusing on marine innovation, we aim to push the boundaries of technology to better understand and protect our underwater world.

HU Robosub is an innovative underwater robotics project, run by teachers, students, and researchers of HU University of Applied Sciences Utrecht, with transformative potential across multiple industries. Designed to revolutionize marine research and operations, HU Robosub offers diverse applications:

- Research
- Safety
- Inspection
- Maintenance
- Students

From advancing marine research to ensuring safety and innovation, this project bridges industry needs with student-driven development. Explore how the Robosub makes an impact in inspection, maintenance, and education.

Name: Physikal AI
Description:
Physikal AI is building an AI operating system capable of operating instantly, no matter the robots configuration (Humanoids, Drones, Cars)

We are building the single intelligence layer that will run the entire physical world. A universal, continuously learning, standards-aware mission brain that turns any sentence into safe, coordinated action across any robot, anywhere.

We give every robot, from any company, in any country, the ability to understand natural human instructions, collaborate with other robots, and report back clearly.

Our only goal is to accelerate the moment when physical work is no longer a limit on human potential. More goods, more care, more exploration, more time — for every person on Earth (and beyond).

Welcome to Mechonyx Automation Private Limited, a cutting-edge startup that is revolutionizing the world of robotics and automation. At Mechonyx Automation, we believe in the power of robotics to transform industries, redefine consumer experiences, and shape the future of technology. Our expertise lies in autonomous vehicles, industrial automation, and product-based solutions designed to meet the evolving needs of businesses across various sectors.

One of our flagship products, SYRAX, embodies our commitment to excellence. SYRAX is a self-driven, yield load carrying robot that is poised to revolutionize the way industries operate. Industrial player and specialist in timber construction, we support Canadian and international companies in their industrial transformation: from Lean Manufacturing and robotic integration to full production line automation.

How? Through mastery of industrial processes, minimizing preproduction errors, and optimizing resources.

Innovative for the timber sector while inspired by proven applications in various industrial fields, Zellips Group has developed a unique, cross-functional approach and proprietary technology based on BIM integration to synchronize information flows between business software and digital data transmitted to robots.

This industrial excellence, applied to timber construction, relies on teams of industry experts who guide you throughout the industrial transformation process:
 Organizational intelligence driven by Lean Manufacturing specialists to identify productivity gains in your factory, and adopt methods and tools efficiently
 Digital and robotic intelligence to deliver a customized, automated, and robotic production line: Smart Factory 4.0

This technology is continuously supported by the group’s R&D teams to ensure ongoing optimization and adaptability for each company and region.

This seamless model, already proven in France, meets strict construction standards and is expanding in North America and Africa.

 Need to move your production lines? Have an industrial transformation project ?
 We guide you every step of the way to unlock your full potential!
The company is based in France and Canada, serving international clients.

Somos Jatta Printi, un equipo de robótica formado por 7 chicas de 3º de ESO. Participamos en la FIRST LEGO League, una competición internacional que promueve las vocaciones STEAM desde edades tempranas, combinando ciencia, tecnología y valores como el trabajo en equipo y la innovación.

 Dos años consecutivos clasificadas para la Final Nacional de la FLL
 Un robot diseñado y programado por nosotras para superar misiones en 2 minutos y medio
 Un proyecto de innovación para el reto Submerged, T-Bubble, un accesorio para hacer más visibles los tags, pequeños dispositivos de rastreo de especies… y ahora también premiado a nivel internacional! PRIMER Premio Proyecto de Innovación Open Africa FLL 2025

 Nuestro próximo reto: seguir creciendo, compartiendo lo que hacemos y demostrando que la robótica es mucho más que construir un robot. Es creatividad, perseverancia y una herramienta para cambiar el mundo.

 Si te apasiona la educación STEAM, el talento joven y el futuro, conectemos.

 Buscamos patrocinadores y colaboraciones que quieran unirse a esta aventura.

Síguenos, escríbenos… y ayúdanos a llegar todavía más lejos!

Name: SYLA
Description:
SYLA is on a mission to bring movement back to life.
We are a pioneering company designing intelligent, motor-powered prosthetic knees that help people walk more naturally, safely, and confidently. Our goal is simple: to make walking feel like walking again.

For too long, prosthetic technology has lagged behind. Many people still rely on outdated mechanical joints that don’t adapt, don’t respond, and don’t support real freedom of movement. At SYLA, we’re changing that.

We’re building smart prosthetic knees powered by AI and robotics—knees that think, learn, and adapt to how people move. Our prosthesis can sense motion, predict behavior, and react in real time to help users walk smoother on stairs, slopes, or uneven ground. This isn’t science fiction. It’s happening now.

Our team of engineers, designers, and researchers spans Ukraine, Poland, and the U.S., and we’re proud to be part of a new generation of healthtech innovation. In 2025, we showcased our work at CES as part of Ukraine’s national tech delegation and continue to grow through partnerships, community support, and user testing.

We believe prosthetics should be personal, powerful, and beautiful. That’s why we’re also exploring ways to blend technology with design—making limbs not just functional, but expressive and human.

At SYLA, we don’t just build devices. We build freedom. We build confidence. We build a future where limb loss doesn’t mean life limitations.

Let’s walk into that future—together.

#Prosthetics #MedTech #HealthTech #AssistiveTechnology #AI #Robotics #SYLA

Name: Aaveq Robotics ApS
Description:
Aaveq Robotics develops unmanned boats to sail in arctic conditions, handling logistics between towns and smaller settlements.

Our focus and first step is tackling the waste management problem in Greenland, halting the endless growth of garbage dumps in the smaller settlements, where it is not effective, nor always possible, to send larger manned ships to collect the waste.

The large dumps are a threat to the environment, as well as the health of the local population. 

We were founded in April 2025, originating from a student team in SDU's DreamLab since 2022. We have secured the Innofounder grant, which runs from October 2025 to September 2026.

Name: Institute for Man-Machine Interaction (MMI) @ RWTH Aachen University
Description: The Institute for Man-Machine Interaction (MMI) is a nationally and internationally active and recognized research institution in the Faculty of Electrical Engineering and Information Technology at RWTH Aachen University. MMI is a specialist for modeling, simulation, and analysis of complex systems in their operational environment, for interaction and networking of machines, devices and people as well as for intelligent systems – in space and on earth. Digital Twins realized at MMI revolutionize development processes and the operation of (not only) robotic systems and are – across industries and disciplines – the basis for the convergence of virtual and real worlds. The diverse fields of application range from space to production engineering to forestry.

Description:

ASIRA is our next-generation robotic innovation, designed to physically adapt to a variety of tasks while maximizing energy efficiency. Our vision is to transform industrial robots from rigid, task-specific machines into intelligent, adaptive systems. Inspired by nature, ASIRA dynamically adjusts its configuration to meet diverse task demands, from delicate assemblies to rapid operations, all while prioritizing energy efficiency.
By leveraging AI, ASIRA bridges the gap between hardware and software, enabling quick, autonomous adaptation and optimization. This revolution in automation sets new standards in precision, flexibility, and sustainability.

The team’s flagship project—a modular rescue rover—is engineered to navigate hazardous environments like collapsed structures, rough terrain, and disaster zones, delivering real-time data to emergency responders.

By integrating cutting-edge robotics and artificial intelligence, ALTER is committed to improving the speed, safety, and efficiency of rescue operations during events such as earthquakes, floods, and industrial accidents. The team blends academic research with real-world application, striving to empower first responders and save lives when every second matters.

Name: Rengøringsrobotten
Description:
Velkommen til rengøringsrobotten.dk! Vi er specialister i udlejning af avancerede rengøringsrobotter til fejning og gulvvask af store gulvarealer. Vores mission er at gøre rengøringen mere effektiv, hurtig og problemfri for vores kunder.

Med vores topmoderne rengøringsrobotter kan du effektivt håndtere selv de største gulvarealer uden besvær. Vores udvalg af robotter er designet til at levere enestående resultater og optimere rengøringsprocessen. Uanset om det drejer sig om kontorer, fabrikker, lagerhaller eller butikslokaler, har vi den perfekte rengøringsløsning til dig.

Vi er stolte af vores innovative teknologi og vores robotter er udstyret med avancerede funktioner, der sikrer en grundig og pålidelig rengøring hver gang. Med vores professionelle support og vejledning er du i trygge hænder, når det kommer til valg af den rette rengøringsrobot til dine specifikke behov.

Når du vælger rengøringsrobotten.dk som din partner, får du ikke kun adgang til branchens førende rengøringsløsninger, men også en dedikeret og pålidelig service. Vi er her for at hjælpe dig med alt fra rådgivning om produktvalg til teknisk support og vedligeholdelse.

Gør rengøringen af store gulvarealer til en leg med vores avancerede rengøringsrobotter. Kontakt os i dag for at få mere information og en skræddersyet løsning til dine rengøringsbehov. Sammen kan vi optimere din rengøringsproces og frigøre tid og ressourcer til andre vigtige opgaver.

Our mission: real-time visibility and full control over material flows – indoors and outdoors.

The result: a unified platform that orchestrates all mobile assets across hybrid logistics environments.

Datacept operates across diverse industries with complex logistics needs – from indoor-heavy sectors like food & beverage and manufacturing, to rugged outdoor domains like building materials, chemicals, and automotive.

We’re not another point solution.

Name: PREXROBOT CEE LTD
Description:
Fenntartható robotizált és automatizált takarítás vállalkozásoknak!

A PrexRobot CEE a robotizált takarítási technológia élvonalában áll Közép- és Kelet-Európában. Automatizált padlótisztítási megoldásaink segítenek a vállalatoknak időt, költséget és munkaerő-erőforrást megtakarítani, miközben hozzájárulnak a fenntartható működéshez.
Legyen szó irodai-, közintézményi-, kórházi helyiségekről, kis- és nagykereskedelmi létesítményekről, parkolóházakról, ipari létesítményekről, logisztikai központokról vagy közterületek tisztán tartásáról, a PrexRobot ajánlatai között biztosan talál céljainak megfelelő automatizált asszisztenciát.

Mit kínálunk?
• Automatizálás: Robotizált takarítás, amely minimalizálja a kézi beavatkozást. 
• Költséghatékonyság: Csökkentse munkaerő- és üzemeltetési költségeit.
• Fenntarthatóság: Energia- és víztakarékos padlótisztító robotjaink a környezeti terhelés csökkentéséért dolgoznak, akár 80%-kal csökkenti az édesvízfelhasználást.
• Rugalmasság: Innovatív technológia irodák, ipari és kereskedelmi létesítmények, parkolók, valamint közterületek folyamatos és maximális hatékonyságú tisztítására.
• Megbízhatóság: Automatizált takarítás, amely folyamatosan biztosítja a kiváló minőséget. A rövid töltési időt leszámítva akár 24 órás folyamatos munkavégzésre képes. 

Miért válassza a PrexRobot CEE-t?
Technológiánk nemcsak hatékonyabbá teszi a takarítást, de lehetővé teszi, hogy Ön a valóban fontos üzleti tevékenységekre koncentráljon. A robotizált padlótisztítás és az AI-vezérelt takarítás a tisztább, költséghatékonyabb és fenntarthatóbb jövőt építi.

Name: OctaPulse (YC W26)
Description: OctaPulse develops AI-powered inspection systems that automate the grading of juvenile fish in aquaculture hatcheries. Today, hatcheries rely on manual inspection of hundreds of thousands of fish per cohort—a labor-intensive process that takes up to 10 technicians over a week and is prone to human error. Our computer vision system detects deformities with over 90% accuracy, reducing labor costs and improving the consistency of fish selected for grow-out pens. This boosts survivability, reduces feed waste, and increases profitability.

Name: Team Cyberweb
Description:
We are FRC Team Cyberweb, a FIRST Robotics team in formation. It is our mission to connect tech, education and industry in the southwest of our country.

We call FIRST Robotics Competition the ultimate Sport for the Mind. High-school student participants call it “the hardest fun you’ll ever have.”

Under strict rules, limited time and resources, teams of students are challenged to raise funds, design a team "brand," hone teamwork skills, and build and program industrial-size robots to play a difficult field game against like-minded competitors. It’s as close to real-world engineering as a student can get. Volunteer professional mentors lend their time and talents to guide each team. Each season ends with an exciting FIRST Championship.

Name: UBC Okanagan Marine Robotics
Description:
We're thrilled to introduce Okanagan Marine Robotics at UBC Okanagan. If you're as passionate about marine technology as we are, this club is your next port of call. We have two main focus areas: Electric Propulsion Boats and Autonomous Underwater Vehicles. This is the first and only club at UBCO dedicated to autonomous vehicles! We compete in various competitions such as the Monaco Energy Boat Challenge, Toronto Solar Boat Race, American Society of Naval Engineers for the Electric Propulsion Boat team, and RoboSub for the AUV team.

1. Autonomous Underwater Vehicles (AUVs):

This team is preparing to compete at the RoboSub competition by navigating through different levels of underwater obstacles. These obstacles include tasks such as shooting torpedoes, grabbing objects, dropping markers, and much more, testing the skills of our multidisciplinary team.

Join the Voyage:
Chat with us at Okanagan Marine Robotics, where your ideas can set sail!

Name: Formal Control and Autonomous Systems Lab
Description: With the rise of robotics and autonomous systems, there is a need for robust, reliable, and safe controllers. Robotics and autonomous systems is an inherently multi-disciplinary field that requires expertise in Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering and Computer Science. Despite advancements in this field, developing controllers for a robotic system is challenging, especially if it is complex, large and has multiple agents. We work on interdisciplinary research, developing controllers and implementing them in hardware.

Name: Robotics Norden
Description:
Robotics Norden gjør norsk arbeidsliv enklere, tryggere og mer effektivt, med serviceroboter som faktisk virker.
Vi leverer smarte løsninger for automatisk renhold, servering og logistikk, og hjelper kundene med å ta i bruk teknologi som frigjør tid og reduserer belastning i hverdagen.

Vår spesialitet er praktisk automatisering: Vi forenkler tunge og repeterende oppgaver i dagligvarebutikker, skoler, hoteller, kommunale bygg, sykehjem og helseforetak, med dokumentert effekt. Kundene rapporterer bedre kvalitet, lavere sykefravær og mindre behov for vikarer. Flere velger nå å utvide bruken etter gode erfaringer.

Vi tilbyr hele prosessen fra A til Å: demonstrasjon, pilotfase, opplæring, finansieringsløsninger og norsk driftssupport. Alt vi leverer er tilpasset nordiske forhold, med fokus på brukeropplevelse, kvalitet og reell nytte. Vi tror ikke på teknologi for teknologiens skyld, vi tror på løsninger som virker i praksis.

Robotics Norden er norsk distributør for Pudu Robotics, og tilbyr blant annet PUDU CC1 – en fire-i-én rengjøringsrobot som mopper, støvsuger, feier og vasker gulv i én og samme operasjon. Den navigerer selv, fyller på vann, lader automatisk og dokumenterer renhold digitalt. Robotene våre brukes i både offentlig og privat sektor – fra kontorbygg og kjøpesentre til museer og skoler.

Vi er mer enn en forhandler. Vi er en partner med lokal tilstedeværelse, egne teknikere og rådgivere som følger opp hele reisen, fra første møte til full drift. Hos oss er roboten ikke en fremmed maskin, men en ny kollega. En hjelp i hverdagen.

Teknologi som virker. Folk som bryr seg.
Serviceroboter | Automatisk renhold | Robotvask | Praktisk automatisering | Pudu Robotics | Forhandlersamarbeid | Norsk arbeidsliv

Name: TUM DASH
Description:
DASH is an exoskeleton student team at the Technical University of Munich (TUM) founded in November 2021. It consists of motivated students from their bachelor, master, and PhD studies. To our knowledge, we are the first student-initiated team in Germany working on lower-limb exoskeletons.

Our goal is to build a lower-limb exoskeleton for fully paraplegic patients to help them regain mobility. An exoskeleton that can be worn in real life setting would certainly change the world of many. It is important for us to overcome the significant sociocultural and physical barriers paraplegic people face and make a contribution to the development of exoskeleton technology by pursuing this project.

Name: POLARIS - ARIS focus project
Description: The aim of this project is to develop a modular, scalable, remotely observed swarm of underwater robots capable of performing real-time data collection and coordinated actions across a broad range of environments. Unlike fully autonomous systems, our robots will initially focus on proving practical feasibility within our limited timeframe and resources, while keeping open the possibility for future autonomous functionalities. This will involve building working prototypes and iterating on current research in underwater swarm communication and control.

Name: MARS Lab
Description:
MARS Lab – Mechatronics and Advanced Robotic Systems Laboratory

Where Academia Transforms into Industry Innovation…a DTU Electro initiative

MARS Lab is a collaborative research and development hub dedicated to bridging the gap between academia and industry in robotics and hardware development. We bring together students, researchers, and industry professionals to transform cutting-edge academic research into real-world applications.

Our expertise spans mechatronics, advanced robotics, autonomous systems, and hardware design, focusing on innovation-driven solutions that address industrial challenges. We believe in hands-on learning, interdisciplinary collaboration, and developing technologies that push the boundaries of what is possible in automation, AI-driven systems, and intelligent robotics.

What We Do:
	•	Research & Development: Exploring next-generation robotic systems and hardware technologies.
	•	Industry Collaboration: Partnering with companies to bring research innovations to market.
	•	Student & Researcher Engagement: Providing hands-on opportunities for young talent to work on industry-relevant projects.
	•	Open Innovation: Encouraging knowledge-sharing and co-development of robotic solutions.

Join us in shaping the future of robotics and hardware development. We welcome collaborations with academia, startups, and established industries looking to innovate through cutting-edge technology.

 Let’s build the future of robotics together!

Description:
Rotor Teknoloji, tecrübeli mühendislik ekibi ve yerli imkanlarla, ihtiyaçlara yönelik teknolojinin en ileri noktasında elektrik motorları geliştirmekte ve üretmektedir. Firma ilk olarak fırçasız, oluksuz motor ailesini üretmiştir. Geliştirilen motorlar, yüksek hassasiyet, sıfır titreşim ve yüksek güç yoğunluğu özellikleri ile benzer özelliklere sahip motorlara göre daha iyi performans sergilemektedir. Ayrıca Rotor Teknoloji, robotik, medikal, havacılık ve uzay alanlarında farklı ihtiyaçlara yönelik özel tip motorlar tasarlama ve üretme kabiliyetine sahiptir.

Name: Sidekick Robotics
Description:
Let your staff be the hero: give them a Sidekick. 

Founded in 2025 out of Stanford University by identical twin brothers, Sidekick Robotics develops self-improving robots for the healthcare labor shortage that can see, think, and act, with increasing precision. Unlike base foundation models, we’re training Sidekick to master full real-world workflows: the boring, backbreaking physical tasks that staff do every day, but shouldn’t have to. 

Sidekick is an intelligent AI robot that handles the heavy lifting in healthcare communities—managing laundry, housekeeping, and other physically demanding tasks that take staff away from patient care. By automating physical labor in split labor and care environments, we enable better quality of care.

Sidekick Robotics is part of the Google DeepMind Gemini Robotics Trusted Tester Program.

Name: American Droid
Description:
Built for the Field. Engineered for Public Service.

American Droid is a robotics engineering and integration company dedicated to enhancing public service missions.


With our partners, we are currently prototyping and advancing toward the commercialization of telepresence robots equipped with dexterous end-effectors (robot hands) —designed to perform tasks that require durable, human-like fine motor skills in the field.

This work is supported by the Department of Defense’s Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) program and centers on the development of a next-generation robotic hand system aimed at closing a critical capability gap. The system features highly dexterous manipulators with precision control and human hand-like strength, enabling operators to perform complex tasks via teleoperation in austere and high-risk environments.

As we continue to develop our robotic hands, integrated platforms, and teleoperated and autonomous capabilities, our broader goal is to strengthen the domestic robotics industrial base and establish a resilient, U.S.-based production model that supports national readiness and long-term innovation.

We are actively seeking government customers who can benefit from teleoperated end-effectors for integration into new or existing platforms, as well as technology partners interested in advancing U.S.-based robotic solutions.

Contact us to learn more and explore how we can work together to bolster American-made robotic capabilities in service of public missions.

Name: RAAD Conference 2025
Description:
The 34th International Conference on Robotics in Alpe-Adria-Danube Region, RAAD 2025, will be held 18-20 June, 2025 in Belgrade, Serbia.

The conference will bring together academic and industrial robotics researchers from the member countries of the Alpe-Adria-Danube region and their global partners in a collegial and stimulating environment.

As is tradition, RAAD 2025 will cover all major areas of research, development and innovation in robotics, including new research trends such as: bioinspired and cognitive robots, visual servo control of robotic motions, human-robot interaction, cloud robotics and personal robots for ambient assisted living.

Important Dates
Proposal for Special Session: September 30, 2024

Full paper submission: January 20, 2025

Notification of acceptance: February 24, 2025

Final paper submission: March 10, 2025

Early Registration: April 28, 2025

Learn more at https://raad2025.etf.bg.ac.rs/media/RAAD2025_Call_for_papers.pdf

Description:
Founded in 2018 in Denizli, Türkiye, NCT Robotics was established to be a pioneer in both academic and industrial robotics. We are dedicated to shaping the future through innovative technology and education.

In the academic side, NCT Robotics empowers the next generation of creators and innovators with comprehensive robotics coding education for ages 7-18 and advanced CAD/CAM training for individuals over 18.

On the industrial field, we specialize in the development and deployment of autonomous robot services and solutions. Our expertise lies in creating indoor robotic systems, meticulously designed with open-source codes and tailored to meet the unique needs of our clients. Our offerings include advanced CAD and design services to ensure precision and customization.

As NCT Robotics, we are ready to help you in the following areas:
- Designing and producing industrial Autonomous Mobile Robots (AMRs) and service robots, optimized for specific tasks and environments using cutting-edge autonomous navigation software.
- Providing high autonomous mobility solutions that navigate both static and dynamic obstacles with ease.
- Enhancing workload efficiency, optimization, and time savings through integrated systems.
- Delivering customized economic solutions and plans tailored to your needs.
