Name: CoMPAS Lab
Description:
The Control, Modeling, and Perception of Autonomous Systems (CoMPAS) Laboratory is committed to building scalable foundations for marine robotics. We develop essential tools that support ongoing research and lay the groundwork for future innovations.

Our mission is to facilitate autonomous ocean exploration in complex, unmapped environments using multiple robotic platforms. By tackling fundamental challenges in autonomy, we aim to support persistent presence, dynamic ocean visualization, adaptive sampling, environmental change detection, and long-term monitoring—from the seafloor to the midwater column.

Our efforts concentrate on advancing SLAM techniques that integrate visual and acoustic data for robust navigation, developing adaptive control strategies for complex marine settings, and enabling coordinated multi-vehicle operations with both homogeneous and heterogeneous platforms.

Together, these focus areas constitute the backbone of scalable, intelligent, and resilient marine autonomy, making ocean exploration more accessible and widespread.

Robotten er modulopbygget, så metoden til ukrudtsbekæmpelse kan udskiftes med andre teknologier, afhængigt af behovet.

Med fokus på præcision, datadrevet landbrug og minimal miljøpåvirkning arbejder vi på at revolutionere økologisk ukrudtsbekæmpelse og gøre det lettere for landmænd at drive en mere bæredygtig produktion.

Name: ACRA 2025
Description:
The 2025 Australasian Conference on Robotics and Automation (ACRA 2025) is jointly organised by Edith Cowan University (ECU) and Australian Robotics and Automation Association (ARAA). The conference will be held in person on 1-3 December 2025 at ECU Joondalup Campus in Perth, Western Australia. 

Since 1999, ACRA has brought together Australian and New Zealand researchers and students in robotics, automation, and mechatronics. It provides a forum to present a breadth of research activities to the broader Australasian community. ACRA is a quality, peer-reviewed conference featured in Scopus, Google Scholar, and other ranking engines.

Our TC seeks to bring together researchers with interest in innovative mechanical hardware in robotic systems in order to share knowledge, standardize practices/procedures, and raise the profile of the exciting new work being done in robot mechanisms and design. Additionally, we seek to establish strong ties to the robotics and robotic components industries to facilitate technology transfer, educate researchers on new available technologies, approaches, and standards, and help anchor research approaches to commercial needs and market viability.

Name: MX Interface
Description:
MX Interface helps manufacturers' machines and robots work autonomously together to maximize output.
Here’s how…

We see lots of manufacturers, from small production shops to global OEMs, with the same problems…

Machines sitting idle
Robots not fully utilized
Teams struggling to fill the repetitive or unsafe jobs no one wants

Which lead to inefficient production and lost revenue.


So our team at Process Robotics built MX Interface!

A software that enables manufacturers to flexibly automate and assign load/unload jobs across any mix of robots and machines. 

- One robot can manage multiple machines
- Multiple AMRs can respond dynamically to shop floor needs
 
No complex integrations, custom wiring, special drivers, or waiting for a button to get pushed…
 
Just 1 platform that autonomously determines which robot is free & what machine task it can handle. 


With it, clients have automated up to 60% of machine output, creating safer environments while scaling production.


WHAT MX INTERFACE DOES

Maximize Utilization, Minimize Downtime → All robots and machines on your factory floor coordinate automatically and seamlessly without human button-pushing

Store Data Locally & Securely → Data is owned by you and can be stored and analyzed anywhere…no cloud-dependency or 3rd party subscriptions!

Amplify Your Workforce → Get more work done with the same resources, eliminate unfilled job positions, and keep workers safe from dangerous machinery

Help When You Need It → We provide ongoing software maintenance and support that keeps production running


For small production shops to global OEMs, MX Interface unlocks machine and robot efficiency that’s been hiding in plain sight!

Curious to see it in action?

We’ll show you exactly how it works for your facility.
Book a demo... mxinterface.com/#book-a-demo 
or DM us

Description:
𝐃𝐞𝐦𝐨𝐤𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐬𝐢𝐞𝐫𝐮𝐧𝐠 𝐝𝐞𝐫 𝐑𝐨𝐛𝐨𝐭𝐢𝐤 – 𝐠𝐞𝐧𝐚𝐮 𝐝𝐚𝐬 𝐢𝐬𝐭 𝐝𝐚𝐬 𝐙𝐢𝐞𝐥 𝐝𝐞𝐬 𝐧𝐞𝐮𝐞𝐧 𝐙𝐈𝐌-𝐍𝐞𝐭𝐳𝐰𝐞𝐫𝐤𝐬 𝐑𝐨𝐛𝐨𝐭𝐢𝐜𝐬 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐇𝐮𝐦𝐚𝐧𝐬

Derzeit stehen die Akteure im aufstrebenden, aber noch nicht sichtbaren Bereich der Menschennahen Robotik jedoch vor vielfältigen Herausforderungen. Zukünftige Robotergenerationen müssen zum Beispiel durch Sicherheit und Akzeptanz bei den Nutzern überzeugen, gleichzeitig jedoch auch wirtschaftlich hergestellt werden können. In den nächsten Jahren möchte das ZIM-Netzwerk Robotics for Humans an diesen (Weiter-)Entwicklungen arbeiten und so einerseits die internationale Wettbewerbsfähigkeit und andererseits den frühzeitigen Zugang zu einem vielversprechenden Marktsegment gewährleisten. Unsere Vision dabei: Roboter näher an den Menschen bringen und sie dazu befähigen, echte Helfer zu sein!

Folgen Sie uns hier auf Linkedin und lernen Sie unsere Partner, Ideen und Projekte kennen!

What makes EXOTU unique is our interdisciplinary approach. Members gain hands-on experience in design, prototyping, testing, and systems integration. Skills that go far beyond the classroom and directly prepare them for careers in emerging technologies.

Beyond technical expertise, EXOTU fosters a strong community built on teamwork, leadership, and collaboration. We provide opportunities for professional development, industry networking, and participation in design competitions. Through these experiences, our members grow not only as engineers but also as innovators, problem-solvers, and leaders.

As Ontario Tech’s exoskeleton design team, we strive to showcase the university’s commitment to cutting-edge innovation while building technologies that can make a meaningful impact on society.

Description:
At RoboJain, we believe robotics should be accessible, practical, and built for real-world needs. We specialize in developing DIY to medium-scale robotic solutions across a wide range of price points—making innovation affordable and achievable for businesses, institutions, and individuals alike. Whether it’s hardware, software, or an integrated system, we transform ideas into engineered realities with precision, creativity, and cost-effectiveness.

Name: Team Lambda
Description:
At team Lambda, we're on a mission to fuse robotics with sustainable innovation. While rooted in India, our global vision is to transform how renewable energy systems are maintained worldwide.

As countries work toward Sustainable Development Goal 7: Affordable and Clean Energy, the demand for efficient, reliable infrastructure continues to grow. Solar power is at the forefront of this transition, but one critical challenge remains: maintenance.

Dust accumulation on solar panels can drastically reduce energy output. Traditional cleaning methods are costly, labor-intensive, and inefficient at scale. That’s why Team Lambda is developing a fully automated Solar Panel Cleaning Bot, built to operate with minimal human intervention!

Our solution improves solar farm performance, reduces maintenance costs, and supports the long-term success of clean energy, not just in India but globally.

By combining robotics with sustainability, Team Lambda is helping power the future of energy.

Name: Lightside Surgical
Description:
Lightside Surgical is advancing the future of trauma care by bringing surgical expertise to the point of injury. Using a combination of telepresence, robotics, AI, and simulation, we are developing portable systems that enable medics to stabilize critically injured patients during field evacuation. Our technology connects frontline providers to remote surgeons in real time, allowing for earlier intervention, guided procedures, and continuous patient monitoring—when every second counts.

Initially serving military medical units, Lightside Surgical will expand into EMS systems, hospitals, and ultimately rural and austere environments. Our long-term vision is to create a compact, deployable “OR for One” that empowers urgent care clinics in remote settings to deliver life-saving treatment. By closing the gap between injury and access to care, we aim to prevent avoidable deaths and transform how trauma is treated anywhere in the world.

Our mission is to merge biology, robotics, and artificial intelligence to build highly advanced humanoid systems and bio-integrated prosthetics. We are creating a world where humans control robotic avatars remotely, work in hazardous environments safely, and extend their physical capabilities through AI-powered wearable technology.

Our cutting-edge research includes:

Remote-Controlled Humanoids: Built for use in military, disaster relief, space exploration, and industry — operated via neural interfaces, haptic suits, and 360° vision systems.
Fully Autonomous Androids: Powered by proprietary AI, designed to perform complex tasks independently in dangerous or labor-intensive settings.
Lifelike Smart Skin: We are developing synthetic skin with realistic texture, temperature control, and embedded sensors — bridging the gap between machines and human perception.
Next-Gen Prosthetics: Biomechanically adaptive, AI-enhanced prosthetics that feel and behave like real limbs — restoring human functionality and confidence.

Name: Sole1
Description:
Sole¹ | Never Stop Moving.

Sole¹ exists to stop mobility loss caused by disability, injury, and aging. We’re starting with foot drop, a condition that impacts millions and often forces reliance on painful, stigmatizing braces.

Our solution: a wearable robotic sock powered by AI-driven synthetic muscles. It adapts to each wearer’s unique gait, acting as a second layer of muscle that restores dorsiflexion, function, and autonomy. It blends biomechanics and fashion to create a low-profile, comfortable wearable that people can wear proudly. The device supports people as their needs evolve and helps them stay active throughout life.

Machine learning is at the core. Our sensor algorithm learns each user’s unique gait pattern and improves over time, making the device increasingly natural and integrated with the user.

Beyond the technology, what drives Sole¹ is a commitment to designing around natural human motion so mobility is never a limitation, creating wearable robotics that are as iconic and expressive as personal style, and ensuring every step restores not just physical ability but also mental confidence and independence.

Our Mission: to make lifelong mobility an expectation, not a challenge.

Follow @sole1augmentation
Join the community to test the device, shape the future, and move with us.

Description:
Welcome to Automation X, where we're committed to transforming businesses through the power of robotics. Our mission is clear: to aid businesses in growth by utilizing the potential of automation. We're passionate about raising awareness of the numerous benefits that robotics can bring to manufacturing, including increased productivity, profitability, and enhanced safety measures.

Our journey began with a small group of engineers united by a common purpose: to make robotics accessible and affordable for businesses of all sizes. We recognized the potential for automation to revolutionize industries, while also understanding the challenges that many businesses face in adopting this technology. That's why we set out to offer a phased approach, tailored to the unique needs of each customer, guiding them on an automation journey that's both practical and cost-effective.

At Automation X, our values are at the heart of everything we do. We believe in delivering quality solutions that exceed expectations, with honest pricing and competitive costs. Transparency is paramount in our interactions with clients, ensuring they have a clear understanding of the processes and outcomes. Above all, safety is our top priority in all aspects of our work, prioritizing the well-being of both our clients and their workforce.

Our team of directors brings a wealth of experience and expertise to the table, with a collective focus on engineering and automation. Having dedicated our careers to this field, we are committed to staying at the forefront of technological advancements and industry best practices.

What sets us apart at Automation X is our unwavering commitment to our customers' success. Unlike profit-driven enterprises, we are driven by a desire to see businesses thrive and grow. We view our relationship with clients as a partnership, and we strive to ensure that the automation solutions we implement benefit both Automation X and our valued customers.

Name: PPM Robotics AS
Description:
Our vision: Humans and service robots in perfect harmony and synergy
The founders, PhD Trygve Thomessen, and MSc Per Kristian Sannæs founded Productive Programming Methods AS, in year 2000. The intention was to create business by utilizing their strong R&D competence to create new, ultra flexible industrial robot solutions.

Since then, PPM (the company name was shortened in 2004) has delivered high-tech industrial robot systems for small series production, even one-of-a-kind, to the Norwegian and the international industry.

In 2010, PPM entered a strategic partnership with the Japanese robot manufacturer NACHI-Fujikoshi and expanded our business to Asia, by delivering the software module, FlexGui to NACHI's robot controller.

From 2019, PPM started a strategic process, to enter the market for service robots in elderly care. This, to meet the demand for high quality elderly services, under the coming, challenging conditions, due to lack of health care personnel. With PPM's strong industrial experience, combined with the lack of professional service robots for this demand, PPM has a strong competitive advantage.

This was even strengthened when PPM became share holder in the French service-robot manufacturer, KOMPAÏ Robotics SAS, from 2023. Together, PPM and KOMPAÏ develop state-of-the art within service-robots with artificial social skills; especially dedicated applications in modern nursing homes and hospitals.

With our 20years experience both within industry and R&D, we're offering our customers functional, user friendly and reliable service-robot solutions, being installed and certified according to the modern quality standards.

Name: CODE-A-ROBOT
Description:
CODE-A-ROBOT: Robotics and Coding for Children and Teens

Welcome to CODE-A-ROBOT, an innovative program introducing children and teens to the captivating world of robotics and coding. Our mission is to empower young minds with essential skills for the digital age.

Learning at CODE-A-ROBOT is educational and fun. Participants engage in hands-on robotics activities and coding exercises, exploring programming principles while building and interacting with their own robots. This fosters critical thinking, problem-solving, and creativity.

Key Features:

1. Robotics Workshops: Expert instructors guide participants through exciting workshops covering fundamental robotics concepts—mechanical design, electronics, and automation. They build robots using provided kits, gaining hands-on experience.

2. Coding Challenges: Participants dive into coding, programming robots for tasks and navigating obstacles. Challenges cover languages like Scratch, Python, C/C++, JavaScript, and Java, honing logical reasoning and computational thinking.

3. Collaborative Learning: CODE-A-ROBOT promotes teamwork and communication. Participants work in teams, sharing ideas and collaborating on projects, developing social skills and teamwork abilities.

4. Progression Pathways: Our program accommodates all skill levels. Participants progress through a structured curriculum, building upon knowledge and skills, tailored to challenge each student appropriately.

6. Showcase and Competitions: Participants showcase creations and compete in robotics challenges, celebrating achievements, boosting confidence, and encouraging healthy competition.

7. Future-Ready Skills: CODE-A-ROBOT equips children and teens with future-ready skills for today's digital age—problem-solving, critical thinking, and innovation.

Join CODE-A-ROBOT, where robotics and coding come alive! Unlock your child's potential, ignite curiosity, and set them on a path to success in the exciting world of robotics and coding.

Name: PaInt Lab
Description: Welcome to the Personal and Interactive Autonomous Systems (PaInt) Laboratory at Concordia! Our goal is to develop AI systems that seamlessly integrate with and support people in their dynamic environments over the long term. Towards this goal, one of the core problems that we work on is long-term personalization through a bi-directional relationship between AI agents (such as robots, autonomous vehicles, LLMs, recommender systems, etc.) and their users. In this relationship, users can continually interact with and teach AI agents about their individual preferences and unique environments, and these agents can provide long-term personalized assistance to the users by reasoning on the continually acquired knowledge. Our lab's expertise spans robotics, human-robot interaction, machine learning, computer vision, artificial intelligence, human-computer interaction, and cognitive science. We ground our work through various applications, such as assistive robotic arms and mobile manipulators, autonomous vehicles, smart systems, and experiments with real human participants.

Name: Down Deep Drones
Description:
Who We Are 
At Down Deep Drones, we are pioneers in underwater drone technology, committed to revolutionising marine sustainability. Our cutting-edge remotely operated vehicles (ROVs) are equipped with an array of multi-tool capabilities, open-source options, and plug-and-play instruments, empowering us to tackle critical challenges in the marine environment.
 What Sets Us Apart 
 Environmental Stewardship: We are on a mission to protect our oceans and marine ecosystems. Our underwater drones specialise in marine debris collection, invasive species control, and ship hull inspection, promoting cleaner, healthier oceans.
 Innovation at its Core: We thrive on innovation, continually expanding our underwater drone functionalities. Our open-source approach allows us to adapt and add tools like ping sonar, spear guns for sustainable fishing, rake, ghost net cutters, heavy lift bags, and can/bottle collectors, making us a versatile solution provider.
 Technology for Good: We harness the power of robotics and automation to not only enhance efficiency but also to make a positive impact. Our technology-driven solutions contribute to a sustainable and responsible use of marine resources.
 Our Vision 
We envision a world where the oceans flourish, where sustainable fishing practices are the norm, and where marine debris could be a thing of the past. We are dedicated to making this vision a reality by pushing the boundaries of what underwater drones can achieve.
 Collaborate with Us 
Join us in our journey towards a cleaner, healthier ocean. Whether you are a researcher, environmentalist, or industry partner, let's collaborate to create innovative solutions for marine sustainability.
 Connect with Us 
Connect with us on LinkedIn

Name: Robotics Inclusive
Description:
Robotics Inclusive offers a community within the robotics research, industry, and education sectors to bring together people of all backgrounds and create a space for collaboration, connection, and changemaking. 

We invite our members to help us in our core mission to support recruitment, retention and promotion in robotics.

We elevate the voices of those within the robotics community who have traditionally been marginalised.

We are a community in which everyone feels included and is able to share ideas, and be listened to. This also means that everyone takes the time to actively listen to and learn from others, especially to those with diverse life experiences.

We want everyone to feel supported, and in doing so we welcome and celebrate diversity in backgrounds, including but not limited to, gender, sexuality, neurotype, ethnicity, class, age, and ability. Many in our community exist in an intersection of diverse groups and we understand that privilege takes many forms and is often not able to be compared across groups.
We host events and hold spaces for particular groups, such as women or neurodiverse people, in order to address issues which affect specific sets of people. All are welcome to attend such events with a respectful and open mind.

We welcome the membership and participation of those who have traditionally been positioned as privileged within the robotics community, in an effort to share experiences and work together to make the world of robotics a more inclusive space.

We acknowledge that we are all learning and strive to continually improve in our approach to inclusiveness. We welcome feedback and contribution from our community in achieving this goal.

Name: Thinker Inc.
Description:
To further automate human work, Thinker is working on next-generation end-effectors that use our proprietary "Pre-Touch Sensor" and "Flexible Actuator" technologies to realize a world with plenty of dexterous robots everywhere.

* A robot must be dexterous : There is a limit to the "dexterity" of robots. This is why automation sites still require a lot of human effort, such as lining up the workpieces and teaching for the robots. A robot must have the ability to sense and adjust the work situation and its changes by itself and achieve its goal with minimum teaching.

* Pre-Touch Sensor (TK-01)  : A high-performance sensor equipped with Edge AI that can measure the distance and angular orientation of an object at high speeds. It works even in camera blind spots and can measure a variety of materials such as glass and mirrors.

* Flexible Actuator : To instantly respond to an unstable and changing environment, it is essential to “handle objects like a human,“ "adjust the strength and grip with precision,“ and seamlessly combine both "flexibility and rigidity". We have two types of actuator technologies, Floating mechanism and MagLinkage.

Where our products can be used : Robot picking in these cases
①Irregular
Example: Part of an object lined up at equal intervals shifts and a bottle falls over on the bottling line
②Amorphous object
Example: Food, masterless
③Bulk pile
Example: Screw, O-ring

Finance : We are planning Series-B finance round next year.

Şirket yapımız sayesinde proje geliştirme, sponsor bulma ve ekonomi gibi alanlarda önemli deneyimler kazanıyor, geleceğe yönelik becerilerimizi geliştiriyoruz.


As Laissez Faire, we are an independent FRC team that comes together with students from many different high schools and the support of our volunteer mentors. We are preparing for our 6th season in the tournament this year with our team, which was founded in July 2018. Our name, which means "Let Them Do It", reflects our independent and creative structure. We progress regularly and effectively with our own company structure, without being affiliated with any institution or school.

With our priority of providing equal opportunities, we work so that every child can realize their dreams, and we bring the FIRST and FRC culture to wider audiences. With our members coming from different schools, we make presentations in schools, work in collaboration with teachers and inspire communities. Thanks to our company structure, we gain important experiences in areas such as project development, finding sponsors and economy, and develop our skills for the future.

Name: FIRST Robotics Team 1778 - Chill Out
Description:
Team 1778 Chill Out: Inspiring STEM Leaders

Team 1778, known as Chill Out, is a student-led robotics team from Mountlake Terrace High School, competing in the FIRST Robotics Competition (FRC) in the Pacific Northwest District. Our team provides students with a dynamic, hands-on experience in STEM and business operations through the challenge of designing, building, and programming a robot for competition.

Guided by dedicated mentors and coaches, we empower students to gain real-world skills in engineering, programming, project management, and teamwork. Students take on leadership roles in technical and non-technical areas alike, including marketing, fundraising, and community outreach, preparing them for future careers in STEM and beyond.

Our mission is to foster curiosity, innovation, and resilience, embodying FIRST’s core values of Gracious Professionalism and Coopertition. Beyond building robots, we emphasize the importance of collaboration, integrity, and service, creating an inclusive space where students are encouraged to push their boundaries, take risks, and develop a growth mindset.

Each season, our team takes on the challenge of building a competitive robot within a strict timeline, competing alongside hundreds of other teams in events across the Pacific Northwest. ll team si compone sia di menti giovani che di figure senior esperte, tutte accomunate da una caratteristica: essere NERD.
Robotic’s Revolution è il nome commerciale ma il nome della società è LBN: La Banda dei Nerds!
Ognuno presenta le sue peculiarità, i suoi punti forza e quelli di debolezza ma il team, nella sua integrità e completezza, si impegna a soddisfare tutte le richieste del cliente ed accettare ogni sfida senza paura.

Amacımız, gençleri Bilim, Teknoloji, Mühendislik ve Matematik (STEM) alanlarındaki fırsatlar ve ödüller konusunda heyecanlandırmak, özgüvenlerini artırmak ve yaşam becerilerini geliştirmektir. Takım öğrenciler tarafından yönlendirilir ve öğrenci odaklıdır; deneyimli mentörlerimiz ise destek, teşvik, yönlendirme ve eğitim sağlar.

Welcome to CybaNorth! We are a dynamic team of around 15 passionate students from the University of Groningen, Hanzehogeschool, and UMCG in the Netherlands. United by our shared enthusiasm for technology, we are committed to driving impactful advancements in prosthetic innovation.

Our mission at CybaNorth is clear and ambitious: to empower lives through the development of cutting-edge and affordable prosthetic technology. Embracing diversity and interdisciplinary collaboration, we bring together students from various fields such as Biomedical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Human Movement Science, and Physics. We believe in the transformative power of co-design, harnessing the unique perspectives and expertise of each team member.

For those unfamiliar with Cybathlon, it is a pioneering competition that showcases the incredible potential of human-machine collaboration. Participants with amputations or congenital malformations take on tasks reflecting daily life challenges, pushing the boundaries of prosthetic technology and highlighting human resilience.

If our mission resonates with you and you are interested in joining our team, supporting our cause, or learning more about what we do, please do not hesitate to reach out to us (cybanorth@rug.nl). We look forward to connecting with you!

Nuestras Áreas de Especialización:

Automatización Industrial: Implementación de sistemas automatizados para optimizar procesos industriales.

Programación de PLC y HMI: Desarrollo y optimización de programas para controladores lógicos programables (PLC) e interfaces hombre-máquina (HMI) de marcas líderes como Siemens, Omron y PLCs basados en Codesys.

Programación de Robots Industriales: Configuración y programación de robots de ABB, Fanuc, Universal Robots y otras marcas para diversas aplicaciones industriales.

Modificación y Mejoras de Programas: Ajuste y actualización de programas de PLC y robots para optimizar la productividad o añadir nuevos formatos y funcionalidades.

Documentación Eléctrica en Eplan: Creación y edición de esquemas eléctricos detallados y conformes con las normas industriales.
Construcción de Armarios Eléctricos: Diseño, montaje y cableado de armarios eléctricos a medida, adaptados a las necesidades específicas de cada proyecto.

Formación Especializada: Cursos y talleres en diferentes niveles para capacitar a su personal en la programación y manejo de PLC, HMI y robots.
Integración de Sistemas de Visión Artificial: Implementación de sistemas de visión artificial de Keyence para mejorar la precisión y control de calidad en los procesos industriales.

En Daxa Automatització i Control, estamos comprometidos con la excelencia y la innovación, ofreciendo soluciones que se adaptan a las necesidades específicas de cada cliente. Únase a nosotros en nuestro viaje hacia la transformación industrial.

Name: ReconCycle Project: Self-reconfiguration of a robotic workcell for the recycling of electronic waste
Description:
Reconcycle's mission is to develop innovative solutions for automation of electro-recycling. By developing a robotic system that automates disassembly processes, that are currently done manually, we strive to optimize efficiency, sustainability, and adaptability in the recycling of electronic waste. Through the use of reconfigurable hardware, computer vision, robotic sensing, intelligent control, and soft grippers, we aim to achieve adaptable disassembly. We utilize various novel technologies, including our modular robotic workcell, to implement adaptable disassembly workflows.

One specificity of electronic recycling is that usually many different models of the same type but in vastly different conditions need to be dis-assembled, for example, a wide variety of models of mobile phones, hard-drives, or GSM-amplifiers, etc. from different companies. Thus, in this domain we have to deal with large batches of the same type of device, which limits variability at least to some degree, but we are at the same time confronted with many different models within any given device-type and the fact that they are in vastly different conditions, which requires a flexible robotic system that adapts to this.

As a consequence, electronic waste recycling offers an ideal test-bed for self-reconfiguration of robotic cells as the complexity of the required changes within a given device-type is not too high.

Name: Logibot
Description:
Logibot is building the next generation of warehouse automation to solve one of logistics’ biggest challenges: the growing shortage of labor. We enable companies to deploy human-like robots in real, operating warehouses—without expensive redesigns, rigid systems, or vendor lock-in. 

Logibot adapts the best possible robot to the use case - not the way around. 

Our hardware-agnostic AI platform helps logistics and postal operators simulate, select, train, deploy, and continuously improve human-like robots for intralogistics tasks such as sorting, unloading, internal transport, and pallet handling. 

By combining high-fidelity simulation, embodied AI, and real-time monitoring, our robots learn from their environment and get better over time. Human-in-the-loop capabilities ensure operational reliability from day one, even in complex or dynamic scenarios.

Logibot is designed specifically for space-constrained, high-variability logistics environments where traditional automation struggles. Our vendor-neutral approach allows customers to choose the best-fit robot hardware while avoiding long-term lock-in. With a flexible SaaS + Robot-as-a-Service model, we make advanced automation accessible without heavy upfront investment.

We work closely with postal operators, CEP companies, 3PLs, and industrial players across Europe to turn labor-intensive warehouse work into scalable, resilient, and future-proof operations.

Logibot — Human-like robots, built for real warehouses.

Name: GoMyRobot
Description: GoMyRobot is pioneering the era of Physical Intelligence by building the foundational "brain" and architectural "nervous system" that allow robots to transcend pre-programmed commands and achieve true autonomous generalization. Our flagship π-Zero Foundation Model utilizes Vision-Language-Action (VLA) architectures to enable real-time action generation at 30Hz with zero-shot transfer across robot platforms. This intelligence is supported by a rugged, high-performance stack built on ROS2 kilted, custom Embedded Linux with RT-PREEMPT (Real-Time Linux), and custom RISC-V hardware acceleration for energy-efficient neural network inference at the edge. By combining Deep Reinforcement Learning (PPO, SAC, TD3) with advanced Sim-to-Real pipelines and domain randomization, we provide an end-to-end platform for dexterous manipulation, autonomous navigation, and 3D perception. At GoMyRobot, we are leveraging open standards and safety-by-design to create robots that don't just execute instructions they learn, adapt, and evolve through real-world interaction.

We provide observability and operational tooling for robotics systems by bringing together infrastructure metrics, ROS2 insights, and real-time task tracking into a single view.

Ferronyx helps teams understand what their robots are doing, why failures happen, and how to diagnose and fix issues safely. Our platform includes context-aware alerts, diagnostic and healing runbooks, AI-assisted debugging, secure remote SSH access, and deployment tracking to correlate changes with failures.

Ferronyx is designed for robotics teams running robots beyond the lab where reliability, uptime, and fast recovery matter.

Name: Zostera-Marina
Description:
Zostera-Marina is a start-up company founded by inventor Morten Thomsen - The company vision is to use automation and mechanical technology to help our oceans to regain its lost areas of eelgrass (Zostera Marina). M. Thomsen has developed at new method/system and technology that allow the replanting proces to be automated for large scale under water planting.
The new method is designed to carry and handle both eelgrass plants and seed. The method is patent pending and called a CARIPS wich is the aconym for Cover Anchor Root Insertion & Protection System. By using the CARIP-System for transplanting eelgrass combined with upscaling the mechanical technology, will allow a special designed vessel to automatically plant both seedlings and seeds with a planting capacity of 100 hectars per year at a water depth down to 12 meters.
The method and technology has been testet and shows that the plant has a positive survival and growth rate up to five times compared to existing transplanting methods.

Description:
The company Compas has been the main supplier of large industrial works for a long time, but our know-how has always been based on the perfect knowledge of automation of manufacturing processes. In order to satisfy the needs of our customers for complete industrial works, we opened our own department of mechanical construction at the beginning of the year 2018 and this year a new company Compas robotika has been founded. The new company is a part of Compas family and offers its own construction and production capacities. We focus on manufacturing and delivery of robotic workstations or single-purpose “turnkey” machines. At the moment, we can offer a complete package of services ranging from conceptual study and simulation of robotic workstations to detailed development of construction solutions and launch and installation of new machinery including technical support. We use the power of robots in manufacturing processes and therefore we aim to design such projects where human labour could be replaced by robots and where the common mechanization is no longer possible. Our target is to reduce human labour costs or to eliminate manual activities, which affect health and safety of employees.

Our clients are mainly from automotive, pharmaceutical and food industry. We cooperate with leading robot manufacturers, whose products are implemented in the production processes of our customers.

The use of simulation tools for feasibility studies of robotic workstations is also common since our target is to offer the best solution to our customers whenever they request a modification in their manufacturing process. When proposing our solutions, we use the newest technologies available on the market and in many cases we are among the first in the Czech Republic who applies them. We have been cooperating with trusted suppliers of special technologies for a long time, which ensures reliability of the whole delivery.

Name: HB Nexus
Description:
HB Nexus is an optimum and professional solution for the interior washing and disinfection of refrigerated trailers and shipping containers. This innovative automatic machine ensures controlled disinfection thanks to the rigorous application of the disinfectant product in line with concentration, quantity and reaction time requirements for each transport fleet. A powerful high-pressure system allows the equipment to thoroughly clean the interior of up to 16 metres long trailers and up to 45'ft containers, washing away all the dirt. 

Developed and patented by HB Group the HB Nexus is the ideal system for a safe and effective interior wash and disinfection of refrigerated trailers and shipping containers. 60% cleaning time reduction versus manual wash processes, better control of chemical products, top performance and a sturdy design are some of the defining features of this model. The equipment consists of a fixed base, with an elevator platform or with lateral movement as an option, and can be installed outdoors or on a wash bay.

This revolutionizes the cleaning of the loading space of trucks through speed, predictability, cleanliness, sustainability and certifiability

Here are the most important facts:
- Significantly faster cleaning
- Certificate of cleanliness according to HACCP protocol
- Less water consumption
- Less chemistry
- More sustainable 
- No need for staff
- Interface with our cloud and booking system Nuvix

Name: Can we Build Baymax? Workshop
Description:
Baymax is a humanoid robot character in the Disney animation “Big Hero 6.” It is a healthcare robot with an inflatable body, capable of walking, bumping into surrounding objects, manipulating, learning new skills, talking, and physically interacting with people. In the movie, students from the Nerd Lab in the fictional San Fransokyo Institute of Technology develop many interesting technologies including Baymax. However, in the real world, it is not easy to build and teach such a robot.

As a continuation of our previous workshops, this workshop will bring together robotics researchers working robots like baymax, including HRI, sensing, compliant hardware, and internal humanoid structures.

Since our first workshop in 2015, the series has taken place at the IEEE-RAS International Conference on Humanoid Robots every year. For our 10th anniversary, the workshop will discuss how close we are to reaching our goal today, and we will organize a real-world demonstration session called BYOB (Bring Your Own Baymax), inviting researchers to participate with their own Baymax robots. We invite you to contribute and to participate in this workshop!

baymax.org

Name: Gridiron Robotics
Description:
Gridiron Robotics: Revolutionizing Package Handling Through Advanced Automation
Gridiron Robotics is a leading robotic logistics automation company specializing in the automation of inbound and outbound package handling processes. With a mission to transform the logistics industry, Gridiron Robotics leverages cutting-edge robotics, artificial intelligence (AI), and advanced data analytics to deliver innovative solutions that significantly enhance efficiency, reduce costs, and improve accuracy in package handling.
Recipients of a Phase 1 SBIR Grant, Gridiron Robotics has developed a suite of state-of-the-art technologies designed to streamline and optimize package handling operations. Their advanced robotic systems, specifically engineered for package handling tasks, are capable of picking, sorting, and transporting packages with remarkable speed and precision. These robots, equipped with sophisticated sensors and computer vision technology, can identify and track packages in real-time, ensuring each package is handled with optimal care and efficiency.
At the core of Gridiron Robotics' solutions is a powerful AI software that enables the robotic systems to learn and adapt to their environment. By constantly analyzing data from the robots' sensors, the AI algorithms identify patterns and optimize the robots' movements to maximize efficiency and minimize errors. This continuous improvement process results in increasingly streamlined package handling operations.
Gridiron Robotics also harnesses big data analytics to provide valuable insights into the performance of its robotic systems and identify areas for improvement. By analyzing key performance indicators such as package handling times and error rates, the company can fine-tune its solutions to better meet client needs and maintain a competitive edge in the rapidly evolving logistics industry.
Designed to seamlessly integrate with all existing warehouse management systems (WMS) via API.

Name: AWZ
Description:
The Air Wing Zero (AWZ) is an autonomous hydrogen-propelled octocopter engineered for single-passenger, intercity air transit. Its hybridized propulsion chain—fuel cells, capacitors, batteries—targets 1,000-mile endurance, with ultra-high reliability (≥10⁹ hours MTBF) and multilayered safety incorporating dual parachutes, ejection mechanism, ballistic shielding, and biometric oversight. Autonomous navigation integrates neural computation, stereoscopic vision, dual flight processors, and nonlinear PID-SMC controllers. Structural integrity derives from graphene nanocomposite frameworks enabling optimized strength-to-weight with modular assembly. Refueling relies on swappable hydrogen reservoirs, reducing infrastructure dependency.

Key specifications: payload 250–300 lbs, aerodynamic shrouded rotor configuration, EMI-resistant cabin, mesh networking communication, redundant control layers, and rapid egress capability.

Development trajectory:
• Phase 1 (Years 1–2): scaled demonstrator validation, control law refinement, material benchmarking, autonomy verification.
• Phase 2 (Years 3–4): full-scale integration, safety instrumentation, AI navigation trials, range optimization.
• Phase 3 (Years 5–6): certification, manufacturing expansion, operational deployment.

Differentiation: hydrogen-powered 1,000-mile operational envelope versus short-range electric eVTOLs; modular refueling architecture allowing rapid turnaround; extreme reliability metrics; protective occupant systems.

Market outlook: global aerial mobility sector projected exponential growth, with North America leading share and Asia-Pacific fastest CAGR. Strategic positioning places AWZ as a long-range, safety-first alternative to incumbent prototypes with scalability across civilian, defense, and emergency response domains.

We are on a mission to bring high-quality STEM education to classrooms in a way that’s hands-on, engaging, and future-focused.

Our core services include the setup of fully functional Robotics Labs with DIY kits and AI software, Entrepreneur Incubators for young innovators, Take-Home Robotics Kits, Drone Engineering Workshops, and Teacher Development Programs with year-long handholding support. We also provide a structured curriculum along with dedicated teacher and student resources to ensure smooth and effective delivery.

Robotify aims to make STEM education accessible and inclusive for all students especially underrepresented and curious young learners by offering affordable, value-driven programs that spark imagination and a genuine love for science and technology. We believe this spark is essential for the development and progress of our country.

Our curriculum is thoughtfully designed by engineers with hands-on industry experience in AI, robotics, and coding, and it is fully aligned with NEP 2020 guidelines. The program integrates AI learning, robotics training, coding classes, and drone engineering into the school ecosystem. All sessions are delivered by expert trainers with strong backgrounds in engineering and artificial intelligence, ensuring students receive practical, real-world knowledge from passionate educators.

For students with creative and innovative ideas, our Entrepreneur Incubator, run in collaboration with The Planning Company, offers expert mentorship and a platform to bring ideas to life.

Our vision is to empower the next generation with the skills and confidence they need to thrive in the age of AI and robotics, because the future belongs to creators, not just consumers.

Robotify – Where Imagination Meets Innovation

Name: Learning AI for DeXtrous Robots (AIDX)
Description:
We are a joint research lab of the professorship “Learning AI for Dextrous Robots” at the Technical University of Munich (TUM) and the Institute of Robotics & Mechatronics at German Aerospace Center (DLR).

Our goal is to create intelligent autonomous humanoid robots that come close to human capabilities, esp. with regard to dextrous manipulation with multi-fingered hands. We are conducting research in “Autonomously Learning AI”, i.e., deep (reinforcement) learning based on first principles and simulation models (no human demonstrations or arbitrary internet videos …) and the results are zero-shot transferred to the real system.

Our program connects basic research in artificial intelligence with highly relevant applications for society, such as industrial manufacturing (given the problem of labor shortage) or service robots as a “helper for the elderly” at home.

At our lab, we implement our methods on the award-winning mobile humanoid robot DLR Agile Justin with its torque-controlled DLR-Hands II. We also work on commercializing our ai methods and a new generation of a highly dextrous but robust robotic hand.

Name: RoboCup Singapore
Description:
RoboCup is an international scientific initiative aimed at advancing the state of the art in intelligent robotics. Established in 1997, its foundational mission was to develop a team of robots capable of defeating the human soccer World Cup champions by 2050.

RoboCup has consistently been at the forefront of robotics and AI research and education. Beyond RoboCupSoccer, the initiative includes RoboCup@Home, which focuses on robotic solutions for societal needs; RoboCupRescue, which innovates in robotic responses for search and rescue operations; RoboCupIndustrial, which establishes a framework for industry-inspired applications; and RoboCupJunior, an international educational program that has engaged thousands of students in robotics, AI, STEM, and ICT.

The RoboCup Asia-Pacific (RCAP), based in Singapore, serves as a super-regional headquarters, coordinating RoboCup activities across the Asia-Pacific region. 

The RoboCup Singapore National Committee (RoboCupSG), established in 2008, functions as a regional committee of the RoboCup Federation, coordinating RoboCup activities in Singapore and ASEAN.

Our core mission is to strengthen the defense, security, and rescue capabilities of Georgia and the wider region by engineering innovative, locally-developed technologies tailored to real-world challenges.

As the architects of "Intelligence in Motion,"  we bridge the gap between advanced academic research and practical, field-ready solutions. Our work is focused on three core R&D pillars:


Unmanned Ground Systems (UGV): Developing modular, multi-mission robotic platforms like the "AMBRY" military-rescue robot.




Autonomous Reconnaissance: Creating compact, intelligent systems like the "Mstovari" tactical robot for critical-zone operations.



Advanced Electro-Optics (EO): Engineering specialized tactical systems like the "Achrdili" (The Ghost) IR illuminator for night navigation.

KineCore is committed to creating effective, accessible, and life-saving technologies that expand operational capabilities and enhance the safety of personnel on the front line.

Name: Institute of Culinary Robotics (ICR)
Description:
The Institute of Culinary Robotics (ICR) accelerates the responsible adoption of robotics, automation, and AI in foodservice.
We unite leading universities, research institutions, technology partners, and industry operators to define what is possible, and what is safe, efficient, and future-ready for real food service environments.

ICR combines deep foodservice expertise with advanced engineering and applied research. Supported by a strong network of engineers, robotics and AI, our specialists evaluate emerging technologies, develop process-proven automation concepts, and translate operational requirements into robotics-ready workflows.

Our core work focuses on five pillars:

1. Foodservice Intelligence for Robotics
We bridge the gap between culinary operations and robotics by mapping workflows, hygiene requirements, sensing needs, and real-world use cases.

2. Real-World Technology Testing & Future-Readiness
We test and benchmark current and emerging robotic systems, assessing what is ready today, and what will shape tomorrow.

3. Automation Concepts Built for Food Services
We design process-proven automation strategies and define the robotic behaviors required for safe, efficient foodservice integration.

4. Safety & Hygiene Requirements
We bring foodservice-specific safety, hygiene, and certification needs into the robotics domain to ensure practical, compliant implementation.

5. Ecosystem Guidance & Scalable Adoption
We align pilots, partners, and industry collaborations to build the frameworks, standards, and knowledge required for scalable automation.

With robotics at the center, ICR transforms complexity into clarity, connecting science, engineering, and culinary operations to deliver intelligent, sustainable solutions that make foodservice smarter, safer, and more resilient.
⠀
The Institute of Culinary Robotics (ICR) is a brand and initiative operated by ICA Laboratories GmbH.

Name: Apolo 27 GT
Description:
Apolo 27 GT es el equipo universitario dominicano que está dando el salto de la NASA HERC a Formula Student Spain, llevando la ingeniería local a uno de los escenarios estudiantiles de automovilismo más exigentes del mundo. Somos un grupo multidisciplinario de jóvenes que diseña, construye y gestiona un monoplaza tipo fórmula con estándares internacionales, integrando mecánica, electrónica, materiales, gestión de proyectos y negocios. 

Nacimos en 2019 construyendo rovers para el NASA Human Exploration Rover Challenge en Huntsville, Alabama, experiencia que nos formó en creatividad, resiliencia y trabajo en equipo, y que nos convirtió en un referente de orgullo nacional e inspiración para la juventud dominicana. Hoy, esa trayectoria evoluciona en Apolo 27 GT, con el objetivo de competir en el Circuit Barcelona-Catalunya y consolidarnos como primer equipo del Caribe en la Formula Student Spain. 

Nuestra misión es inspirar vocaciones STEM con impacto real, conectando a estudiantes, universidades e industria mediante proyectos de alto rendimiento tecnológico. Las empresas que se unen a Apolo 27 GT acceden a talento preparado, soluciones de ingeniería aplicadas, visibilidad nacional e internacional y un impacto social medible a través de programas de divulgación y mentoring para jóvenes. 

Si compartes nuestra visión de un futuro más innovador para la República Dominicana y la región, te invitamos a seguir nuestra historia y construir con nosotros el próximo capítulo de la ingeniería dominicana.

Name: ROBIN Education For Kids
Description:
Robin education for kids
We use the latest developments in our approach to teach children and teens. The project is unique as it сombines such diverse areas as Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM). 
Our services and products give children and teens such opportunities as:

1. STEM Skills Development: Robotics and electronics education promotes the development of science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) skills. These skills include logical reasoning, problem solving, analytical skills, and creative thinking, which can be useful in future career choices or further education.

2. Practical application of knowledge: Robotics and electronics classes give children and teens an opportunity to put their knowledge into practice. They can create and program robots, build electronic devices, and see their ideas turn into reality. This helps students grasp information much better than just by learning theory and strengthens their understanding of the concepts and principles of science and technology.

3. Developing communication and collaborative skills: Working in robotics and electronics projects often requires team collaboration. Students learn to communicate, share ideas, solve conflicts, and work as a group to achieve a common goal. This develops their communication and collaborative skills, which are important in almost any area of life. Assembling a robot brings a family together as a team helping them spend more time together doing fun activities. 

4. Stimulating creativity and innovation: Robotics and electronics provide children and teens with opportunities to be creative and innovative. They can experiment with different designs, functionality and software to create unique solutions and projects. This develops their ability to think creatively, find new approaches to problems, and stimulate an interest in innovation.

Name: Flapping Wing MAV Project
Description:
We are a student-led research team of 16 aiming to develop bio-mimic flapping wing drones to achieve better agility and power efficiency.

Our first prototype is of 30g weight class and 30cm wing span micro air vehicle (MAV). 
The work we have done/been doing:
• Writing MATLAB script with quasi-steady aerodynamic modeling of the flapping lift and drag of one cycle.
• Designing and optimizing the mechanical linkage and mechanism to transmit rotary motion of the motor to flapping motion.
• Conceiving a conceptual design of and prototyping the bio-inspired flight stability and pitch control abdomen joint.
• Developing the manufacturing techniques for ultra-lightweight wing and stiffener complex and designing a test stand for rapid testing of the wings.
• Setting up simulation environment in Webot and developing Simulink model for control.

Description:
Hos Dansk Robot har vi arbejdet med servicerobotter siden 2017, hvor vi introduceret de første modeller til det danske marked. 

Vi specialister i implementering af avancerede robotter og har idag mere end 160 robotter, der skaber effektivitet og driftssikkerhed for virksomheder, offentlige institutioner og kommuner.

Vi sikrer en fuldt integreret løsning fra den første besigtigelse til den daglige drift. Robotten bliver opsat og idriftsat, medarbejderne får en grundig introduktion, og vi tilbyder løbende support og optimering for at sikre stabil og effektiv rengøring. 

Vores tilgang gør det nemt at implementere og vedligeholde en driftssikker robotløsning, der skaber værdi fra dag ét. Vi samarbejder med faste servicepartnere over hele Danmark, hvilket sikrer hurtig support og landsdækkende service.

Vores robotter er allerede i drift hos kunder som Danish Crown, Københavns Lufthavn, flere Danske kommuner, Arla, LEGOLAND Hotel & Konference, skoler, universiteter, plejehjem, Rema 1000, Hilton Hotel på Færøerne, Hotel Bella Sky og mange flere.

Med vores erfaring og gennemprøvede løsninger sikrer vi, at automatisering af  f.eks. Receptions opgaver og rengøring og  bliver en værdiskabende investering – både i forhold til arbejdsmiljø, økonomi og bæredygtighed.

Name: 4MySmartHome – Dé Expert in Robotoplossingen
Description:

Wij leveren niet alleen robots, maar bieden een volledig pakket aan diensten:

Advies op maat: Wij analyseren de behoeften van elke klant en bieden oplossingen die perfect aansluiten op hun situatie.
Installatie en training: Onze experts zorgen voor een professionele installatie en trainen uw team om optimaal gebruik te maken van de robots.
Onderhoud en ondersteuning: Wij bieden onderhoudscontracten en staan klaar voor snelle ondersteuning, zodat uw robots altijd blijven presteren.
Consultancy: Met onze expertise helpen we bedrijven bij het integreren van automatisering in hun dagelijkse processen.
Onze robots worden succesvol ingezet in diverse sectoren:

Sportscholen: Voor schone vloeren en kleedkamers.
Horeca: Automatische schoonmaak van restaurants en keukens.
Kantoren: Voor een frisse en hygiënische werkplek.
Fysiotherapiepraktijken: Hygiëne in behandelruimtes.
Campings: Schoonmaak van sanitaire voorzieningen en gemeenschappelijke ruimtes.
Showrooms: Glanzende vloeren en een professionele uitstraling.
Wat ons onderscheidt?
Wij combineren meer dan een decennium ervaring in robots met een praktische en klantgerichte aanpak. Onze oplossingen besparen tijd, verlagen kosten en maken dagelijkse processen eenvoudiger. Van advies tot onderhoud – wij zorgen voor een naadloze implementatie en langdurige ondersteuning.

Bij 4MySmartHome draait het om meer dan alleen robots: wij maken technologie toegankelijk en praktisch, zodat mensen zich kunnen richten op wat écht belangrijk is.

Vraag een gratis demo aan en ontdek hoe onze robotoplossingen uw bedrijf kunnen optimaliseren!

Name: CORD-E Robotics
Description:
Young robotics innovators often face a significant challenge: finding the right components at the right time to bring their groundbreaking ideas to life. This struggle is not just about access; it’s about losing the momentum of creativity and passion. Witnessing this firsthand and inspired by the trust people placed in me during urgent times for robotics components, I founded CORD-E—a platform designed to change the game for robotics enthusiasts.

At CORD-E, our mission is clear: to empower innovators by providing high-quality robotics components and equipment exactly when they need them. More than just a business, CORD-E is a vision—a place where the thrill and excitement of creating something extraordinary is shared and celebrated. Robotics is not just about machines; it’s about imagination, problem-solving, and the joy of bringing ideas to reality. This belief fuels everything we do.

The heart of CORD-E lies in its commitment to supporting innovators—whether they are students, hobbyists, or aspiring professionals—in achieving their vision of innovation. We prioritize quality and reliability, ensuring that every component we provide meets the standards required for success. Equally important is our focus on timing; we understand that innovation cannot wait, and our goal is to deliver components swiftly and efficiently to keep your projects on track.

CORD-E stands as a bridge between the dreamers and their dreams, offering not just products but also a community that values curiosity and ingenuity. Together, we’re shaping a future where robotics is accessible, exciting, and within reach for everyone who dares to imagine.

Name: Ars Vallis Ingenium Robotics
Description:
Introducing the Valley of Artistic Ingenuity, an association of students from all over STEAM, humanities, and social sciences who blend their interests and talents in developing industry-level solutions. Through training members in new concepts and skills, utilizing small group projects, and engaging with our community, Ars Vallis Ingenium Robotics enables students to develop real world aptitudes not found in traditional academic settings.

AVI Robotics takes inspiration from previously participating in the Marine Advanced Technology Education (MATE) Remotely Operated Vehicle (ROV) competition as a part of Engineering Renaissance/Crush Depth at Clovis Community College. Following this legacy, AVI Robotics plans to compete in the MATE ROV 2026 competition held in Newfoundland, Canada in June.

The Fall 2025 semester will consist of instruction and projects in order to build technical development abilities, documentation and marketing methods, while always harkening back to teamwork and communication.

Successful competition in MATE ROV requires the structure of multiple departments that requires many different types of majors and people. These include physical design & manufacturing, programming & electronics, plus marketing & logistics. Underpinning the whole competition is a focus on ethics and safety for participants and the environment.

Stimulated by this broad requirement of talents, Ars Vallis Robotics is made up of and welcomes students from all majors. This includes engineering, computer science, business, digital design, English, and many more. No matter your major, you and your talents have value here!

Despite Ars Vallis Robotics focusing on underwater robotics, we strive to explore and support all kinds of ventures, robotics or otherwise.

Description:
Robonest – AI-Powered Robotics for Smarter Poultry Farming
Robonest is an innovative agri-tech company developing AI-powered autonomous robotic systems designed to transform poultry farming. Our mission is to enhance animal health, optimize farm operations, and reduce production costs through cutting-edge robotics, artificial intelligence, and real-time data analytics.

At the core of Robonest is an autonomous robot equipped with advanced computer vision (YOLO), sound analysis (CRNN), and environmental sensors. The system continuously monitors poultry health, detects early signs of disease, tracks equipment performance, and optimizes environmental conditions — acting like both a skilled farmworker and a veterinarian.

Unlike traditional automation systems, Robonest offers real-time, mobile monitoring inside poultry houses, providing farmers with instant insights, predictive maintenance alerts, and AI-driven recommendations via an intuitive digital dashboard.

Key Features:
 Early Disease Detection (90%+ AI accuracy)
 Real-Time Equipment & Climate Monitoring
 Labor Cost Reduction – 24/7 Autonomous Operation
 AI-Driven Predictive Analytics & Reporting
 Seamless Integration with Existing Farm Management Systems

Our Vision:
To become a global leader in precision livestock farming, enabling poultry producers worldwide to improve animal welfare, reduce costs, and maximize productivity through AI and robotics.

Robonest is committed to scaling operations into the European and global markets, driving the future of smart farming.

Name: Collaborative Robotics Lab
Description: Welcome to the Collaborative Robotics Lab (Collab) at Virginia Tech! We create learning and control algorithms for robots that collaborate with people. Our goal is to develop intelligent robots that understand — and are understood by — their human partners. These robots personalize their behavior: continually adapting to what the human wants and proactively helping them to achieve their goals. Overall, our research explores the intersection of human-robot interaction, machine learning, and control theory, with applications in personal, interactive, and assistive robots.

Name: RoboServer Corporation Pty Limited
Description:
The inspiration behind starting RoboServer Corporation, a service robot business, stemmed from a combination of factors and experiences. Over the years, I observed the rapid advancements in artificial intelligence and robotics, witnessing how these technologies have the potential to revolutionize various industries, including hospitality and customer service.
Additionally, I recognized the increasing demand for automation and enhanced services in our modern society. People are seeking efficient and personalized experiences, and businesses are constantly striving to meet these evolving expectations. It became clear to me that service robots could offer a valuable solution, bridging the gap between technological innovation and human-centric service.
Furthermore, I noticed the challenges faced by businesses in optimizing their operations, particularly in labor-intensive sectors like the food and beverage industry. Staffing, training, and operational costs often pose significant obstacles. By introducing service robots, I envisioned a way to streamline processes, reduce costs, and enhance overall efficiency while maintaining a high level of customer satisfaction.

Driven by a passion for innovation and a desire to create positive change, I embarked on the journey of establishing RoboServer Corporation. Our goal is to provide businesses with cutting-edge service robot solutions that not only address their pain points but also elevate their service standards, revolutionizing the way they interact with customers and optimize their operations.
Through the relentless pursuit of excellence, continuous research and development, and a customer-centric approach, we strive to be at the forefront of the service robot industry, empowering businesses to embrace the future of automation and enhanced services.
In essence, the inspiration to start RoboServer Corporation stemmed from the transformative potential of service robots to meet the evolving needs of businesses and consumer
Location: Sydney Olympic Park, New South Wales

Chase Robotics is pioneering a new era of intelligent construction oversight.

We believe the future of construction will be defined by those who deeply understand the field — and build technology that respects it.
At Chase Robotics, we are shaping that future with discretion, discipline, and a relentless commitment to innovation.

Name: Underwater Remotely Operated Vehicles (UWROV)
Description:
UWROV was founded over 10 years ago by UW Oceanography students interested in ocean technology and has continued to expand its scope to other engineering and marine science disciplines ever since. Our team has competed in the MATE ROV Competition since 2015, as well as supporting regional events through volunteering and mentorship.

UWROV members served as key volunteers during the 2018 MATE International ROV Competition held in Federal Way, WA, and returned to take 6th place at the 2021 MATE World Championships.

In 2022, we took 2nd place at the MATE World Championships and took 1st place for our submission to the NOAA Ocean Exploration Video / Machine Learning Challenge!

Most recently, we placed 9th at the 2023 MATE World Championships overall, 1st in Technical Documentation, and 2nd in the NOAA Ocean Exploration Video / Machine Learning Challenge! We strive to continue giving UW students hands-on experience in ocean technology, interdisciplinary engineering, and professional skills.

Name: Artemis 3083

With a team composed of approximately 20 talented and motivated students, Artemis 3083 is committed to making a positive impact on their community and beyond.

At Artemis 3083, our mission is clear: to inspire and nurture the next generation of innovators, problem solvers, and leaders. We firmly believe in the power of hands-on learning and the endless possibilities that STEM education can offer. By combining the principles of FIRST and our own unique approach, we strive to create an inclusive and supportive environment where students can explore their interests, develop valuable skills, and grow both personally and academically.

As a team, we are driven by the core values of FIRST. We embrace the spirit of discovery, innovation, and impact as we continuously push the boundaries of our knowledge and abilities. Inclusion and teamwork are fundamental to our culture, as we respect and value the diverse perspectives and contributions of each team member. Welcome to Xplora — The Ultimate Celebration of Innovation! 

Get ready to dive into the world of technology, creativity, and limitless imagination at Xplora, the grand Techfest hosted by Krishna Public School, Raipur, in collaboration with ATL and the Coders Club.

Xplora isn’t just an event — it’s an experience. Over two electrifying days, the brightest minds will come together to innovate, compete, and explore through a series of tech-driven competitions, workshops, and interactive showcases. From the thrill of Robo Soccer to the strategy of Gamers’ League, from AI Talks that spark ideas to Virtual Reality adventures that redefine boundaries — Xplora is where technology meets imagination.

At its core, Xplora is about inspiring curiosity, fueling innovation, and celebrating the spirit of discovery. Whether you’re a coder, gamer, creator, or dreamer, this is your stage to shine, learn, and lead the future of tech.

Step into Xplora — where ideas spark, innovations rise, and the future begins!

Name: Jona Engineering
Description:
Jona.AI: Bring AI Power to Your Machines

Jona.AI equips engineers with compact, understandable AI models that run directly on machines (edge AI).  We analyze data from any sensor to improve:

- Industrial Control Systems: Optimize and automate machines with real-time, on-device decisions.
- Quality Control: Integrate fast, accurate defect detection using any sensor data (cameras, temperature, vibration, etc.).

Benefits:

- Faster Decisions: Make real-time choices on your machines, without relying on the cloud.
- Improved Efficiency: Lightweight models use fewer resources.
- Clear Understanding: See exactly how the AI arrives at its conclusions.

Name: Stellaris Robotics Pte Ltd

Name: Centre for Industrial Automation
Description:
The Center of Excellence for Industrial Automation was established to provide training in industrial robotics and automation. 

In 2017, the centre expanded with the addition of an Industrial Automation facility in collaboration with Tianjin University, China, and Mitsubishi Robots. This facility includes four industrial-grade Mitsubishi robots and automation infrastructure for practical learning in automation, PLC programming, and more.

Training here is split into two levels:

The initial phase covers sensors, actuators, and interfacing techniques using kits like Lego Mindstorm and C6 Creative Bricks.
The second phase involves working with industrial-grade automation systems, including the Mitsubishi Melfa RV4FLD robotic arm and PLCs, offering students exposure to complex automation setups.
The centre also provides consultancy services, attracting attention to industrial solutions and product development. Students from the centre have achieved national and international recognition in skill competitions, gaining global attention.

Our NextBrainTM AI system is integrated with robot brain cognition, AI neural sensing (of force, touch, vision and hearing), intelligent path planning, and intelligent process recipe generation. Name: Estella Humanoids
Description:
Estella is an open-source humanoid robot dedicated to building a lifelike anatomical replicant with expressive design and human-centered interaction. The project currently includes detailed designs for the spine, face, shoulders, and hands, forming the foundation for a modular and extensible humanoid platform.
The idea of Estella was introduced in 2016 by Prof. Behnam Rahnama at the Robotics Department of Medis Startup Studio. Since then, Estella has been shaped through the joint efforts of Prof. Rahnama and Negin (Fatemeh) Heiran, with valuable support from colleagues who contributed to different stages of its development. Together, the team worked on core elements such as the humanoid face, spine, shoulders, and hands, building Estella into a distinctive research prototype.
For us, Estella has been more than just a project; she has been a teacher. Through Estella, we explored robotics, innovation, design, and many of the skills that shaped our path. That is why we believe Estella can now play the same role for researchers, engineers, and robotics enthusiasts around the world.
Now, Estella’s journey continues as an independent open-source project, guided by Negin Heiran and other enthusiastic contributors into a new chapter. This transition honors the collaborative foundation laid at Medis while opening the door for the global community to contribute, expand, and redefine what humanoid robots can become, a true mirror of us.

Description: The DN-ID on Social aWareness for sErvicE roboTs aims at training a new generation of research and professional experts able to advance the development of robots capable of perceiving, interpreting, and appropriately responding to humans. Doctoral Candidates (DCs) will be immersed in cutting-edge research and innovation with a training program that will offer an integrated curriculum, encompassing theoretical knowledge and real-world application scenarios. The network’s interdisciplinary approach includes various fields, such as artificial intelligence, machine learning, human-robot interaction, computer vision, psychology, and cognitive sciences. A Scenario-Based Learning approach will provide co-working activities where collaborative design and implementation are fostered. The scenarios considered include social awareness in working environments, in private environments, and in public places. DCs will participate in a unique training program for continuous professional development of their soft and research skills. The inter-sectoral collaboration between academia and industry will further strengthen the novelty and impact of the research and training by ensuring that relevant users’ needs are addressed and that research results are technically, economically, and socially feasible.

Name: SVD Robotics
Description:

• Ingénierie et simulation robotique
De la coordination de systèmes multi-robots à l’optimisation des trajectoires d’outil, en passant par la résolution automatique des problèmes d’accessibilité et de collision, SVD Robotics utilise des techniques de pointe pour automatiser la programmation des robots et faciliter le déploiement de projets robotiques.

• Développement de procédés robotisés
Avec une expertise couvrant des champs de compétences complémentaires telles que la création de jumeaux numériques, l’intégration de capteurs ou encore la programmation de trajectoires sur des modèles CAO et des scans 3D, SVD Robotics accompagne les industriels pour développer des solutions robotiques particulièrement adaptées au traitement de petites séries, de pièces complexes ou de produits variables.

• Interfaces de pilotage sur-mesure
SVD Robotics propose des interfaces de pilotage sur-mesure pour créer des systèmes de production flexibles qui s’adaptent à la variabilité de la production. Configurées en fonction des paramètres métiers, ces IHM intuitives et no-code permettent aux opérateurs d’adapter très rapidement le procédé à la production de nouvelles pièces.

Name: SIRo Lab
Description: The Soft Interactive Robotics Laboratory (SIRo Lab) at Berliner Hochschule für Technik (BHT) specializes in designing and developing interactive soft robotic systems, with particular emphasis on variable-stiffness robotic hands and, ultimately, an integrated soft robotic hand-arm system. Embedded within BHT’s Bachelor's program in Humanoid Robotics, the lab's research activities are currently supported by two prominent DFG-funded initiatives: the "tele.interaction" project (part of the DFG GGA HAW 2023), focusing on advanced humanoid robot teleoperation using a TIAGo robot from PAL Robotics and a dedicated visuo-haptic input station, and the "APPL-FM" project (within the DFG's Forschungsimpulse für HAWs framework), exploring the application of foundational AI models across various domains including robotics. The laboratory presently comprises five members.

Name: Loop Robotics Technologies
Description:
Loop Robotics is revolutionizing industrial automation by providing autonomous mobile robots (AMRs) designed to function as AI-powered employees operating around the clock.

Vision

The company aims to transform perceptions of AMR robots by developing modular systems that integrate seamlessly across various industries and environments.

Product Lineup

Loop Robotics offers a diverse range of AMRs tailored to specific operational needs:
	•	Loop Fork 500: Automates point-to-point material movement within controlled environments without human intervention.
	•	Loop Lift 500: Designed for autonomous lifting and transporting tasks, enhancing efficiency in material handling.
	•	Loop Tug 500: Capable of moving carts and carrying payloads autonomously, streamlining internal logistics.
	•	Loop Arm 500: Equipped with pick-and-place capabilities and QR code scanning, facilitating precise item handling.
	•	Loop UV 500: Provides 360-degree effective disinfection autonomously, ensuring sanitary conditions without human assistance.
	•	Loop Defense AMR: Engineered for defense applications, capable of traversing various terrains with multi-weapon attachments and inbuilt machine vision, operating with or without human intervention.

Key Features

Loop Robotics’ AMRs are distinguished by:
	•	Quick and Easy Deployment: Robots can be operational within days, minimizing downtime during implementation.
	•	Multipurpose Systems: Designed for versatility across different tasks and industries.
	•	Flexible and Scalable Solutions: Easily adaptable to changing operational needs and scalable for growing demands.
	•	Collaborative Operation: Engineered to work safely alongside human workers, enhancing overall productivity.

Name: GPSIC - Grupo de Pesquisa em Simulação Computacional
Description:
O GPSlC tem como principal objetivo explorar e aprimorar a capacidade de modelagem e análise de sistemas complexos por meio de simulações realizadas em ambiente computacional. Este campo interdisciplinar emprega técnicas avançadas de computação para criar modelos virtuais que replicam o comportamento de sistemas reais, permitindo a observação e análise de fenômenos complexos em um ambiente controlado.
O propósito fundamental desse grupo é contribuir para o avanço do conhecimento científico e tecnológico, aplicando métodos computacionais inovadores na simulação de processos físicos, biológicos, sociais ou econômicos. A simulação computacional oferece uma abordagem poderosa para estudar sistemas que podem ser difíceis ou impraticáveis de analisar diretamente devido à sua complexidade, escala ou custo.
Em resumo, o objetivo central desse grupo é impulsionar o avanço da ciência e da tecnologia por meio da aplicação de simulações computacionais, contribuindo para a compreensão mais aprofundada de sistemas complexos e proporcionando insights valiosos para diversas áreas do conhecimento.
