Description:
Komunitas Robotic Electrical Engineering (KROENG) adalah sebuah komunitas di Universitas Syiah Kuala yang memfokuskan diri pada pengembangan potensi mahasiswa dalam bidang Robotika dan IoT. Visi KROENG adalah menjadi wadah pengembangan potensi mahasiswa di bidang Robotika dan IoT yang relevant di industry, sedangkan misi KROENG adalah meningkatkan kualitas dan kemampuan mahasiswa dalam bidang Robotika dan IoT melalui kegiatan dan program yang terstruktur.

Tujuan KROENG adalah memperluas wawasan dan pengetahuan mahasiswa dalam bidang Robotika dan IoT, meningkatkan kreativitas dan inovasi, serta memperkuat relasi antar mahasiswa dan industri. KROENG mengadakan workshop, pelatihan, lomba, dan mengikuti perlombaan untuk meningkatkan kualitas mahasiswa di bidang Robotika dan IoT. KROENG ditujukan untuk mahasiswa Teknik Elektro USK yang memiliki minat di bidang Robotika dan IoT.

Jika Anda ingin bergabung atau mendapatkan informasi lebih lanjut tentang KROENG, Anda dapat menghubungi email kroengusk@gmail.com atau mengunjungi akun Instagram @kroengid. KROENG selalu terbuka untuk kerjasama dan donasi untuk mendukung pengembangan mahasiswa di bidang Robotika dan IoT.

Name: Robotriks
Description:
Robotriks Ltd is a robotics R&D company based in Par, Cornwall, UK, specialising in the design and manufacture of affordable, autonomous robotic platforms for real-world use. Founded in 2018, we set out to break down the financial and technical barriers to automation in agriculture and other industries.

Our mission is simple: to make advanced robotics accessible, sustainable, and practical for the people who need them most. We believe that high-performance technology doesn’t need to come with a high-end price tag, and we prove that every day in the field.

Our flagship platform is the Robotic Traction Unit (RTU) a modular, all-terrain, autonomous vehicle designed for a variety of agricultural tasks. With interchangeable attachments, robust connectivity, and real-time onboard processing, the RTU brings intelligent automation to farms, estates, and research environments without the usual cost or complexity.

From autonomous weeding and precision planting to soil compaction testing and infiltration analysis, our solutions are developed with flexibility, scalability, and environmental impact in mind. The RTU is fully electric and engineered to reduce soil damage, chemical usage, and emissions, helping to build a more sustainable future for farming.

We partner with local authorities, researchers, and innovators across the UK and beyond to develop systems that solve pressing challenges in agriculture and land management.

If you're looking for robotic systems that are reliable, field-tested, and rooted in real-world needs, we're ready to talk

Name: Wychar Labs
Description:
At Wychar Labs, our team of passionate engineers is driven by a shared desire to make a significant impact through innovative robotics solutions. We're constantly pushing the boundaries to create technologies that not only advance the field but also make real-world applications more accessible and practical. Here are some of the exciting projects we're currently working on:

1. Robotics Platform for Education and Research :  We're developing a versatile robotics platforms designed specifically for educational purposes, research, and entrepreneurs. These platforms serve as a foundation for others to build upon, enabling the creation of diverse and complex robotics applications. Our flagship project introduces a highly versatile quadruped robotic platform with dual arms, designed to navigate challenging terrains and adapt to dynamic environments. 

2. Robotics App Store : Our Robotics App Store is set to revolutionize the way developers and domain experts interact with robotics. By providing standardized high-level APIs, we facilitate the development of domain-specific applications that can seamlessly integrate with existing hardware platforms. This initiative aims to democratize access to advanced robotics technology.

3. Robotics for Challenging Tasks :  We're leveraging robotics to tackle tasks that are either too difficult, risky, or impractical for humans to perform. This includes critical operations like fuel loading in forests, bomb disposal, and navigating hazardous environments to carry out manipulation tasks. The potential impact of these technologies is immense, providing solutions to some of the most pressing challenges faced by industries today.

Name: Lili-o
Description:
Reliable object manipulation remains one of the fundamental bottlenecks preventing general-purpose robots from leaving research labs and operating in real-world environments. We changed the paradigm and have developed a novel learning method, the Lili-O Teach Engine (LTE), that enables robots to learn complex manipulation tasks without massive dataset. LTE is safe by design, energy-efficient and compatible with robots of any degree-of-freedom (DOF).

We believe the next major revolution will come from intelligent robots that learn quickly and adapt to constantly changing environments without reconfiguration. Our products are built to interact with humans naturally and safely everywhere.

Our Lili-O Teach Engine, powered by our one-shot learning innovation, allows robots to acquire new tasks from a single demonstration. Tasks become predictable, behaviours reproducible, and operations safer, more economical, and more ecological.

On the hardware side, Lili-O’s robot is also designed with social acceptance and seamless experience at its core. From exterior appearance to user interaction, every hardware component is carefully selected, tested, and optimized for smooth integration with our always-learning technology.

To demonstrate Lili-o’s fast-learning capabilities, we are cooperating with clients across multiple sectors, where our approach enables robots to perform complex cleaning tasks; helping reduce physical strain, address labor shortages, and create new opportunities for the existing workforce.

Lili-O’s vision is to bring smart, safe, and eco-friendly humanoids to businesses and people’s homes, assisting and empowering individuals in their daily lives.

Want to learn more? Let’s talk.

Name: SkyLab - Aerial Robotics @ KIT
Description:
SkyLab ist eine junge, technische Hochschulgruppe für Drohnenprojekte am Karlsruher Institut für Technologie (KIT). Unsere ambitionierten Mitglieder vereinen ihr Wissen in den Bereichen Robotik, Ingenieurwesen, Organisation, Informatik und insbesondere Methoden der künstlichen Intelligenz, um autonome Drohnenlösungen für reale Anwendungen zu schaffen.  

°UNSERE MISSION°
Wir entwickeln innovative Drohnen, die mit eigens entwickelten Algorithmen, hochwertigen Bauteilen und KI-gestützter Technologie ausgestattet sind. Wir wollen uns als Team den Maßstäben in der Autonomie, Präzision und Effizienz moderner Drohnen annähern und dabei unsere Kenntnisse schärfen, Interessen vertiefen und gemeinsam Herausforderungen meistern.

°Aktuelles Projekt°
Derzeit entwickeln wir die erste Version einer Basis-Drohne, anhand dessen wir die optimalen Strukturen für SkyLab erkennen und uns für die Entwicklung fortschrittlicher Drohnen vorbereiten.
Durch den Einsatz verschiedener Sensoren, Steuerungsalgorithmen, Computer Vision und Mustererkennung, ist die Drohne in der Lage Gebiete autonom abzufliegen und dabei wertvolle Daten zu sammeln.  

°Unsere Vision°
Wir streben danach, auf internationalen Wettbewerben innovative Lösungen zu präsentieren und durch eigene Projekte – wie beispielsweise die Entwicklung einer leichten und effizienten Wasserstoffdrohne – die Zukunft der Drohnentechnologie aktiv mitzugestalten.

°Wer wir sind°
SkyLab besteht aus hochmotivierten Studierenden, die mit Leidenschaft und Know-how an Drohnentechnologien arbeiten. Gemeinsam schaffen wir eine Plattform für technologische Innovation, kreativen Austausch und berufliches Wachstum.  

°Verbinde Dich mit uns°
Wir freuen uns über interessierte Mitstreiterinnen und Mitstreiter, spannende Partnerschaften und alle, die unsere Begeisterung für Drohnen teilen. 

Let’s build something cool!

Name: SEARCH
Description:
We lead on cutting-edge sensing, robotic and automation technologies to solve real-world industrial challenges. As part of the Centre for Ultrasonic Engineering in the Department of Electronic & Electrical Engineering at the University of Strathclyde, we integrate novel transducers, intelligent data processing, and advanced automation to deliver in-process inspection, asset integrity monitoring, and autonomous control solutions.

Our multidisciplinary team, bringing together mechanical engineers, physicists, mathematicians, manufacturing engineers, and control specialists, works in close partnership with industry to embed high-performance sensing and robotic systems into manufacturing and inspection workflows.

Key research themes include:

Transduction: designing advanced sensors and actuators for harsh or complex environments.

Deployment (Robotics & Automation): embedding sensing into closed-loop, adaptive control systems.

Data Management, Visualisation & AI: interpreting large, heterogeneous datasets for decision support.

Name: Team Equinox
Description:
We are proud to be part of aBAJA SAEINDIA, a national-level autonomous vehicle competition that challenges engineering students to design, build, and test an off-road buggy capable of Level 2 autonomy and beyond.

We aim to develop an autonomous all-terrain vehicle capable of intelligent decision-making, obstacle avoidance, and safe navigation in unstructured environments.

Core Capabilities of Our aBAJA Vehicle:
• Lane Keeping Assist (LKA)
• Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC)
• Autonomous Emergency Braking (AEB)
• Obstacle Detection 
• Real-time Path Planning and Navigation

System Architecture Highlights:
• Onboard Processor: NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano for AI & perception
• Control System: ROS 2 (Humble) powered modular software architecture
• Sensors: RADAR, GNSS/IMU, wheel encoders, and camera modules
• Drive-by-Wire: Custom EPS-based steering with absolute encoder feedback, longitudinal control through custom brake and throttle by wire mechanism for braking and acceleration
• Simulation & Testing: Validated in IPG CarMaker and RViz environments

Our development emphasizes real-time ROS 2 integration, sensor fusion, robust control systems (Stanley, PID), and a hardware-software co-design approach to bring a safe and reliable autonomous vehicle to the off-road terrain.

Our Goals:
Push the limits of autonomous mobility in challenging environments

Demonstrate reliable perception and control systems under dynamic conditions
Inspire innovation in student-driven robotics and autonomous systems

Stay tuned for more updates as we gear up for the aBAJA 2025 event at NATRAX, Pune! 

#AutonomousVehicles #aBAJA2025 #SAEINDIA #ROS2 #JetsonOrin #Robotics #OffRoadAutonomy #StudentInnovation #TeamABaja #MobilityEngineering

Description:
Poseidon International Group (Hong Kong) Limited — Connecting the World Through High Accuracy Navigation Technology
  Based in Hong Kong, we are a global enterprise dedicated to delivering high-quality products and professional services. We have established long-term, stable, and efficient partnerships with numerous suppliers worldwide, ensuring competitive pricing and superior product quality.
Focus on R&D OEM Design
We specialize in the independent research, design, and production of fiber optic gyroscopes (FOG), inertial navigation systems (INS), attitude and control systems, autonomous driving systems, and intelligent control systems for unmanned equipment. By integrating industry-specific requirements, we have made significant technological advancements in digitalization, automation, and intelligence, securing multiple technical patents. With years of experience, we have evolved into a multi-industry intelligent solutions provider with a portfolio of proprietary core technologies.
Industry-Leading Expertise
Our core team comprises seasoned experts with extensive backgrounds in aerospace and inertial navigation. We have successfully tackled critical technological challenges in high-precision GNSS-free navigation, laser measurement, integrated navigation, multi-source data fusion, and precise positioning—areas that were once bottlenecks in the industry.
Innovative and High-Performance Products
Our products are smaller in size, higher in accuracy, and more stable in performance, featuring long-term dynamic auto-calibration with zero-drift operation. They are widely adopted in scientific research, industrial control, intelligent unmanned systems, high-precision detection, and metrology. Having passed rigorous validation in aviation, aerospace, marine, mining, and railway applications, our solutions have been procured in bulk, filling key technological gaps in China.

Name: Seismic AI
Description:
Seismic AI is an advanced artificial intelligence–driven LiDAR drone intelligence platform redefining seismic exploration for the global energy sector. The company delivers rapid, high-precision subsurface intelligence by integrating unmanned aerial systems, machine-learning analytics, and real-time three-dimensional mapping, enabling energy operators to explore with greater speed, accuracy, and environmental responsibility.

Traditional seismic exploration methods are often slow, capital-intensive, and environmentally disruptive, relying on heavy ground equipment, extensive personnel deployment, and prolonged data processing cycles. Seismic AI replaces these legacy approaches with a fully integrated, airborne intelligence platform that significantly reduces operational timelines, lowers costs, and eliminates ground disturbance.

At the core of Seismic AI’s technology is high-resolution LiDAR scanning deployed via unmanned aerial vehicles, combined with advanced AI-driven interpretation and predictive analytics. This enables near-instant data processing, real-time visualization, and decision-ready insights delivered through secure, cloud-based systems. The result is a materially improved exploration methodology that enhances operational oversight, workforce safety, and regulatory confidence.

Seismic AI operates in strict alignment with environmental, social, and governance (ESG) principles. Every deployment is designed to minimize ecological impact, reduce emissions, and comply with evolving regulatory frameworks. In parallel, the company supports local workforce development through structured training programs, building regional capability and promoting responsible, sustainable exploration practices.

The company’s approach and technology are endorsed by leading academic and institutional partners, including the University of Nottingham, MIT GSW, IIT Bombay, Oxford Entrepreneurs, and the British Embassy.

Name: FTC Team 7159 - RoboRavens
Description:
The Robo Ravens is an FTC team established at Canyon Crest Academy in 2013. Working during lunches and after school from 3:00 to 4:30, we design, build, program, and test our robot for the season’s game. 

During our rookie year, our team was a class full of 35 people, consisting of returning members from other FTC teams and incoming freshmen. All members had varying degrees of previous experience in robotics, providing a wealth of knowledge and diversity to the team. New members were introduced to Gracious Professionalism, its importance to the FIRST community, and how it applies in real life situations. The classroom environment fostered an environment of creativity and innovation that helped us better design our robot.

In later seasons, without teacher supervision or the motivation of a grade, we had to adjust ourselves to our newfound independence. Our true understanding of robotics was put to the test when training newer members. Initially, we had to take care of many logistical aspects of the team. Afterwards, the preparation for the competitions truly began. We spent the first few weeks brainstorming and prototyping ideas for our robot. Despite having limited time to meet, our team successfully completed our robot design while learning a lot and having fun.

Name: Roso
Description:
Welcome to Roso! 

At Roso, we are pioneers in the field of robotics, specializing in the design and manufacture of cutting-edge PCB motors, 3D motors, and advanced gripper solutions. 

 With a relentless commitment to innovation and excellence, we leverage our expertise in electrical engineering and robotics to develop state-of-the-art technologies that drive automation and revolutionize industries worldwide. 

 Our PCB motors are engineered with precision and ingenuity, offering unparalleled performance and reliability for a wide range of robotic applications. From compact designs to high-torque solutions, our motors power the next generation of robotic systems with efficiency and agility. 

 In the realm of 3D motors, we push the boundaries of possibility, delivering dynamic motion control solutions that enable intricate movements and seamless operation in complex environments. Our 3D motors empower robotics across diverse sectors, from manufacturing and logistics to healthcare and beyond. 

 Additionally, our gripper technology sets new standards for versatility and adaptability in automation. Designed to handle a variety of shapes, sizes, and materials, our grippers optimize efficiency and flexibility in robotic manipulation tasks, enhancing productivity and streamlining workflows. 

 Committed to driving progress and shaping the future of robotics, we collaborate with partners and clients to customize solutions, address challenges, and unlock new possibilities in automation. Together, we embrace innovation and propel industries forward into a smarter, more efficient future. 

Join us on our journey to transform the world of robotics. Follow Roso ! for updates on our latest developments, projects, and opportunities for collaboration. Let's build the future together!

Our team is made up of 9 members and 2 coaches, from FTC (FIRST Tech Challenge) teams 18438 Wolfpack Machina and 18224 Lupine in Massachusetts. Name: Mobilio
Description:
모빌리오는 휴머노이드, 사족보행, 청소로봇 등 차세대 로봇 솔루션을 개발하는 기업입니다. 우리는 단순히 로봇을 제작하는 것을 넘어, 산업 현장의 문제를 해결하고 인간과 로봇이 공존하는 미래를 만들어갑니다.

핵심 기술은 SLAM, 디지털트윈, AI 자율주행이며, 이를 기반으로 물류, 건설, 안전, 재난 대응 산업에 최적화된 플랫폼을 제공합니다. 물류 현장에서는 자율주행 로봇으로 안전성과 효율성을 높이고, 건설 현장에서는 디지털트윈 기반 로봇 기술로 공정 관리와 품질 검증을 혁신하며, 재난 대응에서는 사족보행 로봇이 위험 지역에 진입해 탐색과 구조를 수행합니다.

모빌리오는 글로벌 기업과 협력하며 성과를 창출하고 있습니다. 현대자동차와의 사족보행 로봇 협력, 한진 물류 안전 브레이크 시스템, HD현대의 자율주행 지게차, SK에코플랜트 디지털트윈 프로젝트 수상, 코닝의 물류 감시 로봇, 삼성중공업 속도 감지 로봇 등이 대표적입니다. 또한 태국, 베트남, 인도네시아, 사우디, 미국 텍사스 등 글로벌 현장에서 로보큐(RoboQ)를 운영 중입니다.

우리는 “로봇 기술로 세상을 더 안전하고 효율적으로”라는 비전을 가지고 연구개발과 파트너십을 확대하며, 글로벌 로봇 시장을 선도해 나가고 있습니다.


Mobilio develops next-generation robotic solutions, including humanoids, quadrupeds, and cleaning robots. Beyond building machines, we design platforms that solve industrial challenges and shape a future where humans and robots thrive together.

Our expertise in SLAM, Digital Twin, and AI-powered autonomy enables us to deliver optimized platforms for logistics, construction, safety, and disaster response. In logistics, our robots enhance safety and efficiency; in construction, digital twin robotics transform project management and quality control; and in disaster sites, quadrupeds enter hazardous zones to support search and rescue.

Mobilio has achieved results through global partnerships: quadruped collaboration with Hyundai, safety brake systems with Hanjin, autonomous forklifts with HD Hyundai, an award-winning digital twin project with SK Ecoplant, logistics surveillance robots with Corning, and speed monitoring robots with Samsung Heavy Industries. We have also deployed RoboQ in Thailand, Vietnam, Indonesia, Saudi Arabia, and Texas, USA.

Driven by our vision — “Making the world safer and more efficient through robotics” — we continue to expand R&D and partnerships to lead the global robotics market.

The inspiration behind starting RoboServer Corporation, a service robot business, stemmed from a combination of factors and experiences. Over the years, I observed the rapid advancements in artificial intelligence and robotics, witnessing how these technologies have the potential to revolutionize various industries, including hospitality and customer service.
Additionally, I recognized the increasing demand for automation and enhanced services in our modern society. People are seeking efficient and personalized experiences, and businesses are constantly striving to meet these evolving expectations. It became clear to me that service robots could offer a valuable solution, bridging the gap between technological innovation and human-centric service.
Furthermore, I noticed the challenges faced by businesses in optimizing their operations, particularly in labor-intensive sectors like the food and beverage industry. Staffing, training, and operational costs often pose significant obstacles. By introducing service robots, I envisioned a way to streamline processes, reduce costs, and enhance overall efficiency while maintaining a high level of customer satisfaction.
Driven by a passion for innovation and a desire to create positive change, I embarked on the journey of establishing RoboServer Corporation. Our goal is to provide businesses with cutting-edge service robot solutions that not only address their pain points but also elevate their service standards, revolutionizing the way they interact with customers and optimize their operations.
Through the relentless pursuit of excellence, continuous research and development, and a customer-centric approach, we strive to be at the forefront of the service robot industry, empowering businesses to embrace the future of automation and enhanced services.
In essence, the inspiration to start RoboServer Corporation stemmed from the transformative potential of service robots to meet the evolving needs of businesses and consumer

Name: Partenit. The App Store for Robot Skills
Description:
Partenit: The App Store for Robot Skills

Partenit turns empty humanoid robots into working professionals. We build ready-to-use Profession Packs — Warehouse Worker, Inspector, Handler — that deploy in days, not months. Our on-device memory system lets robots learn on the job, adapt autonomously, and explain every decision without cloud dependency.

The Problem

Today's robots restart from zero after every task. They can't remember what they learned, adapt to new environments, or work offline. This makes enterprise robotics deployments slow, expensive, and fragile. Companies spend months training robots for basic warehouse work — and still can't scale.

Our Solution

We don't build robots. We give them professions. Our Profession Packs are pre-trained skills that work on any humanoid hardware — Tesla Optimus, Unitree, Figure AI. Download, deploy, and start working. Robots learn from real warehouse conditions, and that knowledge transfers across your entire fleet. One robot learns to handle damaged boxes, all your robots improve.

Why It Matters

We run fully on-device with ontological memory — structured knowledge graphs that give robots context, reasoning, and explainability. This means faster deployment, lower operational costs, and compliance-ready AI. No black boxes. No cloud lock-in. Just transparent, adaptive intelligence that works in warehouses, hospitals, manufacturing floors, and extreme environments.

Who We Serve

RaaS providers, humanoid OEMs, and enterprise customers deploying robots in warehousing, logistics, healthcare, and manufacturing. If you need robots that adapt fast, work offline, and explain their decisions — we're your infrastructure layer.

Partenit is building the profession economy for robotics. Hardware manufacturers make the bodies. We install the skills. Think of us as Microsoft Office for robots — the application layer that makes hardware useful.

Name: The BOTY Award
Description:
We created the BOTY Award to spotlight the most innovative and transformative warehouse robot of the year. More capital and human energy are invested in robotic automation each year, and we want to recognize innovation. The BOTY award provides a fair and inclusive process combining expert evaluation and public choice. 

The 2025 Judges Panel, led by  Robert Trebilcock (former editorial director of Supply Chain Management Review, executive editor of Modern Materials Handling, retired) included Amy Villeneuve (former President and COO of Amazon Robotics and Vice President at Amazon), Chris Lingamfelter (Founder, Managing Partner at Robot Advisors), David Huff (Director Systems and Solutions at Sysco), David Widdifield (Assistant Dean, Academic Operations of University of Texas at Dallas at Naveen Jindal School of Management), Kevin Lawton, (Founder Of The New Warehouse), and Peter Skov (Senior Logistics Consultant focusing on Netherlands, Scandinavia, the UK, and the US.). These industry experts meticulously evaluated the contenders, narrowing down the finalists from an impressive pool of 34 top-tier robots. Their evaluation was based on the following criteria:
Commercial Success or Potential Commercial Success: 20%
Innovative Solution (never been done this way): 30%
Usefulness to Operations (product/market fit): 30%
Expected ROI: 20%
The final phase of the BOTY Award process featured a popular vote, which took place between 10:00 AM on Monday, March 17, 2025, and noon on Wednesday, March 19, 2025. Participants could vote twice: once online at www.thebotyaward.org and once in person at Robot Advisors' Robot Basecamp.

Description:
Team 2220 has 51 students and nine mentors, all dedicated to our primary goal of promoting STEM equity. Our team has a high amount of sustainability, which is based on three main factors. We offered virtual soldering classes and applied for grants to provide our STEM classes for FREE. Third, Team 2220 has a strong vision for the future; we set aggressive goals, work to achieve them, and use a SWOT analysis to evaluate strengths and opportunities and identify areas for improvement.

We do our best to ensure that we uphold our team mission: to conduct STEM outreach, build student leaders and expand FIRST locally and globally. We spread FIRST through our four innovative outreach initiatives: Eagan FIRST Robotics, Women In Robotics Empowering Sisters, Robots Without Borders, and STEM in Action. Name: RoboParty
Description:
A RoboParty consiste num evento pedagógico que reúne equipas de 4 pessoas, durante 3 dias e duas noites (trazem saco cama), para ensinar a construir robôs móveis autónomos, de uma forma simples, divertida e com acompanhamento por pessoas qualificadas. Inicialmente, é dada uma curta formação (para aprender a dar os primeiros passos em Eletrónica, programação de robôs, e construção mecânica), depois é entregue um KIT robótico desenvolvido pela botnroll.com e pela Universidade do Minho, para ser montado pelos participantes (Mecânica, eletrónica, e programação) e que no final do evento pertence à equipa. Todas as equipas têm acompanhamento de pessoas com conhecimento para ajudar na construção e programação do robô.
Decorrem em paralelo várias atividades lúdicas como desporto, música, internet, jogos, festas, etc.
Cada participante traz o seu saco cama e fica lá durante todo o evento.
A RoboParty é idêntica a uma LANParty e também funciona 24h/24h mas tem um objetivo pedagógico e educacional. São ainda dadas a conhecer as regras das competições nacionais e internacionais de robótica mais importantes, para poderem participar.

Name: NeuroRobotics Lab
Description:
The Neurorobotics group at Newcastle University works at the intersection of robotics/mechatronics and neuroscience. On the one hand, it uses robotic and mechatronic technologies to study how the human brain plans, controls and executes movements, as well as how it acquires new motor skills, from the early stage of its development to its maturity. On the other hand, it aims to exploit the knowledge acquired from a healthy brain for a human-centered design of robotic and mechatronic systems, to better assess and intervene on movement-related brain pathologies, such as stroke and Parkinson’s disease. These research activities are strongly interdisciplinary and benefit from the opportunity to work with neuroscientists, clinical neurophysiologists and developmental psychologists.
The  group is led by Prof. Domenico Formica and has expertise in robotics for applications in neuroscience, control of physical human-robot interaction, and experimental motor neuroscience. On these themes prof. Formica is coordinating the EU project CONBOTS (www.conbots.eu), which aims at designing and testing a new class of robots, the CONBOTS, which physically couple people to facilitate the learning of complex sensorimotor tasks.

Name: roboLoop
Description:
roboLoop is a Pennsylvania-based technology company delivering automation to the e-waste industry. We are a spinoff of eLoop, a certified leader in IT asset disposition, and the Carnegie Mellon Robotics Institute, one of the world’s foremost robotics research institutions.

At roboLoop, we are redefining the future of electronics recycling. Using proprietary robotic systems designed at the intersection of artificial intelligence and advanced engineering, we automate the dismantling of flat panel displays (FPDs) — one of the fastest-growing and most challenging e-waste streams in the world.

At the heart of roboLoop is a proprietary AI-driven robotic system built specifically for flat panel display disassembly. Unlike traditional shredding, which produces contaminated and lower-value scrap, our robotics remove fasteners, identify components, and separate materials into clean streams.

From televisions to monitors, our process delivers cleaner, safer, and more valuable results than traditional shredding methods. By focusing on precision robotic disassembly, we protect workers, maximize material recovery, and contribute directly to the circular economy.

roboLoop is more than just a recycler. We provide a range of services that help recyclers, municipalities, and OEMs maximize recovery, reduce risks, and achieve sustainability goals.

Our Core Services:

Flat Panel Display Recycling – Robotic disassembly of TVs and monitors at scale, delivering clean, high-value commodities.

Critical Material Recovery – Recovery of elements like indium and gallium from thin-film transistors, with adaptability to future rare earth material recovery.

Commodities Sales – Clean, uncontaminated metals and plastics available for resale in global commodity markets.

Consulting and Technical Services – Partnering with recyclers and OEMs to improve material recovery strategies.

𝗔 𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗽𝗹𝗶𝗮𝗻𝘁 𝗺𝗮𝗻𝗶𝗽𝘂𝗹𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗽𝗮𝗻𝘆

𝗪𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝘄𝗲 𝗱𝗼
Our grippers reliably handle small, delicate, and curved parts, including those with porous, dirty, or wet surfaces—unlocking new possibilities for automation in advanced electronics, semiconductor packaging, and precision manufacturing.

𝗛𝗼𝘄 𝘄𝗲 𝗱𝗼 𝗶𝘁
Through careful, mechanics-driven design of dynamically tunable systems, TunaBotics is advancing the next generation of soft robotic manipulation for modern manufacturing — and beyond.

𝗢𝘂𝗿 𝗻𝗮𝗺𝗲
TUNA → tunable — controllable, switchable, adaptive.
BOTICS → robotics — advancing robotic manipulation.

Description:
At MoltenForge, our mission is to empower human-robot synergy while ensuring that autonomous systems serve society safely and ethically. We envision a natural way to control and supervise robots that harness the benefits of robot autonomy without compromising human authority.

To achieve this balance, we develop enabling technologies that blend the best of robot autonomy with seamless human control. Think authoritative frameworks and intuitive interfaces leveraging:

- Visual dashboards and immersive feedback systems
- Teleoperation to control robots from any distance, especially crucial in hazardous or hard-to-reach environments
- Tools and guardrails to ensure humans can intervene when needed and set the boundaries for robot behaviour

Based in Leiden, The Netherlands, MoltenForge is committed to bringing teleoperation technology into real-world applications, enabling meaningful work with robots. We continuously seek new opportunities to enhance industrial relevance — improving human safety, increasing task efficiency, and unlocking new possibilities for remote operations. We are eager to collaborate with partners who share our passion for advancing human capabilities through robotics and who are inspired to innovate alongside us.

Our Robotic Kitchen-as-a-Service blends industrial-grade robotic arms, VLA-powered orchestration, and proprietary training data to deliver scalable, repeatable, and margin-expanding food preparation.

In a world where labor shortages, quality inconsistency, and margin compression cripple the foodservice industry, Kitchen OS offers a post-human alternative: an autonomous production line that can replicate culinary precision with near-zero human input.

We start with burgers — the world’s most iconic, high-frequency, and technically challenging food item — to establish training moats and market entry via dark kitchens. Our ambition? Redefine food production, one robotic station at a time.

The future of food isn’t in kitchens. It’s in code.

Description:
ROX Automation specializes in open source software and integration/development services for robotics. Our aim is to make advanced robotics technologies ready for production, affordable, and accessible, focusing on sustainable solutions in agricultural robotics. We are committed to open-source, actively contributing to and sharing within the community to foster innovation. Our approach to open architecture ensures easy integration and reliability in automation. We offer full-stack robotics expertise, from mechanical design and electronics to software architecture and cloud deployment, providing the necessary support for our clients to achieve their goals.



What We Offer

1. Software for Smart Machines: Our focus is on creating software for robotics and automation. This ranges from basic motion control to advanced image processing and custom RTK-GPS guidance systems. Using open-source projects as our foundation speeds up development, allowing us to provide high-quality, ready-to-use software to our customers. We're also committed to contributing back to the open-source community.

2. Integration and Development Services: We offer more than just software. Our services span all aspects of automation technology. Name: ROBOSERV 4i
Description:
At ROBOSERV 4I PRIVATE LIMITED, we’re all about bringing the spirit of time-tested engineering into the future of robotics. We pay homage to the ingenious designs of previous generations by reimagining them through the lens of modern innovation.

Our name reflects our core approach — Imagine, Innovate, Invent, and Implement. These four I’s aren’t just words — they’re how we move ideas into reality. We imagine bold possibilities, innovate with purpose, invent solutions built on solid fundamentals, and implement them where they matter most.

We blend these timeless principles with cutting-edge capabilities, ensuring our innovations aren’t just for today — they’re built to endure.

At the heart of our company is a powerful motivation: to solve critical safety challenges through robotics. We focus on eliminating high-risk jobs—especially in areas like tank cleaning where humans currently have to work in hazardous conditions. Our goal is to make those tasks safer, more reliable, and ultimately more efficient through robotic solutions. But we don't stop there—we're also exploring the medical side of robotics, envisioning innovations that could assist in areas like diabetes care or other medical fields where robotic input can make a difference.

In terms of projects, we've already completed a standout product: the SWASR, or Storage Water Autonomous Submersible Rover. This robot is built to operate underwater at depths of about 10 meters.

It can handle obstacles up to 80 millimeters high, moves at a speed of 300 millimeters per second, and carries about 100 kilograms. It's designed specifically for cleaning storage water tanks, and it really represents the kind of practical, safety-oriented innovation we stand for.

Our broader vision is to keep pushing into ATEX-rated projects—those are projects that can operate in explosive atmospheres, whether that's Zone 1 or even pushing into Zone 0. We want to be able to solve problems in highly explosive gaseous & dusty environment

We compete in many competitions, including the FIRST Robotics Competition, as well as various regional competitions. We envision a future where advanced robotic systems seamlessly integrate into dental and medical offices, streamlining essential tasks and elevating patient care. Our goal is to empower healthcare professionals with intelligent robotic assistants, reducing the burden of mundane activities and enabling them to focus on providing top-notch medical services.
Key Features:
1. Efficient Sterilization: Our mobile 2 hands robotic systems efficiently handle sterilization, ensuring instruments are clean for each patient, maintaining a higher standard of infection control.
2.  Assisting Dental Professionals: The robots act as invaluable dental assistants, passing instruments and aiding in saliva suction during procedures, allowing dentists to focus on superior patient care.
3.  Cost-Effective Solution:  Sterilization.ai offers a subscription-based model, providing robotic services at a predictable monthly fee, eliminating the need for additional personnel, and offering a cost-effective solution for healthcare providers.
Impact:
Sterilization.ai drives a positive impact on dental and medical practices nationwide. Our state-of-the-art robotic systems streamline operations, enhance productivity, and elevate the overall patient experience. Improved efficiency, hygiene, and safety benefit both patients and healthcare professionals.

We invite like-minded professionals and organizations to join us on this transformative journey. Embracing robotics and AI, we can shape a future where healthcare becomes more accessible, efficient, and patient-centric. Follow us for updates on our groundbreaking advancements.

We also work hard to make sure everyone on our team feels heard, valued, and supported.

Our team rocks purple tie-dye and unicorns wherever we go. As a team, we strive to have fun, learn a ton, and enjoy being a community. We have a strong team dedicated to doing good work together.

We design technology that farmers can build, customize, and maintain themselves.
S.I.R.A.M features a modular, easy-to-assemble design, allowing users to modify or repair it using simple tools.

Our mission is to make robotics simple, affordable, and accessible to all farmers in Malaysia and beyond.

Founded by UiTM Mechanical Engineering students

Presented to lecturers at UiTM Shah Alam, Faculty of Mechanical Engineering

Focused on local innovation, sustainability, and farmer empowerment

This unique marketplace aspect creates valuable opportunities for users to monetize their expertise, discover rare parts, find job opportunities, or expand their professional network within the robotics domain.

We place strong emphasis on community-building and meaningful engagement. Our goal is to encourage high-quality content contributions and ensure that interactions remain professional, informative, and beneficial to all members.

Name: BetterBlends
Description:
BetterBlends is solving the “phone to mouth” problem in nutrition. In 2023, people have a mess of data to tell them what they should eat: nutrition apps, diet plans, doctor guidance. But there is no way for people to use this data to order exactly what they need at a restaurant to hit their specific nutrition goals. Eating out today is like needing a custom suit, but having to settle for an off-the-rack jacket. BetterBlends is the bespoke tailor of food.

BetterBlends’ core product is an LLM AI model trained on nutrition science and thousands of recipes. We call it Blender. Blender makes it easy for customers to connect their existing nutrition data from the most popular health apps. And Blender makes it easy for customers to tell it about themselves. Their preferences, allergies, and goals. Blender takes this information and recommends our customers customized recipes that they can tweak to perfection. Customers can order this one-of-a-kind meal with the click of a button. 

BetterBlends is the first restaurant without menus. Every order is unique, and perfectly designed for exactly what our customer needs at that exact moment.

Name: DigiTwyn
Description:
DigiTwyn: The Proving Ground for the Robotic Age
DigiTwyn is a deep-tech startup building the essential platform for the next wave of physical automation. We provide the critical "digital foundation" that bridges the gap between purchasing a robot and deploying it successfully.

The Problem We Solve
Businesses across warehousing, logistics, and manufacturing are investing millions in robotics, only to face a critical bottleneck: deployment. The current "test in production" model is broken, leading to catastrophic damage, dangerous safety hazards, months of delays, and suboptimal performance. The real world is the most expensive and risky testing ground possible.

Our Solution: The Robotic Readiness Platform
We create hyper-realistic Digital Twins of industrial facilities using our proprietary sensor fusion and NVIDIA's Omniverse platform. Within these physics-perfect simulations, we train and optimize robots to achieve flawless performance through millions of risk-free repetitions. We ensure that every robot is perfectly prepared for its real-world role before it is ever powered on.

Our Unique Edge

Vertical-Specific Focus: We specialize in solving core automation bottlenecks in logistics and manufacturing.

Hybrid Technology Stack: We combine proprietary hardware with NVIDIA's cutting-edge simulation (Omniverse, Isaac Sim) for unmatched real-world accuracy.

Capital-Efficient Global Model: A US corporate structure with a strategic R&D center provides world-class technology at a fraction of the cost.

Join Our Mission
We are a team of entrepreneurs, AI researchers, and robotics engineers dedicated to accelerating the future of automation. We are always looking for visionary talent and strategic partners who believe that the future is built first in simulation.

Explore how we are de-risking robotics and unlocking a new era of efficiency. Visit our website or contact us to learn more.

Name: HuRo-B1
Description:
Synthesizing the realms of neuroscience, electrophysiology, human-computer interaction, and robotics, a pioneering collaborative venture emerges as the culmination of Jahanikia NeuroLab and GyroPalm's amalgamation. This trailblazing initiative ingeniously aims to furnish upper division high school and college students with an all-encompassing STEM learning experience. By bridging the gaps between these multifaceted disciplines, the collaborative effort stands poised to deliver an unparalleled educational odyssey.This groundbreaking initiative aims to provide research students with a holistic understanding of the underlying scientific principles behind neurosciences, neuroimaging and electrophysiological signals such as fMRI, EEG, fNIRS, rTMS, Brain-Computer Interface (BCI), etc. Equally important, the program aims to equip participants with practical skills to use real-time data from neuroscientific findings to control robotics and devices in a significant, impactful way through gesture control, IoT, MQTT, REST API, etc. HuRo-B1 is a visionary project that merges theoretical understanding with hands-on, practical training, introducing the research students to the fascinating world of brain-machine interfaces.

Our HuRo-B1 program is a unique offering and flexibly provides hybrid learning through in-person workshops for those able to attend and live-streamed sessions for online participants. Occurring every two weeks, our Neuroscience-Robotics workshops are designed to be engaging, dynamic, and deeply enriching. Each workshop is anticipated to span 2 hours. Students will be guided throughout this program by industry experts Sahar Jahanikia, founder of Jahanikia NeuroLab, and Dominick Lee, founder of GyroPalm. As part of this program, research students will be provided with hardware materials and components needed to effectively explore the related topics in the course.

Name: University of Aberdeen Aerospace Society
Description:
Welcome to the University of Aberdeen Aerospace Society! 
We are a student run society excited in all things aerospace. If you are looking for a space to learn (without being graded), create things that hopefully fly, or just hang out, then we are the society for you! 

There is a lot of space for growth and creation here at AESOC. We have multiple ongoing student run and industry backed projects that allow our members to practice theory learned in class, pick up industry standards, and create freely. 

Fixed Wing UAV: The main project is our fixed wing UAV which is a huge undertaking and a huge thing - with a wingspan of over two meters. 

Quadcopter: Leaning onto the EEE/Computer Science side, we are developing a quadcopter from the ground up without using standard, off the shelf quadcopter components. The avionics and control systems will be programmed in house by us and we will use as few non-standard libraries as possible. 

Helical Antenna: Still on the drawing board, we are exploring the possibility of creating a helical antenna with the intention to track a weather satellite and get a live picture of the Earth as it passes over.  

Robotic Arm: In a joint venture between the Robotics and Aerospace societies, we are developing a robotic arm as a development platform to use for future projects. We may even try and get it to do the tracking for us on the helical antenna. 

When and Where
We meet weekly during the academic semester in Fraser Noble Teaching Lab 3 from 2:00-3:00 pm. Swing on by if you have any questions, want to check out what we're up to, or watch some frustrated students try engineering.

Description:
Botinfo.ai is the intelligence hub for humanoid robotics and embodied AI. Based in San Francisco, we track and analyze the rapid evolution of physical AI systems—from bipedal humanoid robots like Tesla Optimus and Figure 03 to agile platforms from Unitree, Agility Robotics, Sanctuary AI, Apptronik, and Boston Dynamics.

Our mission is to make the global humanoid robotics industry transparent and accessible through data-driven insights, side-by-side comparisons, and credible analysis. We focus on helping engineers, investors, and decision-makers understand how next-generation robots are being designed, trained, and commercialized.

Every page on Botinfo.ai is built as a structured, research-grade resource with rich technical and market data. Our coverage includes robot specifications, company profiles, pricing updates, and industry benchmarks—supported by schema markup that allows both humans and AI systems to interpret our content with precision.

Beyond written analysis, Botinfo.ai operates a growing YouTube channel (@botinfoai) featuring original Shorts that showcase real-world demonstrations, field tests, and commentary with humanoid robots in action. Together, the site and channel serve as a bridge between emerging robotics technology and the audiences shaping its future.

Our broader vision is to become the definitive information layer for the humanoid robotics economy—where innovation, investment, and intelligent automation converge.

Welcome to UxS Labs, where technology meets the sky. We are a forward-thinking drone solutions company based in Texas, dedicated to delivering innovative and precise unmanned aerial system (UAS) services for industries across oil & gas, agriculture, construction, infrastructure, and environmental management. 

Our goal is to redefine what's possible with drones by combining cutting-edge technology, industry expertise, and a commitment to exceeding client expectations.


Our Mission

At UxS Labs, we believe that drones are more than tools—they are powerful instruments for solving complex problems, enhancing safety, and driving efficiency. Our mission is to leverage the latest advancements in UAS technology to provide our clients with comprehensive aerial solutions that support informed decision-making and long-term growth. We aim to be the trusted partner that brings vision and value to each project.


What Sets Us Apart

Unmatched Technical Expertise: Our team is led by experienced UAS engineers and operators who understand the technical intricacies of drones and their applications across various industries. From telemetry and control systems to advanced imaging, we bring a level of expertise that ensures each mission is executed with precision.

Client-Centric Approach: We work closely with each client to understand their unique challenges, aligning our UAS capabilities with their mission objectives. Whether it’s high-resolution inspections or advanced data analytics, we provide solutions tailored to our clients' specific operational needs.

Commitment to Innovation: At UxS Labs, we stay at the forefront of UAS technology, constantly exploring new ways to enhance our services. This includes custom hardware modifications, software tuning, and payload integration, allowing us to tackle specialized projects that require more than off-the-shelf solutions.

Focus on Safety and Compliance: Our operations are fully compliant with FAA Part 107 regulations

Our products, the WaveBreaker and the KolliKrake, are the result of intensive research, creative engineering and a deep understanding of our customers' needs. The WaveBreaker enables direct loading from high-bay warehouses to lorries and significantly speeds up the material flow, while the KolliKrake with its innovative gripper arms effortlessly handles a wide range of storage and picking tasks.

What makes us special? Not only our technology, but also our values. At Kolliwaver, customer satisfaction is our top priority. We strive to deliver not just products, but solutions that make our customers lives easier and drive their business forward. 

Our team is made up of passionate experts who work every day to push the boundaries of what is possible and set new standards in warehouse logistics. We are proud to be a start-up that has the courage to think big and help shape the future of the industry.

Name: Dollarhide Labs
Description:
Dollarhide Labs, headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas, is an emerging robotics company committed to shaping the future of adaptable, task-centric automation. Our mission is to design and deliver flagship robotic platforms that are powerful, versatile, and accessible to a wide range of sectors—from research labs and universities to industrial facilities, outdoor farms, and working ranches.

At the core of our technology is modularity. We believe robotic platforms should not be locked into one narrow application, but rather serve as robust foundations capable of transforming with the needs of our customers. By creating highly customizable systems, Dollarhide Labs empowers innovators, educators, and businesses alike to take a single robotics platform and tailor it to solve very specific challenges—whether that means automating repetitive tasks in a warehouse, supporting precision agriculture in rural fields, or enabling hands-on experimentation in academic research environments.

Our approach combines cutting-edge robotics engineering with a user-first philosophy. We emphasize accessibility, intuitive design, and plug-and-play modularity, ensuring that our technologies are not only high-performance but also approachable for teams of varying technical backgrounds. The result is a robotics ecosystem that brings together durability, flexibility, and the ability to operate in both controlled and rugged, real-world environments.

By fostering a community around adaptable robotics, Dollarhide Labs aims to lower the barrier to entry for advanced automation while driving innovation across industries. From idea to execution, we provide the tools companies and institutions need to experiment, deploy, and scale. As we grow, our vision remains clear: to position robotics as a universal solution enabler—bridging the gap between imagination and implementation in how people work, learn, and live.

As a trusted equipment distributor, Zynx Systems provides organisations with access to globally proven hardware, supported by local technical expertise, onboarding, and workflow integration. Description:
Adora Robotics is building embodied AI for the real world.
Our first product, Nyx, is an intelligent home companion robot designed to understand daily routines, detect anomalies, provide emotional interaction, and enhance home safety with real-world autonomy. Powered by multimodal AI, world-model perception, and long-term memory, Nyx brings a new level of presence and intelligence into everyday environments.

Beyond hardware, we are developing AdoraLearn — a task-teaching platform that lets everyday users teach robots new actions through photos, annotations, and voice instructions. These learned skills can be saved, shared, and composed into reusable modules, forming the basis of our upcoming Adora Task Memory (ATM) ecosystem for robot task-sharing.

Our mission is to build intelligent agents that live with us, understand us, and learn from us — and to lay the foundations for the next generation of embodied AI systems.

Name: FRUCTHOR-IA
Description:
O projeto FRUCTHOR-IA (código S2/1.1/E0170) é uma iniciativa cofinanciada através do Programa Interreg SUDOE, com o objetivo principal de otimizar a competitividade do setor hortofrutícola no espaço SUDOE através do desenvolvimento de soluções de robótica autónoma.
Indo além da transição digital, concentra-se na análise das necessidades de automatização do setor e no desenvolvimento de 2 tipos de soluções: a) robótica para a monitorização das culturas; b) robótica para o trabalho colaborativo homem-robô.
O projeto tem a duração de 36 meses e conta com uma parceria diversificada de 10 beneficiários de Espanha, França e Portugal.

El proyecto tiene una duración de 36 meses y cuenta con una asociación diversificada de 10 beneficiarios de España, Francia y Portugal.

Le projet FRUCTHOR-IA (code S2/1.1/E0170) est une initiative cofinancée par le Programme Interreg SUDOE, dont l'objectif principal est d'optimiser la compétitivité du secteur hortofruticole dans l'espace SUDOE à travers le développement de solutions de robotique autonome.

Name: Team Chile Condor Machine - Battle of Robots
Description:
La ingeniería chilena se prepara para enfrentar su mayor desafío internacional hasta la fecha. Nuestro equipo nacional de robótica, Cóndor Machine, ha confirmado su clasificación oficial a los Games of the Future 2025, el mega evento que se celebrará en el Centro Nacional de Exposiciones de Abu Dhabi (ADNEC) del 18 al 23 de diciembre. En un torneo que convoca a miles de atletas y desarrolladores de todo el mundo, Chile marca un hito exclusivo: será el único país latinoamericano presente en la categoría "Battle of Robots", la disciplina de combate de ingeniería pesada que es considerada una de las más espectaculares del certamen.

Los Games of the Future no son una competencia convencional; representan la vanguardia del deporte "phygital", una fusión revolucionaria entre la actividad física y el entorno digital.

Mientras otros atletas competirán en formatos como fútbol digital o carreras de drones, la bandera chilena será la única de la región defendiendo su lugar en la arena de combate de robots, enfrentándose a potencias tecnológicas como, Reino Unido, Estados Unidos de América, China, Rusia e India.

Para afrontar un reto de esta magnitud, el equipo chileno ha consolidado un escuadrón multidisciplinario de alto rendimiento. Más allá de la máquina, la fuerza de Cóndor Machine radica en su equipo humano. Representando el talento de tres regiones del país (Santiago, Viña del Mar y Concepción), el team rompe paradigmas con una composición diversa que integra ingenieros civiles mecánicos, estudiantes de ingeniería industrial, expertos comerciales y profesionales de la salud.

At Roboparks, we're passionate about shaping the future, one robot and one young mind at a time. We specialize in two core areas: manufacturing cutting-edge robots and providing comprehensive robotics programming training for children.

Our mission is to make robotics accessible and engaging for everyone. We believe that by equipping children with the skills to understand and program robots, we're not just teaching them code; we're fostering critical thinking, problem-solving abilities, and a lifelong love for STEM.

What we do:

Robot Manufacturing: We design and build innovative robots that are not only technologically advanced but also intuitive and safe for educational environments. Our robots are the perfect tools for hands-on learning and exploration.

Robotics Programming Education: Our carefully crafted curriculum and experienced instructors guide children through the exciting world of robotics programming. From fundamental concepts to advanced applications, we empower young learners to bring their ideas to life through code.

Join us in building a future where every child has the opportunity to become a creator, an innovator, and a leader in the age of robotics.

Name: AICOR Solutions
Description: At AICOR Solutions, we bring semantic intelligence to robotics. Our software adds a semantic understanding of tasks and surroundings to existing robot systems, enabling true flexibility across platforms and environments. By creating semantic digital twins of robots, tasks, and workspaces, we enable virtual commissioning with automatically generated, verifiable control programs. This ensures that robot behavior is always context-aware, reliable, and ready for real-world deployment. Our technology reduces integration effort, accelerates setup, and makes mobile robots smarter, faster to implement, and more adaptable for modern automation.

By participating in these programs, our members gain valuable technical and leadership skills that will benefit them in their future careers. We believe that the future of innovation lies in the hands of young and talented individuals, and by supporting Gold Rush Robotics, you can help us cultivate the next generation of STEM leaders.

At Gold Rush Robotics, we are committed to giving back to the community and promoting STEM education in the younger generation. We have had the privilege of volunteering at local high school robotics competitions to give back to a community we owe everything to. In addition, we hosted a one-day high school sumo robot event, where we worked closely with students to teach them how to program and build LEGO Mindstorms robots. The event was a great success, with students demonstrating their creativity and technical skills in a friendly competition. Through these events, we hope to inspire and encourage young people to pursue their interest in robotics and engineering. We believe that by sharing our expertise and passion for robotics, we can help shape the future of STEM innovation and make a meaningful impact in the community.

Welcome to my engineering journey! I’m an R&D aerospace engineer at the Netherlands Aerospace Centre (NLR), driven by a passion for innovation and problem-solving. My flagship project, the AW95 helicopter, serves as the foundation for many of my side projects and explorations.

We are “Soft Hoarders”, a robotics team from Frații Buzești National College in Craiova, Romania, made up of 18 high school students passionate about science and technology.

As the oldest active FTC team in the Oltenia region, and one of the longest-standing teams in the country, we have become widely recognised for our consistent pursuit of excellence and innovation.

In our very first season, we qualified for the FIRST World Championship in the United States. Since then, we’ve continued to evolve both technically and as a team, in 2023 receiving an invitation to Maryland Tech Invitational, a competition in the USA, where we ranked 8th out of 40 of the best teams worldwide.

In the '24-'35 season, we qualified for the European Robotics Championship in The Netherlands, where we will proudly represent Romania for the third time on the international stage.

By participating annually in FTC, we aim to develop innovative engineering and programming solutions, building high-performance robots and continuously pushing our limits.

Beyond competition, we are committed to promoting STEM education in our community. This year alone, we organized 57 outreach events, with a significant impact both nationally and internationally.

Name: Freaking Aliens!
Description:
Freaking Aliens is building the sensory infrastructure for autonomous AI — a network of intelligent sensor nodes that provide any intelligent system with a shared, real-time understanding of the physical world. 

As AI moves from the cloud into streets, skies, and cities, the bottleneck is no longer intelligence — it's context. 

Every autonomous system today builds its own incomplete picture of reality. They don't share what they see. They don't agree on what's happening. And when you put multiple AI systems in the same space, they don't coordinate — they conflict.

We're fixing that. Freaking Aliens is building the shared nervous system for AI in the physical world. We deploy intelligent sensor nodes that create a single, real-time picture of reality that any autonomous system can plug into. When one machine sees something, every machine that needs to know, knows.

Company Overview:

DeepDynaSim Engineering & Consultancy is a high-tech R&D and consulting firm specializing in the fusion of physics-based modeling and artificial intelligence to tackle complex engineering problems. Founded by Dr. Çağlar Uyulan, the company operates at the intersection of control engineering, mechanical systems, and machine learning—developing intelligent digital twin platforms, predictive diagnostic algorithms, and simulation tools that align with real-world physics.

We partner with academic institutions, research centers, and industry stakeholders to create next-generation engineering tools that are accurate, scalable, and adaptive.

Core Focus Areas:

Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) and Transformer-based Models

Digital Twin Systems for smart mobility, drones, and energy

Battery Management System (BMS) diagnostics using AI-driven SOH, SOC, and RUL estimation

Thermal & Structural Optimization through reduced-order modeling (POD, SINDy)

AI-Assisted Simulation Software for turbo compressors and aerospace components

Transfer Learning and real-time data analytics for predictive maintenance

Flagship Projects:

Digital Twin Development for Drone Battery Systems

Integrated Transformer & PINN models for real-time SOH/SOC/RUL prediction

AI-based thermal management and cell balancing algorithms

Transfer learning implementation and prototype battery testing for delivery drones

 AI-Assisted Software for Hydrogen & Air Turbo Compressor Design

Custom throughflow solver with AI-driven optimization tools

User interface with 3D design visualization for accelerated prototyping

Targets defense, aerospace, and renewable energy industries by reducing dependency on imported design platforms

Description:
The Mechatronics & Field Robotics Lab. is part of the School of Mechanical Engineering & Automotive Convergence at Korea University.

We carry out foundational interdisciplinary researches on mechatronics, robotics, and control engineering, and perform real-field applications of various advanced technologies.

The robotic systems developed in the Mechatronics & Field Robotics Lab., which is called 'Field Robots', are mainly focused on such 'Field' applications as construction automation, military service, nuclear plant, underwater work, emergency rescue, etc.

Other than conventional robotic systems which include industrial robots or personal service robots, 'Field Robots' have following advantages; needs from consumers are very clear due to their specific application purposes, implementations are tangible with current technologies, and high price can be allowed since they are usually used in public sectors.

As well as field robot group, we explore various interdisciplinary mechatronics systems combining mechanical engineering, electrical engineering, medical science, and physics.

We also research Autonomous Vehicle with Hyundai Motor Group.

Examples researched in the Mechatronics & Field Robotics Lab : Robotic Building Construction, Endoscopic Surgical Robot System, Smart Exercise Machine, Intelligent Excavator System, Autonomous Vehicle, Developed 3D Printing Equipment.

Name: REMORA P2030 R&D Project
Description:
REMORA is an R&D project focused funded under the P2030 programme (COMPETE2030-FEDER-01429400) focusing on the development of autonomous swarm robotics and digital twin technologies for monitoring, management, and intervention in aquatic environments. REMORA brings together a multidisciplinary consortium to push the boundaries of surface robotics, AI, and human-swarm interaction. 

The project aims to design and validate scalable robotic swarms capable of performing coordinated tasks such as environmental monitoring, data acquisition, and system optimisation in complex aquatic ecosystems. These robotic platforms are supported by physics-based digital twins that enable virtual replication of both the robots and their environments, providing a powerful tool for predictive analysis, decision support, and operational safety.

REMORA also addresses human-swarm interaction by developing intuitive user interfaces and control mechanisms that allow operators to monitor and influence swarm behaviour in real time. From low-level control to high-level autonomy, the system architecture relies on modular ROS-based components to ensure flexibility, reusability, and integration with community-standard tools.

Applications of REMORA range from sustainable aquaculture to broader marine, aligning with climate resilience, circular economy, and food security goals. The outcomes of REMORA will enable new paradigms in aquatic robotics, reduce environmental impact, and strengthen the digitalisation of the blue economy.

Name: Cacti Industries
Description: Cacti Industries is building the universal human interface for autonomous robotics, the missing layer that will transform how humans command, control, and supervise robotic systems across defense, industrial, and enterprise applications. Just as web browsers democratized internet access by translating natural human inputs into complex protocols for machine-to-machine communication, Cacti democratizes robotic control by translating natural voice and gestures into low-level robot commands through wearable-first interfaces. The robotics industry faces a critical bottleneck: despite advances in autonomous hardware, human operators remain tethered to handheld controllers, requiring line-of-sight supervision and extensive training that costs $70,000+ per operator annually. Current interaction paradigms force operators to choose between watching the robot and watching for hazards—creating safety risks and limiting deployment to constrained environments. Cacti eliminates this trade-off with heads-up, hands-free control that enables beyond-line-of-sight operation across any wearable device and robot platform.

Description:
CleanBot is an innovative, low-cost AI-powered robot designed to autonomously detect, collect, and dispose of litter in various outdoor environments such as parks, beaches, and sidewalks. Its eco-friendly design incorporates solar cells for sustainable energy harvesting, enabling the robot to recharge autonomously and operate with minimal environmental impact. CleanBot’s navigation system adapts dynamically to diverse terrains and weather conditions, ensuring reliable performance across multiple settings.

By combining state-of-the-art AI technology with green energy solutions, CleanBot aims to revolutionize community-driven environmental cleanup efforts, promoting cleaner, safer, and more beautiful public spaces worldwide.

Name: Asturhealth
Description:
Distributor of an advanced interactive robot developed by AIST, a pioneering leading Japanese research center for industrial automation. It allows the documented benefits of animal therapy to be applied to patients in settings such as nursing homes, nursing homes, or hospitals, where live animals pose treatment difficulties or logistical complexities.

The scientific literature shows that the PARO – NUKA robot reduces stress for the patient and their caregivers
PARO – NUKA stimulates interaction between patients and caregivers
PARO – NUKA has been shown to have a psychological effect on patients, improving their relaxation and motivation.
PARO – NUKA improves the socialization of patients among themselves and with caregivers
PARO – NUKA is the eighth generation of a design that has been in use in Japan and throughout Europe since 2003. It has thousands of users worldwide.

PARO – NUKA has five types of sensors: tactile, light, hearing, temperature and posture sensors, with which it can perceive people and their surroundings. With the light sensor, the robot can recognize light and dark. It feels, through the tactile sensor, if it is caressed and if it is hit; or what position you are in thanks to the posture sensor. PARO – NUKA can also recognize which direction the voice is coming from, and words like your name, greetings and praise thanks to its audio sensor.

PARO – NUKA can learn to behave in the way the user prefers and respond to its new name. For example, if you hit it every time you touch it, PARO – NUKA will remember your previous action and try to repeat that action to make it hit. If you hit it, the seal robot remembers its previous action and tries not to do that action.

When interacting with people, PARO – NUKA responds as if it were alive, moving its head and legs, making sounds, and displaying its preferred behavior.

PARO – NUKA also imitates the voice of a real baby harp seal, an animal that inspired the Japanese engineer who created the robot.

Mentorship & Networking: Access to a community of industry experts, mentors, and like-minded peers.
Collaboration & Recognition: Opportunities to contribute to impactful projects and gain recognition at international events.
Join us at The Hague Hub to be part of a global movement shaping the future of AI for a more sustainable and inclusive world.

Get Involved:

Apply to Join a Hub and connect with AI innovators around the world
Together, we can harness the power of AI to create positive change for the planet and its people.

Name: Wayland High School Robotics Team FRC 5735
Description:
As part of the FIRST global robotics organization, we compete along with thousands of teams at the local, regional, and international level. By building a robot and competing, we inspire our members to pursue the STEM fields and learn lifelong leadership skills. While we engage in exciting activities that build science, engineering, innovation, and technology skills. Through our program our members foster self-confidence, communication, and leadership capability -- all skills that help ensure the success of our members in the future.

Since inception, we have participated in eight in-person FRC district competitions, advancing to the New England championship 6 times, and FRC World Championship competition twice.
We are also very proud of our achievements over the years: 
- We were the Finalist at the Greater Boston district event 3rd at the WPI district event in 2024 Crescendo
- We were a Finalist in the Greater Boston District in 2022 “Rapid React” Game
- We won our first district competition in the 2019 season.
- In 2019 at the North Shore Event we received a Pit Safety Award for our awareness and prevention of potential hazards
- In our rookie year, 2015, we won the District Championship Rookie All Star Award and the Highest Rookie Seed Award

We continue to actively support STEM education in our Wayland and surrounding communities.
- We hosted the Wayland Town-Wide STEM Fair in 2019 and 2023.

OMO is a software-defined system for real-time transformation in Vehicles, Robots, and Intelligent Systems.

Based on two PCT patent applications, OMO uses propulsion-based actuation and dedicated control logic to enable structural reconfiguration.

It employs pragmatic technologies in hardware and software to create a proprietary system that uniquely addresses operational, user, and urban pain points. OMO defines a new category of propulsion-based, transformable vehicles that contract or extend in real time to meet operational and spatial demands. Its first showcase application focuses on Commercial Vehicles.

Electrification and SDV technologies are no longer invisible assets or background features. In OMO’s case, they define a new proprietary design and performance archetype for mobility.

New products, new services, new experiences, all within one protected ecosystem.

Now open to ownership or licensing strategies, joint development, and commercialization pathways across Mobility and adjacent Industries.


Developed by 03 design | HanYi Huang & Alexandros Stasinopoulos

Name: Hanse
Description:
Hanse is concerned with enhancing the life of amputees to do their activities of daily living in normal way and interact with the environment. The design and technology behind our novel prosthetic hand is inspired from the anatomical structure of the human natural hand, that allows the artificial limb to perform activities of daily living with dexterity supported with some level of AI autonomy extracted from real behaviour of human hand. The hand is supported with sensory feedback system that allows the user to interact and sense the surroundings and get connected with environment. Hanse Prosthetic limb is silent (no motor noise), compliant (having soft joints can comply to external forces), anthropomorphic (mapping all human hand joints and look like natural hand, have bones and ligaments).
Additionally, our technology in building a human-like robotic hand can perform complex tasks that can only be done by humans, especially in food packing activities That deal with fragile and delicate ones (fresh fruits, bakeries, poultry, etc.) by learning from the human skills and deploying the skills into Hanse Collaborative hand for food manufacturing.
The venture core technologies rely on the extraction of human hand skills to be applied for healthcare assistive device solutions and food manufacturing.

Mit ConfiBOT können Sie Ihre Arbeitsabläufe optimieren, Kosten senken und Ihre Wettbewerbsfähigkeit steigern. Unsere fortschrittlichen Robotertechnologien arbeiten Hand in Hand mit Ihren Mitarbeitern, um die Effizienz zu maximieren und das Arbeitsumfeld zu verbessern.

Wir glauben an eine Zukunft, in der Mensch und Roboter harmonisch zusammenarbeiten und Unternehmen unterstützen.

What EverBot Does

Commercial & Industrial Applications:

Factory Quality Assurance: Uses AI-powered visual inspection to detect manufacturing defects with high accuracy.

Property Inspections: Automates pre-check-in and post-check-out inspections for rental properties like Airbnbs to ensure quality standards.

Building Security: Provides 24/7 autonomous monitoring and real-time motion alerts for commercial facilities.

Consumer & Lifestyle Applications:

Name: Rehab Robotics and Biomechanics (R2B) Lab
Description: The Rehab Robotics and Biomechanics (R2B) Laboratory aims to develop and test new robotic devices and therapies to help persons with disability due to neurological and orthopedic injuries. Specifically, we seek to create unique electro-mechanical mechanisms to retrain an individual’s lost abilities or augment existing ones. We strive to design systems that tap into the principles of movement biomechanics, motor control and learning, and neuroplasticity. Research on these topics includes basic design and manufacturing, benchtop analysis, neuromusculoskeletal modeling, and translational studies in human subjects, where we evaluate the biomechanical (kinematics and kinetics), neurophysiological (electromyography [EMG], brain plasticity, muscle physiology), and clinical effects of training with these devices. Specific populations of interest include individuals with Stroke, Anterior Cruciate Ligament Injury, Amputations, and non-disabled individuals.

At the core of BioBots’ innovation is the fusion of biology, engineering, and artificial intelligence. Our robots are designed to replicate the agility of living organisms while leveraging cutting-edge technologies such as smart materials, adaptive control systems, and machine learning. 

Our approach enables the creation of robots that are not only technically sophisticated, but also resilient, energy-efficient, and highly responsive to real-world conditions.

BioBots aims to redefine how robots operate across industries, from exploration and environmental monitoring, research, and automation... 

By moving beyond rigid, traditional designs, we unlock new possibilities for machines that can navigate complex terrains, collaborate safely with humans, and perform tasks previously considered impractical or impossible.

Driven by curiosity and guided by nature, BioBots is committed to shaping a future where robotics seamlessly integrates with the world around us.

Name: Dalhousie Intelligent Systems Lab (ISL)
Description:
The Intelligent Systems Laboratory is multi-disciplinary and works at the intersection of engineering (electrical, mechanical) and computer science to enable autonomous systems to operate at long ranges from an operator for extended durations in harsh and dynamic environments like oceans and space.  This is achieved by developing and using tools that include adaptive deliberation, machine learning, decision-making, optimization, autonomous mission-planning, etc.  

Areas of research include but are not limited to:
- autonomy for robotic systems in harsh and difficult environments
- localization and mapping
- multi-robot collaboration
- fault tolerance
- autonomous agents for autonomous vehicles
- embedded programming 
- vehicle dynamics and control
- above and below water acoustics

The applications and partners are military, scientific and commercial
