Name: Kontes Robot ABU Indonesia (KRAI) 2025
Description:
Kontes Robot ABU Indonesia (KRAI) 2025 was a prestigious national robotics competition held from July 6 to 9, 2025, at ITB Jatinangor Campus, Sumedang. Organized by the Ministry of Education, Culture, Research, and Technology of the Republic of Indonesia (Kemendiktisaintek) through the Directorate of Learning and Student Affairs (Belmawa), KRAI 2025 served as the official selection event for Indonesia’s representatives to the ABU Asia-Pacific Robot Contest (ABU Robocon) 2025 in Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia.

This year’s competition adopted the theme “Robot Basketball – Team Effort, Ultimate Success”, inspired by ABU Robocon 2025. Participating teams were challenged to design and build two collaborative robots that competed in a basketball match. Robots demonstrated technical precision and strategic teamwork as they dribbled, passed, shot, and dunked to score points.

The national stage brought together 24 of Indonesia’s top university robotics teams, selected through a rigorous proposal and virtual visitation process. During Covid-19 we had a rough couple of years due to not being able to get together.
However, the 2022-2023 season marked our comeback as one of the only "independant" FTC teams (we're not owned by a school).

The FIRST Tech Challenge, or FTC for short is a international competition organized by FIRST to encourage technological research by teenagers and young adults. You may have heard of the FLL and FRC, these are similar competitions organized by FIRST. In the FTC every team gets a set of the same challenges which they have to solve through robotics and automation, your team then plays a number of alliance battles to put your robot to the test.

But Cybotech does not operate in isolation. We are part of a wider group dedicated to delivering real, practical circular-economy solutions:

Restock Resources and Restock Exchange keep redundant print hardware and consumables in circulation rather than being discarded.

C4S Print Management helps organisations, particularly in education, reduce print waste and optimise usage responsibly.

Regen Enviro deploys RVMs capturing PET and aluminium cans, reducing waste and preparing sites for the GOV Deposit Return Scheme.

Together, we form a closed-loop system that protects value, prevents waste, and keeps materials and equipment in use rather than in landfills.

Name: Origami Robotics
Description:
Industrial automation is essential to the future of manufacturing—and fundamentally broken for everyone who isn’t a Fortune 500 company.

Today’s robotics market is built around rigid, over-engineered systems that assume parts never change, volumes are massive, and time-to-deploy doesn’t matter. Automation is sold as a one-time capital asset, but in practice it is a slow, fragile engineering project that collapses the moment a part, fixture, or workflow changes. For small and mid-sized manufacturers, especially machine shops, this model is economically and operationally unworkable. Robotics is supposed to increase flexibility, but today it enforces rigidity.

Origami Robotics exists to invert this model.

We build modular robotic systems powered by chained agents that produce deterministic automation through high-level abstraction. Instead of writing low-level motion code or re-engineering cells for every new part, users deploy reusable “skills” that reason about tasks such as picking, orienting, cleaning, loading, and unloading. These agents are chained together to create predictable, repeatable behavior, without sacrificing flexibility.

Our hardware is designed to reconfigure in minutes, not weeks. Arms, gantries, vision, and end effectors are self-identifying and automatically integrated into the control system, allowing motion, kinematics, and constraints to be generated on the fly. A new CNC job does not require rebuilding the cell, only selecting a new skill chain and, when needed, a simple fixture or nest.

This approach makes high-mix, low-volume automation not just possible, but incredibly useful.

Name: The BotMinds
Description:
The BotMinds is a dynamic and passionate robotics team comprised of talented engineering students studying at Kathford International College of Engineering. With a shared vision for innovation and a drive to push the boundaries of robotics, The BotMinds is dedicated to building various types of bots and participating in exhilarating robotics competitions.

To achieve this, the team organizes training sessions and workshops on basic electronics and robotics. By reaching out to school students, we aim to inspire the next generation of budding engineers and robotics enthusiasts. These initiatives serve as platforms for imparting fundamental knowledge, providing hands-on experiences, and fostering a love for robotics from an early age.

The BotMinds stands as a testament to the dedication, teamwork, and innovation of its members. Together, we strive to make significant contributions to the field of robotics, both in terms of technological advancements and educational outreach. Through our collaborative efforts, The BotMinds aims to elevate the prominence of robotics and inspire a wider audience to explore the endless possibilities that this field offers.

With a commitment to excellence, continuous learning, and a passion for robotics, The BotMinds is set to make a lasting impact within their college, the local community, and beyond.

Name: Every Flavor Robotics
Description:
Every Flavor was born out of frustration with the state of open-source robotics. Academic research often stalls on fragile, half-finished tools that blur the line between negative scientific results and poor engineering. Industry, meanwhile, sidesteps these problems by building expensive proprietary stacks, solving problems privately instead of improving the shared foundation. The result is a tragedy: the open-source tools that drive innovation remain underfunded and underdeveloped.

Every Flavor set out to change that. We build and share open-source robotics tools, spanning embedded systems, sensing, and motor control, designed for accessibility and excellence. Our YouTube channel brings these tools to life through inspiring builds, technical breakdowns, and educational storytelling that demystifies robotics for everyone from students to professionals. By combining education, engineering, and open access, we aim to accelerate the pace of robotic discovery worldwide.

YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@EveryFlavorRobot

Oblivik Robotics is a leading robotics and automation technology company specializing in advanced toolpath planning for additive and subtractive manufacturing. Our solutions enhance precision, efficiency, and process automation across industries such as aerospace, automotive, defense, and industrial manufacturing.

We support robot kinematic solvers for systems with up to 36 axes, enabling high-performance motion control for both robotic arms and CNC machines. Our team also provides customized post processor development for a wide range of machine controllers and robot platforms, ensuring seamless integration and reliable output.

In addition, Oblivik Robotics delivers scalable automation frameworks that streamline production workflows, reduce manual intervention, and improve throughput. Our modular and flexible systems help manufacturers advance toward smart manufacturing and Industry 4.0 standards.

At Oblivik Robotics, we’re redefining the future of robotic manufacturing, enabling smarter, faster, and more agile operations across the global industrial landscape.

Name: Institute of Culinary Robotics (ICR)
Description:
The Institute of Culinary Robotics (ICR) is a key driver of tomorrow’s innovations.
We bring together leading universities, research institutions, and industry partners to accelerate the next generation of automation in foodservice.

Our alliance unites world-class expertise in robotics, automation, and artificial intelligence, creating a high-impact innovation ecosystem where vision becomes process-proven solutions.

Our team of specialists and engineers, supported by Swiss Engineering and its Artificial Intelligence expert group, advances this mission by transforming research into practical, process-proven applications.

With robotics at the center, we integrate emerging technologies, automation, and process optimization to develop systems that make foodservice smarter, more efficient, and more resilient than ever before.

Name: Tough Data, Inc.
Description: We're not just a motion capture studio—we're teaching Physical AI the skills it needs to solve real-world problems. Tough Data captures premium motion capture and real-world skill data for Physical AI and robotics. We record how experts move, decide, adapt, and recover in complex environments, building the world's most comprehensive human skill library—featuring multi-modal data and skill modules designed for seamless AI integration.

Name: Yantronix
Description: Yantronix is a robotics and automation startup dedicated to developing secure, modular, and future-ready robotic systems for industrial and research applications. The company focuses on designing and prototyping advanced robotic platforms that combine intelligent control, reliable performance, and flexibility for multiple domains — from manufacturing automation to experimental research.

Name: CyberCortex Robotics
Description:
We are developing CyberCortex.AI, a lightweight open-source Artificial Intelligence Operating System (OS) for distributed autonomous robotics and complex automation.

Using CyberCortex.AI, we provide spatial AI solutions for teams of autonomous robots designed for factory deployment, autonomous inspection and collaborative robotics.
